user_comment: Flowy & comfortable palazzo pants, but long. The material has a really nice feel to it. Listed as 70% cotton/30% linen. They are flowy & full. Very comfortable. I am 5’4” & they are a bit long on me, but will be fine with a small heel. I mostly wear a size 12, occasionally a size 10 if an item runs large. I ordered an XL, but think a Large may of fit more appropriately, both in length & size. My suggestion would be to size down, if in between sizes like myself. They came in a vacuumed packed bag, so they were quite wrinkly, but steamed out nicely & easily. They are very pretty pants if a palazzo style appeals to you. The pockets are a decent size, but are not overly deep. I think they might look a little more swanky on someone taller than myself, but they are attractive pants. To me, the color is more of a periwinkle, than a true blue, but they are a pretty color. I wish there was a petite option, but these are stylish pants that can be dressed up or down. I would recommend, if fuller pants appeal to you.

Beautiful quality but will shrink! I think I have fallen in love with these linen pants. The quality is amazing. It’s lightweight when on and heavy when holding it because of great quality. This isn’t those cheap ones, these are the real deal. It’s very flattering and make your bum look fantastic. Pictures don’t do it justice. It also has real functional pockets as well as drawstrings. You could wear this low or high waisted and it looks good both ways. I must warn you though, it will shrink if you wash and dry them in the machine. They were long on me before washing but now they are shorter than I want. I think they are suppose to be almost below ankle length but I liked them longer especially since I’m barely 5ft tall and want to wear heels with them. Anyway I got the size XS in khaki (last picture attached is put against standard cardboard box color). If you want them more loose and long, go up a size especially if you wash them in washing machine and dry them in dryer. Overall, I still love them regardless if they shrunk. This is a must for spring, summer and early fall wardrobe.

They look great with the right pair of heels though! I thought they felt very comfortable to wear. I am short waisted so I am not sure if they were as flattering for my body type as they may be on someone with normal proportions but I would definitely wear them still. They also weren't very flattering on my backside either but I think that is kind of the way it goes with this cut of pants. Overall I would recommend them.

Love the pants but they’re not flattering from behind. These pants are mice and lightweight in a pretty pink color. The length is great as a cropped pant, even for someone at 5’2”. I wanted them as pants to wear out to casual but comfortable outings like brunch. You could dress them totally down with a tee but also dress them up with a nicer top and cover the elastic. They’re comfortable enough that I could see someone wearing these as house pants. These fit the bill of what I wanted but with some caveats. I’m keeping them but won’t wear them nearly as often as I would’ve if the cut was better. There’s an imbalance between the waistband and the amount of fabric around the rise area. I got a 0-4 and the waistband is just a smidge too tense for my liking but there’s all this excess fabric around the lower torso. If I went up a size to get a looser waistband, I’d be even worse. I washed them in hopes that it would shrink the fabric—which it did a bit—and it looks better from the front but not from behind. It’s super unflattering from the back. I have a bubble butt so it’s not about having a flat butt. The only way to really wear them so they look right is to pull the back waistband halfway up my back and leave the front just above my belly button. This means you can’t wear tops and tuck them in. I found that waist-length shirts look best. Anything longer and you look super boxy. Anything shorter and you’ll see how much you’ve pulled your pants up. I might have them altered to take in some of that fabric through the back of the rise. It’s a good price point so a few more dollars in alterations would be worth it to get more wear and to look good walking away!

Liked better than expected Based on reviews, I didn’t have high expectations. I actually like the fabric, especially after washing the pants. I think you could dry them on low and hang them promptly and not have to iron them. I expected them to shrink based on reviews—they did not shrink (washed in cold, dried on low). I normally wear size 8/10; I ordered the 12/14 and could easily have worn a 10. There is a lot of extra fabric around the waist area. They are very comfortable. They hit just above my ankles and I have a 29” inseam. Very summery pants.

Comfortable, maybe not stylish After reading the reviews, I decided to try these out given I'm looking into giving up my jeans and skorts this summer. Jeans can be hot, and skorts seem to all be too short (even though I'm 5'3"). I am a medium (145 lbs), but based on reviews I ordered an XL. When I tried them out of the bag, I thought he length is great, the leg fit is great, the bum section is baggy. The elastic waistband was so tight I couldn't believe it - I thought, well I'll give them a wash and decide - they can always be yard pants. They washed and hung dry well - but now the elastic waist is much looser! How does elastic stretch in one wash? I have sewing skills, so I've figured out how to make two tucks along the back waist. However the look just seems too baggy, especially as the bum is large. I think they will be fine for a hot summer day with a tunic type cotton or linen blouse. The pictures are after washing them and the waistband stretching.

PERFECT (or pretty darn close) First, comfort is key, and these are ridiculously comfy. Material is sturdy yet soft and not see-through. I wanted them baggy and to hit just above my ankle…perfect. I think the key is to order the right size, because I think they will flatter a lot of sizes. But they are definitely “supposed” to be baggy. I am 5’2 and weigh 107 lbs so I ordered the 0-4. I am generally a 2 or small in fitted pants. Waistband fits snugly, but not too tight and I like a longer length, (close to the ankle and not mid calf) on myself. So I love them and I ordered 2 more colors.

Comfy Very comfortable, nice color. A little too much material around waist. I’m 5’7”, 135# and ordered 10 (size in pants says large). I’m going to try next size down in grey for a little less material. Wanted something a little more dressy than these. I saw someone in airport with these on and they looked like good comfortable travel pants.

Plus size casual trousers that actually fit. I was looking for a loose fitting, linen like trousers. I have been wearing leggings forever and wanted to go into Soring with something that has a bit mkre style. They do have an elastic waistband which I don't like but I am going to add a drawstring because the waist is a little loose. I am plus sized 18/20 so I ordered a 5x because Chinese sizes are typically so much smaller. This size was a bit large but I have not washed them yet so I will update fit once they are washed and dried. Color was correct and what I expected. Length is good for me but I am 5'7 so make sure to look at that measurement before you order. I love the feel of the fabric but can also tellnit has sizing in it so after they are washed they will soften up. I think if you are looking for a casual trouser for Spring or Summer these would be a great choice. I will be buying another many more.

Size (L)8/10 I am 5"7" , 176 lbs. Most of my weight is on my upper body, so the size was just right. The waist band was stretchy enough for my 38 1/2" waist but the hips were slim enough to not look like a balloon but roomy for sitting and moving. If you are curvier in the derriere you may want a size larger to be loose and comfy. I tucked the shirt in on a couple of pictures to show the stretchy waist. They look better with the shirt out of course. The tag says 100% cotton. The dark gray has a slightly wrinkly linen feel, though they are thinner than linen. Nice summer play clothes. A third of the cost of the linen pants I bought at Chico's

Perfect for hiking! These are exactly what I wanted! I work at home and do yoga, so I live in leggings. I bought these so that I could throw them on over the yoga leggings when it is chilly, because we hike every morning. I can just peel them off when we get home. Since they go over the leggings, I can wear them a few days in a row and then toss them in the wash. They are water resistant, so dew or rain won't bother me at all. I will stay warm and dry. These are so much better looking than most pants of this nature. They actually have a style that isn't weird or baggy in the butt. They are a little long on me, but once you get a pair of hiking boots on it isn't a problem. I bought another pair so that I will always have one clean and ready to go. They run true to size. I typically wear a size 4, I purchased a size 4 and they fit nicely right over the leggings. Not too loose or too tight. I am 5'4" and 125 lbs. in this photo. They do have some zippered pockets in the front, but not in the back. I think it looks nicer, and I don't need any of the pockets anyway because I have a small bag that I carry my cell phone in with a few other things when we hike. I can grab it and go any time. They also make this same pair of pants with a fleece lining. I might give those a shot for the really cold weather. I highly recommend them!

Great! I love these pants and I can’t believe how cheap they are. So much so that I’m going to get all the colors they have. I’m 5’9” and around 150 lbs and ordered a large in these thinking that they looked like they run small, and that I would tuck shirts into them, and that size works great but I totally could have gone for the medium. The material is 97% Polyester, 3% Spandex so there’s stretch to them. I would definitely say true to size. They look really good on and slimming. They also look pricier than they are and feel like they will last. I actually work from home but I do travel for work so I would definitely wear these, but they are versatile and look great with a tank top and sandals or sneakers. Overall these are awesome and I highly recommend.

Super cute, comfy and flattering! These pants are great! I love how comfy they are, they're flattering and they fit well. I sized up to Medium b/c I hate a muffin top, so that meant a bit longer legs than I have (I'm 5'3"), but nothing that some cute heels or boots won't fix! Really happy with these pants and the price is awesome! Dress up or down, these are great!

Different Fit For Different Colors! I am 5'9" and a pretty skinny 126 lbs. I bought 2 pairs in the exact same size - Red, Brown, Medium. I returned the red. They did not fit the same neither were they both of consistent quality. The red fit smaller & were narrower/tapered in the legs. The material was poly/cotton & thinner. The brown was a great fit, long enough and thick, comfy material. They are 65% Rayon, 35% Spandex! That means they will never shrink in the dryer. I don't detect any stretching from the Spandex content, which is odd, but for me, not a bad thing. Haven't washed them yet, so we'll see how they hold up otherwise. Maybe these pants were made in different countries, but now I'm looking to get a different color that is exactly the same as the brown pair. If you have bought other colors & you see Rayon/Spandex mix, post it in your review. I'll def order that color. It seems that I can't post 2 different reviews, Amazon keeps giving me the first review to edit for the second pair. So I've combined both reviews here. 2 stars for the Red, 4 stars for the brown .... Guess I'll average to 4 stars so the browns don't reflect badly.

Great for lounging, would not wear them for workouts. Fit to size. I am wearing these at home, they are so soft and light - you almost do not feel them. Ultrasoft & Extremely Comfortable I ordered these pajama pants for my girlfriend. She was specifically looking for a pair with pockets that would be both functional and comfy. These pants truly hit the mark. She loves them. She says they're extremely comfortable. They're ultrasoft and the material is not too thin or thick, just right.Her favorite feature is the pockets, which are perfectly placed and a practical depth. They're incredibly stretchy and roomy. I ordered an XL, but she said she'll order a size down for the next pair (because she definitely wants another). She is typically between a 14-16 and a 16-18 and is 5' tall. The XL is not only large in the waist, but also long on her.I ordered Dark Grey and she said the color's incredible. She even thinks if styled correctly, you could get away with wearing them out and about. These pajama pants are a great value.

Pajamas for warmer climates I am 5'4" and 135lbs. I ordered a medium. Great basic pajama bottoms. The fabric is lightweight so they're perfect for warmer climates. The fit is good. The length might be too short if you're very tall. Overall, I'm pleased with these.

Overall Great Product These pants are not a cotton material or actual flannel material, so they most likely won't shrink in the wash. They are very lightweight, and soft. It's hard to tell if they will be hot to sleep in. Even though I live in Florida, I'm cold natured so this most likely won't be an issue for me. However, sometimes polyester type material can be warm, despite these feeling lightweight. So keep that in mind when purchasing. The colors are very vibrant as well. I really love that they have pockets. My last pair didn't. Depending on how you wear PJs, is how I would suggest choosing size options. I usually almost wear a size small in lounge pants. Size 2-4 in pants, 115 lbs and 5'3. I'm petite but I have larger hips. I decided to go with a size medium because I wanted them to be more baggy. I usually wear my lounge pants low on my hips, so even though these pants were pretty big, they fit nicely this way because they have an elastic waistband with a tie string. I've uploaded pics so you can see that if you wear your pants higher on your waist and you have a smaller waist like me, you may want to order true to your size. Even with them being worn high waisted, I like that the crotch didn't ride up. There was still plenty of room. Unfortunately, they were too big for me to wear high waisted, even with the string tightly tied. I'll stick with wearing them lower on my hips, until I get pregnant. These were also pretty long on me, but I don't mind that. Overall, I'm happy with these and can't wait to wear them in December, since I purchased them to match hubby's flannel Christmas pj's.

Ordered a Large I'm 5'3 and 175lbs So far I absolutely love these, they are soft and very comfortable. However there's a couple of things that I didn't particularly care for about them the first would be is I wasn't expecting printed material. So its a good thing I bought a large or I would've had that stretched whiteout appearance on my larger area, if you get what I'm saying 😬 Buying the large however means that there made for a taller person? So the waist band of the pj pants almost meets my bra. Which is perfect if your looking to put your tummy in. The 4th picture is what the material looks like all stretched out the 5th still has my hand behind it. So if you grab the wrong size it won't look so good. But as a side note the material is not see through otherwise. The tag though, was awful. But no biggie I seam ripped it out. Advice to the producers bigger tag that is sewn down all the way around maybe that would help, shouldn't take anymore time because of how tiny the current one is. Thank you for the comfy pj pants 🙂

Overall Great Product These pants are not a cotton material or actual flannel material, so they most likely won't shrink in the wash. They are very lightweight, and soft. It's hard to tell if they will be hot to sleep in. Even though I live in Florida, I'm cold natured so this most likely won't be an issue for me. However, sometimes polyester type material can be warm, despite these feeling lightweight. So keep that in mind when purchasing. The colors are very vibrant as well. I really love that they have pockets. My last pair didn't. Depending on how you wear PJs, is how I would suggest choosing size options. I usually almost wear a size small in lounge pants. Size 2-4 in pants, 115 lbs and 5'3. I'm petite but I have larger hips. I decided to go with a size medium because I wanted them to be more baggy. I usually wear my lounge pants low on my hips, so even though these pants were pretty big, they fit nicely this way because they have an elastic waistband with a tie string. I've uploaded pics so you can see that if you wear your pants higher on your waist and you have a smaller waist like me, you may want to order true to your size. Even with them being worn high waisted, I like that the crotch didn't ride up. There was still plenty of room. Unfortunately, they were too big for me to wear high waisted, even with the string tightly tied. I'll stick with wearing them lower on my hips, until I get pregnant. These were also pretty long on me, but I don't mind that. Overall, I'm happy with these and can't wait to wear them in December, since I purchased them to match hubby's flannel Christmas pj's.

100% cotton, dry clean recommended The size XL fit my 180 lb, 5'3" body. BUT girls with thick thighs like me won't get that baggy pant look you see on the models. That said the pants are nice. Elastic leg bands fall just above the ankle and the button detail is a nice touch. I didn't realize that detail faces forward, not to the side like I thought, but it's fine. Also from the pictures, I thought the pockets were wider/bigger which isn't a problem but just pointing it out. Fabric has a chemical smell so you need to wash before wearing. I'm holding my breath they don't fall apart in washer since professional dry cleaning is recommended for these 100% cotton pants, but the tag also says wash separately. I have to learn to read descriptions more carefully, but when I saw them I just had to have them, especially for only $19!! Ordered another pair in XXL to see if I get that baggy leg look.

MC Hammer's got nothing on these Bad Larries. I've bought pants like these before and been wildly disappointed by the diaper look of them however these pants exceeded all expectations and are amazaballs. They are comfortable and fit great. I wear a size 8 but ordered the size large after reading reviews and I'm glad I did. They aren't tight in the waist at all and can easily be worn low on the hip if desired. The fabric is comfortable and not see through. They hit right at my ankles, I'm 5'7" with long legs. The cuffs are soft and fit well without being tight. Overall a really fun and comfortable pair of pants that are sure to elicit unfiltered feedback by those who see you. It's either a love them or hate them thing. But you know what they say about opinions......If recommend these for anyone who is a fan of David's style on Schitts creek.

I want to like these but just can’t. You guysssss I was so excited about these! I haven’t bought new pj pants in years and I love palazzo pants so I figured these could be both lounge and going out pants. But no, just no. First thing I noticed it the gold at the top is shiny. It looks tacky and cheap. Very synthetic looking and definitely not my style. I’m not a true fabric snob but I do prefer natural fabrics. That being said these pants are really soft. And they are comfy to walk around in. I know a lot of reviews mentioned the length. If you aren’t tall these will absolutely be all over the ground. I like long pants but for pajamas I need them to be short enough I’m not tripping over them. These are a couple inches past my feet (5’4 with slightly short legs) My main complaint is how incredibly UNFLATTERING these are. They accentuate my panty line and my belly. I’m 32 and just had my third kid so I’m definitely not saying that it isn’t there but I have a lot of clothes that still make me feel okay about myself. These are definitely not them. If you’re skinny and tall you’ll look great but then again, if you’re skinny and tall you look great in whatever. I almost never leave 3 stars but like I said, these are comfy.

It looks tacky and cheap. Very synthetic looking and definitely not my style. I’m not a true fabric snob but I do prefer natural fabrics. That being said these pants are really soft. And they are comfy to walk around in. I know a lot of reviews mentioned the length. If you aren’t tall these will absolutely be all over the ground. I like long pants but for pajamas I need them to be short enough I’m not tripping over them. These are a couple inches past my feet (5’4 with slightly short legs) My main complaint is how incredibly UNFLATTERING these are. They accentuate my panty line and my belly. I’m 32 and just had my third kid so I’m definitely not saying that it isn’t there but I have a lot of clothes that still make me feel okay about myself. These are definitely not them. If you’re skinny and tall you’ll look great but then again, if you’re skinny and tall you look great in whatever. I almost never leave 3 stars but like I said, these are comfy.

For reference, I'm 5'8" and 132 lbs. I got a medium.

I actually like these & they're incredibly comfortable - review from a fluffy mom I am reviewing this as a mom with a bit of a fluffy body (I am EXTREMELY self-conscious about my waist and belly so no photos of the waistband on), 5'5"ish and usually wear an 8/10 -- so definitely not young and cute like the model. I ordered a Large in these pants and I am very pleasantly surprised.First, they are a cotton-twilly material that doesn't feel particularly soft initially but actually moves pretty well when wearing the pants. They have a boxy feel -- but that is typical of the cargo look (and my lack of an actual waist). I give the waist band 100 stars -- it is a mix of elastic and button/zipper so it has some give, doesn't compress on my belly or slip below a muffin top and it and is really super comfortable. I won't show any photos, but the band comes right above my belly button and securely above my hips and covers the small of my back completely-- so they cover everything and sit in a very comfortable spot. Even when I sit they are very comfortable and stay in place above my belly and hips. There are belt loops too just in case you need them, but waist wise the size is perfect for me, esp with the elastic!Length wise, these seem to run a bit long for me -- however I actually really find the rolled-up style with the sandals cute. For tennis shoes I rolled the hem up once and I think they are okay. The boots photos have no roll. The sandals photos took 3 full wide rolls on the hem. These could easily be hemmed, but I often like my pants on the longer side so I can wear them with different types of shoes and in different season -- so I am good with these. But, yes, I would say they run long.Lots and lots of pockets -- 2 back pockets, 2 hip pockets, and 4 cargo pockets. The cargo pockets are real and not just decoration. The top ones just have a flap, the bottom ones have a button with the flap. So lots of storage if you want it.I actually surprised myself and like these. I am always nervous about ordering online because I never feel anything fits me in a flattering way, and I second guessed my order a million times. But now that I have them on and see how incredibly comfortable they are on even with my body style, I am really glad that I pushed order. These are a keeper and I would definitely recommend.

Nice fabric, nicely tailored. Good fit. I'm 5'3" tall, weight 150 pounds. This is a size medium. Plusses: They have the tailoring of the 1940s, with snug fit over hips and waist and flaring to the legs. The elastic on the waistband is wide and quite stretchable to get over hips to put on. Pockets are well made and have the placket to carry tons of stuff. The buttons are 5/8" across giving a nice decorative look. The pants are long for taller people, yet you can tie the legs to lift the hem above your shoes. Minuses: Wish the pockets were velcro closed as the buttons are hard to get through the buttonholes. And when unbuttoned, you can't see them at all. Strings on pant legs are long and could trip up the wearer unless double tied. Nice pair of cargo pants and will be my go-to for casual wear.

Large = 28 to 34-inch waists. 40-inch hips OK. I ordered Large because size chart says it's for 28-inch waists. The waistband of Large stretches from 13-inches to 19-inches (14 to 20 once worn). Large will thus fit anyone with a 28-inch waist (relaxed) all the way up to a 40-inch waist (fully stretched). Technically, you want to be on the relaxed side, and I would say 28 to 34-inches is fine for Large. I have 40-inch hips and Large fit fine despite size chart saying it's for 33-inch hips. The material is very stretchy. I posted some pictures showing that white is very see-through, and if you stretch these enough, you will even see camel toe silhouette as the material is very light and thing. and I recommend wearing a white slip-short underneath. I'm 5'6 and the pants are too long, and I'd have to put on some really high heels so they don't touch the ground. A 31.5-inch inseam, for high-waisted pants, should fit fine on my low-waisted 29-inch inseam, but these are too long.

The waist has an elastic band with a good amount of stretch and there is a functional drawstring as well. The length goes to the tops of her feet, but she prefers to cuff them into a cropped length. She thinks the material is soft and comfortable and has a decent amount of stretch and loves the pockets. Overall she is happy with them and thinks the price is fair for what you get. Her only issue is lighter color is slightly see through, she tried them with a brown bodysuit and felt like her bodysuit was too noticeable. She likes how these pants look with a tank or T-shirt. I hope that sharing my opinion and experience here provides you some value when shopping and deciding on something like these pants, if so please let everyone know below.

Super soft and fit great! I love these corduroys! Nicely soft, inside and out. I ordered the X-Large as per the size chart numbers and the fit is perfect. I would say the sizes are true with these pants. They look like they are a thick corduroy, even when you see them in person, but when you put them on then you can feel they are not as thick as they look. That is not meant as a negative, just something I noticed. I think it gives them an extended time period of wear, as in fall and early spring, instead of just the really cold wintery days like a heavier corduroy would have. They are so comfy when wearing them with nice roominess for moving around and nothing binds. Also, since I am 5'4", the length was nice for me as well. So many times things are too long with me being borderline short, lol. If I had to pick something to grumble on, I guess it would be that I am not a fan of the square front pockets. Just do not look so nice, to me. I prefer the sloped, side pockets like are usually the normal type. Fashions come and go though, so this season it is square pockets. Next season, who knows?! All in all, I am very glad I got these and look forward to the days when it is cool enough to be able to wear them now. I can definitely recommend these!

Love these corduroy pants I recently got a pair of baggy corduroy pants on Amazon which I love so I jumped at the chance to order these ones. I got the brown color, which my gut told me would be more of a milk chocolate brown in person, and I was right. Good thing because I planned some outfits around these already lol. I wanted these to have a baggy fit so I could wear them loose and have the option to roll up the bottoms. I normally wear a size medium in pants but went with a large as that's the size I ordered on my other pair. They fit perfectly and look exactly how I wanted them to look. For reference, I'm 5'7" and 155 lbs with a slim build and 29-30" waist. The top area is roomy but not overly so, the waist fits great, and the pants are loose in the legs and taper slightly at the bottom. I flipped the hems up once and that gives them the rolled cuff look. They're super comfortable material, they have pockets, and have an elastic waistband for an easy pull on and off style. I'd say they're medium weight so in moderate fall and winter temperatures they should be fine. I'm not a fan of the more slim and fitted look with these, but that's my preference. With a baggier fit, I think these look amazing. They look fabulous paired with oversized chunkier sweaters, turtlenecks, longer cardigans and ankle boots, and definitely give chic fall vibes. I highly recommend these if you like that kind of style and can get the sizing right. I absolutely love these and would come back for the navy pair as well. 5/5 stars!

Not for petites 5’1, 135 lbs, 30” waist. - The medium is quite tight at 28.75” even with the elastic, and the Large would have been too large. - The belt loops stick out and are a little visually jarring. - But the biggest issue is probably the inseam. Usually there’s a little bit of give on the inseam. Nope, the medium was 30.25” and even in 4.5” platform heels, still touched the ground for me. So, these are good if you are 5’6”+ or have high heels.

The waistband didn't pinch too much, and the length was just right. They were a little wrinkly outside the bag, but that's expected. Unfortunately I couldn't get past the 100% polyester fabric. Also, in the summer heat, humidity and polyester don't mix. These were returned.

My favorite pants! After looking at the dress code at my new job, my goal was to find dress pants that felt like sweatpants. Goal achieved, ladies! This is it! These pants are SO comfie, I now own them in 3 colors, and I plan on buying more. They are PERFECT! The waistband has enough stretch to be comfortable, and there are beltloops if you prefer wearing a belt. I'm 5'4, wearing a large, and the length is perfect for ballet flats or loafers. So these might not work for the tall girlies. At first, I wasn't sure about the pleated front; I was always told pleats in the front make you look bigger, and I'm already a hippie girl. I know pleats are in style so I gave it a try. They don't bother me at all; they make them look more like dress pants. And I didn't even get to the best part yet...the inside is a soft fleece material! So soft, so warm! It makes them feel like soft fleece sweatpants. I imagine these are going to keep my legs warm in the winter! Buy these pants! You won't regret it!

These are more like sweat pants than anything. I definitely would not try to pass these off as business casual. MAYBE you could get away with it with the black color, but I still think you’d be pushing it. The green is much brighter than I was hoping for, and kind of just makes them look like pajama pants. The material certainly doesn’t do my body any favors. Shrinks a bit in the wash. I’m 5’7” 200 lbs and ordered the XL. Just a smidge too short on me too, so not for the tall girlies. Wouldn’t recommend unless you’re looking for a strictly lounge pant, and then I’d still probably tell you to look elsewhere.

Soooo Comfortable I'm always worried about sizing when buying clothes online so I tend to order up a size no matter the reviews. The reviews were really mixed for these pants. I'm 5'5" 215lbs right now and I ordered the red in XXL. The fabric is crazy stretchy, soft, and medium weight (not too heavy, not too thin). Everyone's body is different, but I feel pretty confident I could go all the way down to a large, but I wanted the really baggy look so I am definitely keeping these. I don't need to use the zipper or button at all, they will just pull up. The waist band is elastic across the back. The length is long enough for me to roll up the bottoms the way the model pictures are. The red color is great, it's a deep wine color (looks a little brighter in my pics than in person), it looks great for fall. I will buy more when I can afford to. These will be a fall and winter staple for me. Comfy enough to wear working on the farm or dress up for going out.

The MOST comfortable Trousers! I have to agree with other reviewers who referenced these as a Halara dupe 👍🏽. Fantastic! I love these. They’re surprisingly comfortable and true to size. I got a large, I’m a size 10/12 and 5’2”, 160 lbs. These pants are amazing! I bought these for a concert and I have to say I'm very impressed with the quality! First thing is they are long. Styled with aviator sunglasses, gold dangling earrings and belt chain, a white tank, and mustard yellow faux leather jacket. I’m not quite 5’6” and am like 145 lbs, so do with that whatever you need to. The pros: these were perfect for the 70s theme birthday party I went to. I was in theme and felt cute which was all that really mattered to me. That said, the cons: I would have liked these to have been longer and for comfort around my soft midsection, went with a Large size. The sequins do catch, so I had to keep my legs/knees apart or I’d be pulling the two pant legs apart and tearing sequins in the process. Had to stand and walk strategically (aka did the Hustle) in order to minimize damage. Sensorywise, the pants were a nightmare… the sequins that got caught in the seam were scratchy against my thighs in a way that had me fantasizing about soft, comfy pjs. Takeaway: cute, not comfy. Would wear again but only for a specific event like Halloween, New Years, or I’ve become Taylor Swift.

glam central but so let me start by saying that these flare pants are so glam and glitzy that they are a head-turner piece for sure. they are lined and relatively comfortable to wear. they might be a little scratchy for long-term wear, especially in between the thighs. BUT they are not short girl/petite woman friendly. these are very long pants and I will need to wear platforms when wearing them out (I'm 5'1). Otherwise, they are a few inches too long for me. also, I'm in between sizes (M-L) and ordered a Large and it barely fits me around the waist. the elastic waistband is a little snug but then the hip area is too big for me. you're going to need an hourglass (thick) figure for these to look good. I don't have hips/butt, so I'm more of a square silhouette and these look sad on me :'( make sure to look at measurements before ordering

Cute but a bit uncomfortable to wear These pants are super cute, but they are a bit uncomfortable and itchy to wear. They have a layer of white fabric that is meant to be a barrier between the sequence and your skin, but the fabric is very thin and porous, and unfortunately the sequence just pokes through making the pants very itchy while you wear them. Other than that, they are well made, and very pretty. They are stretchy and they fit as expected. I am 5'1 so they are a little long on me, but that is expected. Overall, these are very nice pants but they are not the most comfortable to wear. They are itchy and thin, but they are stretchy and pretty.

Must-have! Every woman should have a pair of these sequined pants in their closet for concerts and parties! Super flattering and fun! Smooth line prevents itchiness from sequins. Length is perfect for heels or boots. I'm 5'7 185lbs and large fits like a glove.

Ok sequins!! Okay. So. Sequins are kind of my thing. I'm a singer and have been wearing sequined apparel for 10+ years. I'm familiar with all the common issues (improper inner lining so it's scratchy, sequins coming loose and getting snagged, falling off, bending, etc). In many cases, pants in this style are EXTREMELY scratchy due to thin lining and are highly unpleasant to wear. Not the case here - they are soft, stretchy, and comfortable enough to wear for hours (which is usually how long I'll need to wear them for so that's good!) Additionally, most of the time sequined pants highlight my legs in ways I'm not a fan of, as I'm on the curvier side. They usually make me look heavy and short. Due to the flare, these are very flattering and will look great with a cute top or cropped blazer. The only issue is that they're long. I'm 5"1' so most pants are obviously going to be too long on me. These are probably a good 3-5 inches too long and unfortunately it won't be remedied with high heels. However, I'm so in love with the way these look that I'm planning to get them hemmed so I can wear for gigs - it's worth it!

Love the pants! A little bit "tall" FYI Reviewed by spouse: These are so fun, I cannot wait to wear them! I think you could pull these off with a concert T-shirt and sneakers, and wear them during the day or dress them up to wear to a concert or out and about and they would be just a great asset to your wardrobe. I would not call these brown, as the sequins is gold with black netting between them that makes the pattern. These are quite versatile, I think, and could act as a neutral with other things on top. I’m wearing the medium and I think this is the correct size for me. For sizing reference, I’m 5’3 and 155. They are quite stretchy, so I think they could accommodate someone 165-170 without having to go up to a size large. They are quite long on my frame and I will need to wear either a 3-4 inch heel or get them altered if I want to wear them with sneakers.

Flowing and comfy Being 5’7 215 lbs it’s important to feel fabulous and comfy when out. These pants helped me do just that! Great find! They fit great! Love the pockets , breathability, the color! Great Value for the money!

Double layered but underwear is still visible 🙈 My inseam is 28” so I was hoping I could pull this off, but as a 5’1”, 140 lbs woman with a body type that resembles a can of soup, this was a no go. I loved that it’s viscose (bamboo) material and it has built-in “shorts” to line the pants, but I could still see where my tucked in shirt ended and also my underwear line, and the waist band accentuated my potbelly no matter what I did, so I had to return it. It also has that linen-like stiffness which I’m not crazy about.

These will shrink IN LENGTH ONLY if you need them to These pants in Medium fit exactly as I expected them to on my 5'3" 150lb frame; comfortable around the waist & hip, flowy through the legs, and approximately half a mile too long. The first photo is them right out of the package. The second is them after a quick wash then dry on high heat. Fit around the waist & hip was the same. So if you do NOT want to lose any of the length, do not expose these to heat in the dryer. If you do need to lose some of that length, you have that option. Other than that, they're pretty straightforward palazzo pants. The fabric is light and soft, the waistband is wide enough to fit securely without feeling like it's cutting in, they have pockets, and I suspect I will be living in them for a large part of the summer.

Awesome, super high-quality comfortable and they look great Products like this restore my faith in online shopping. These hiking tights are absolutely awesome. They are thick cut, stretchy, and super comfortable. The sizing is accurate; these are a size small and I usually wear a size 4, am 5‘5“ and 130 to 135 pounds. There are two zippered pockets at the hips, in addition to two buttoned pockets on the legs. The button pockets would be great place to store your phone and the zipper pockets are perfect for valuables like car, keys and wallet. I know these are hiking tights, but I would wear these on the weekend to run errands. They’re also super flattering. you could style them in a variety of ways. Contemplating buying them in navy blue as well… Do I need them? No! But I want them!

Warm SOFT & GREAT fit I’m always cold walking and working out and had been considering Woolf leggings. At about $150 I figured I better settle for Amazon! I ordered up a size b/c I don’t like things tight around my waist (I’m an Apple shape). I have really long legs also and am 5’8”. Inch bought these in spite of a few bag reviews and have to say I LOVE them! As some said, the pocket is at an odd spot….between hip and knee sort of on the side, but it was easy to reach in and out or remove phone and keys. I’m walking in 37°-59° weather and these were perfect. There was a funny smell when package was opened, but it disappeared within a day. The length was perfect for me with an XL. They butt and thighs weren’t bags as can sometimes happen when I order up a size on leggings. The waist is high and fits in a way that doesn’t smoosh my fat stomach causing pain! Huge plus haha. I received two days ag. Washed. Wore. Now ordering another pair or two. THAT is how awesome the are!

My soft lower tummy looks like a bubble between the waistband and legs. I can wear them with a long shirt, but they are most definitely not flattering or supportive. They are warmer than the sheer, good quality ones I buy at brand name stores, but not thick enough to stay warm in temps below 40 plus degrees. They are not fleece lined, but made of a material that has a fleece feeling back, with a sheer outside. I would not buy these again, but I may use them to layer like I would thermals. I may just give them to my daughter.

Such a sleek and simple design that you cant find for a good price nowadays. I'm 6 foot 1 and 235 pounds, and with an XL pair they did a great job of form fitting me for the most part, but in hindsight I should have sprung for the XXL for added length and a little bit more room (a little tight after a big meal).

Excelente prenda Buen material, no es transparente, la tela es muy suave, la parte de la cintura sirve como una pequeña faja que aprieta sin dejar de lado la comodidad. Pedí una talla S y peso alrededor de 130 libras y me quedo justa pero cómoda, así que si te gusta estar más holgado y pesas lo mismo te conviene una talla M. Yo preferí la talla S por el largo, pero aún así me quedo un poquito larga pero ni modo soy bajita 4' 11" . La única queja que tengo es que parece que a la tela se le va a pegar mucho la pelusa, pero es cuestión de tener un cuidado adecuado al usar y lavar la prenda. En el bolsillo entra perfectamente un teléfono sin quedar apretado ni sentirse incomodo.

New favorite workout pants! First off they are good quality workout pants and decent price. I usually buy workout clothing from Ross and I prefer to try on before buying but due to covid19 amazon has been my only online shopping, amazing fast delivery. I wasn’t too sure on sizing at first but I went with my instinct and purchase the usual size I usually get which is large. I am 5’4 and fluctuate from 165-170lbs. I have a belly that I’ve been working hard to get rid of and these pants do an AMAZING job of controlling my mid section when running. I hate stopping and readjusting but They don’t roll down at all! KEEP IN MIND if you do wear these during summer, it is a thick material so it might get a little hot.

A bit long for the petite girlies, no usable phone pocket I’m 5’2” and 185 pounds and normally wear an XL. I sized up as according to the size guide my measurements would put me in a XXL. I kinda wish I went with my normal size as the tummy compression in these are nice but I think they would be more flattering a bit tighter. The cut isn’t super flattering on the dreaded “fupa” either. Not sure if it would be any better with a tighter size or not because the hem line is right along the bottom of my belly so I imagine it may accentuate it even more. The length of this style is “regular” and as someone petite they are definitely on the long side and run me about 2.5-3.5 inches too long. The regulars would be ideal for someone 5’5” and up probably. I am able to hike them up a little so they don’t drag on the floor while I’m wearing them but obviously not ideal. They are super comfortable and very soft. They feel buttery—almost velvety without all the fuzz that comes with true velvet. They are a little on the warm side but more so “cozy” than hot. The style is very cute and flattering with the bell bottom split hem. It makes my small calves look more balanced with my curvy physique and makes me appear less top heavy. They are also completely squat-proof which is a huge plus. They do tend to attract fuzz and pet hair for some reason, although it wipes off easily. My biggest issue is the one pocket they have in the waistband is much to small to function as a phone pocket for my iPhone 10 Pro Max. It can fit vertically about halfway but feels very insecure and there’s no way it can fit sideways as it’s much too small. It’s a weirdly shaped pocket because it’s more wide than it is deep. It’s maybe 3 inches deep and 3.5 inches wide. Probably better to be used for just cards and keys. A pocket that holds my phone comfortably it a must for yoga pants that are this expensive for me, so I’m taking off a star for that. Otherwise they are pretty great! If that isn’t a deal breaker for you, then I would definitely recommend these if you are 5’5” or taller.

A bit long for the petite girlies, no usable phone pocket I’m 5’2” and 185 pounds and normally wear an XL. I sized up as according to the size guide my measurements would put me in a XXL. I kinda wish I went with my normal size as the tummy compression in these are nice but I think they would be more flattering a bit tighter. The cut isn’t super flattering on the dreaded “fupa” either. Not sure if it would be any better with a tighter size or not because the hem line is right along the bottom of my belly so I imagine it may accentuate it even more. The length of this style is “regular” and as someone petite they are definitely on the long side and run me about 2.5-3.5 inches too long. The regulars would be ideal for someone 5’5” and up probably. I am able to hike them up a little so they don’t drag on the floor while I’m wearing them but obviously not ideal. They are super comfortable and very soft. They feel buttery—almost velvety without all the fuzz that comes with true velvet. They are a little on the warm side but more so “cozy” than hot. The style is very cute and flattering with the bell bottom split hem. It makes my small calves look more balanced with my curvy physique and makes me appear less top heavy. They are also completely squat-proof which is a huge plus. They do tend to attract fuzz and pet hair for some reason, although it wipes off easily. My biggest issue is the one pocket they have in the waistband is much to small to function as a phone pocket for my iPhone 10 Pro Max. It can fit vertically about halfway but feels very insecure and there’s no way it can fit sideways as it’s much too small. It’s a weirdly shaped pocket because it’s more wide than it is deep. It’s maybe 3 inches deep and 3.5 inches wide. Probably better to be used for just cards and keys. A pocket that holds my phone comfortably it a must for yoga pants that are this expensive for me, so I’m taking off a star for that. Otherwise they are pretty great! If that isn’t a deal breaker for you, then I would definitely recommend these if you are 5’5” or taller.

So cute and comfy, but shrunk in the wash I got this in both Apricot and Khaki XS and am in love. They’re so so comfy and cute and I feel like they can actually be dressed up or down depending on what you’re going for. It feels like you’re wearing pajamas but they still look elevated and presentable. They have a linen look with very little stretch (they’re so loose that I wasn’t bothered by lack of stretch). The tag says they’re 70% cotton and 30% linen. For reference, I’m 4’10”, 108-110 lbs, 26-27” waist, 36-37” hips and I had to get around 4” hemmed for these to fit as they do in the photos. Comfy Casual but Put together 5’3” 29” waist ~145lbs Fabric : Nice drapey linen blend(?) fabric does well in heat and humidity, and has a flattering flow to it. Fit: has a real draw string, with no hidden elastic waist, so they are easily adjustable. I ordered the XL, for a drapier fit, and they did shrink a bit in length after the first cool wash. Definitely wash on cold to avoid shrinkage. Color & Transparency: Color is as pictured, slightly transparent but wearably so- i have tattoos on my legs and bum and if pulled tight you can see them, but for normal wear they’re just fine. You’re not seeing right through them. Overall very versatile, good for hot and humid weather, comfortable and flowy, and look really put together with monochromatic looks. Can be dressed up or down a bit depending on what you pair with them. I’d say they’re a good value.

I would recommend these linen pants as they are flowy, fun and very cute for summer/spring!

To Size Up Or True To Fit? Going through the reviews it was challenging to decide whether or not to size up or not. If you have thicker legs and a booty, I would size up so you have a bit more room and it gives you the wide leg look. If you have thinner legs, I suggest purchasing your normal size. I’m 5’5” and 155 pounds, pear shaped. Pic 1 is a large and pic 2 is an extra large. I kept the xl and returned the l, normally I wear an 8. There’s not much of a difference outside of it giving me more room in the hips and bum area. The added benefit of sizing up was to minimize the panty line showing or any marking. The label says cotton and linen, it’s a medium thickness fabric. This is the perfect, lounge, work or travel pants. I loved it enough to buy a second pair in apricot. Oh! If you’re shorter than 5’7” you’ll probably have to get the pants hemmed. Sharing for mental preparation.

Warm, but short The pants feel super nice and warm, but they are for super skinny, shorter women. I am 5'7", 140lbs, and I usually wear a size S for these types of pants. I've ordered M just in case. They fit just a little short at one inch above my ankle in the beginning, but after wearing them for a few hours they settled at the length of a 7/8 cropped legging.

Marvelous warmth in light weight lounge pants These pants are super warm and comfy. I love how soft they feel against my skin. I was cold in the house last night and couldn’t get warm for nothing, the instant I put these on I started warming up, incredible! Fabric is stretchy but not very elastic, meaning they stretch a bunch but don’t pull or feel like tight yoga pants, they also don’t fully recover right away when stretched. They act in that way like memory foam with a slow recovery to normal. They have the look of terry cloth. The inside and outside of the fabric are the same. The waistband is comfortable and wide with a gentle tension just enough to keep them up while the leg cuffs have no elastic just sewing or knitting that provides the lightest of elasticity. I did not find any drawstring in the waist nor do I need one. The sky-blue color is pretty and to my eye, matches the advertising photos. Fit: I used the size chart to select XL based on my body dimensions and they fit great. For reference I am 40/35/39, 184 pounds. Length is perfect for my 6-foot height and so is the in-seam. Overall: I love these and would buy again.

I love these so much that I bought more! (Colors and sizing are issues, though) These are SO cute and comfy!! I initially only ordered one pair, but I liked them so much that I ordered another two pairs! In terms of colors, however... The green is a much lighter shade compared to the deep emerald depicted in the listing. Also, like some other reviewers, I'm not sure if they sent me the pink instead of purple, or if that's genuinely the color of the "purple" (but since these are just loungewear for home, I'm not really bothered). The grey blue is pretty spot on, though. Keep in mind that these DEFINITELY run small - I'm normally a medium/large-ish in USA women's (5'3" and roughly ~150lbs), but I ordered these in XL and I feel like they fit appropriately (slightly baggy). I do have to roll up the bottoms 2 times, but that's super easy because of the elastic. All in all, if you're not too worried about the exact color, I think these are a great option for comfy lounge joggers.

Not The Same Costco Quality Because we all know 5 star reviews rise to the top on Amazon - I wanted to make sure mine is honest. For those of you hoping these Felina leggings are the same as the ones from Costco, you will be disappointed to learn these are not. The Costco brand is 90% polyester and 10% Spandex. These new Felina leggings are 88% polyester and 12% elastane. Sure elastane and spandex are essentially the same but the polyester percentage change between the two has certainly made a difference in texture. Truly, I would give these about 3 stars because while they are acceptable they are not comfortable. They have a slick texture and are shiny compared to my old Felina from Costco. The sizing is way off as well. The Felina I got from Costco is a size small and fit rather loosely (but comfortable in the thighs and hips). They are also super high waist. These new ones from Amazon are tight! Like pressing into my stomach tight and at 5’1 I weigh only 109 lbs! They cling to my skin and feel a bit too much like tights for biking or better yet, they remind the of the same material you might find in swimwear. It’s just not what I was expecting. I plan on returning them.

The best leggings a tall girl will ever own! Hang tight, this may be long because I am in love. A few years ago, I bought a double pack of black Felina leggings at Costco. As woman that's 6'2, finding leggings that don't look like capris on me has been a challenge but I was over the moon thrilled that the pack I got was not only soft, not see-through and were comfy, but were also long enough that they laid at my ankle and the waist was still pulled up high enough over my plus size body that I could do flips and cartwheels and these things don't fall. You know what else is hard to find in leggings? Ones that don't lose elasticity while you wear them and suddenly develop loose, droopy knees. Felina leggings don't do this either. I've slept in them and worn them for extended periods of time and even a few years later, my original pairs STILL hold tight, retain their original black color and have NOT pilled at the inner thigh like most things do for us ladies with thighs that touch. I was absolutely disappointed when Costco stopped carrying this version with the elastic in the waist but was thrilled to not only find them on Amazon, but learn that other colors beyond black existed! I own about 12 pairs of the black. I live in them. So I decided to add some color and bought the gray/navy pack. The same amazing softness. The same length and comfort. I am so happy to have found these here and will likely end up buying the other colors, as well. Seriously, if you're tall, you'll be so happy you bought these, you'll never buy another brand again. The one photo is of the gray on me but the angle and camera flash makes the color a little wacky looking, but I can verify they were true to color in the listing!

Unfortunately Disappointed I really really wanted to like these leggings. I normally use the target xhilaration brand leggings for their feel and thickness, but I couldn’t find any in store so I decided to try these since the reviews seemed good. Upon opening the fabric truly is buttery soft. They’re so velvety even putting them on felt good, and the stretch on them is pretty decent as well. Unfortunately once the leggings are on you do notice that they are a bit short and VERY thin. Even pointing a phone flashlight on them from a few feet away you can see EVERYTHING, I can’t imagine going out in public in these. For reference I am 5’9 and 211 and got the XL size. Will be returning these since they are not right for me but if you’re under 5’5 and closer to 100 lb these might be the right fit for you!

Lovely length and fabric. Could be tighter at the ankle. *edit— The lack of compression makes these leggings a bit revealing in public. While they are not see-through, they do show any and every imperfection and while walking I notice my thighs kind of bouncing around, and they also accentuate the small amount of fat around my hips, giving me subtle love handles under the leggings. I’m quite thin— between a zero and a 2, and these leggings don’t give me the greatest confidence when I’m even a little bloated. Any tummy you may have will be highlighted by these if out in the sun. Definitely lounge leggings for home. Still love the length as I’m 5’11” and they aren’t 7/8ths length — they actually go down to my ankle. Love the fabric, love the high waist, but ankles could be a bit more fitted. Perfect lounge leggings, but not quite enough compression to feel fully comfortable wearing them outside in the sun, without a covered bum/groin. I’m 5’11” 115lbs and got the xsmall. The fit very comfortably and are long enough! Not as tight as other xsmall leggings I’ve purchased but they stay up! I’m happy. Would recommend.

Tall women, take note! I am a 5’10” and 160 lbs midwesterner, who has searched high and low for a full length non-fleece lined, legging to wear daily in the winter. One weekend, after 2 1/2 hours of reading legging reviews, I thought I had finally found them . If you filter the reviews for this legging by my height all the tall women seem to love them, so I bought a couple pairs to give them a try. And, I have to say… they don’t come all the way to my ankles. Furthermore, even though I am washing them in cool water and line drying them, the gap between my shoe and the bottom of the legging seems to get longer and longer. HOWEVER! It didn’t take long for me to understand what all the fuss was about. These are seriously awesome leggings. They’ve got a great pocket for your credit card and ID on the waistband, and a pocket on each side for cell phone or other items. The material is very soft and they’re just a joy to wear. So I’ve decided to describe these as, “my favorite pair of too-short leggings”. I’ve already bought a couple more pairs, and they’re perfect for temperatures above 49°. I don’t look forward to continuing my search for an actual full-length legging, but as it happens, I get to enjoy a warmer climate now for six weeks, so I guess I can put off that research until next winter! And in the meantime, I’ll probably order a couple more pairs of these!
These are good quality leggings. The only complaints I have are that they run big. If you like them to fit snug, size down. They also run long. I'm 5'1" and they are baggy at the ankles.

Great leggings, not see thru! I really like these leggings. They are really soft, truly high waisted, squat proof, pockets on legs plus a pocket in the waist band that is big enough to fit a slim wallet, ID, keys or a snacky snack. I wore these for about 12 -14 hours to see how they would hold up and they did really well! Very minimal pulling them back in place which is great! I’m in between sizes right now on my weight loss journey, so currently a X-Large would be too snug for my liking and the 2Xlarge has a little bit of wiggle room for when my food baby gets bloated. I’m 5’8, 260 pounds - bottom heavy, waist 42 inch and hips 49/50inches. You can see from the pictures my body type and how they look. I also noticed I don’t see as much of my cellulite through these as I do my other leggings, which my thighs and butt is ALL cellulite. Really good price for two for really good quality. I have a hard time finding leggings that are not see thru that I do not have to constantly keep pulling up to try and get to stay around my waist. I plan to order another set.

Sizing a bit small I normally wear size L, but I ordered XL. I am 5’7” and weigh 150 lbs. these looked extremely small out of the package, but I will say that they are super stretchy and soft. Very flattering fit, soft, and hits the waist perfectly When I got these, the first thing I noticed was a slight smell to them, either a heavy plastic smell or something, and likely came from the packaging. I'm sure it will wash away once I wash these. I got the black and the grey in the long version. For reference, I am 5'0" and 118 Ibs. These are a little more relaxed fit than the usual stuff I get (I usually get things that are super tight and hold and hoist things up... but I am just getting back into working out and figured it wasn't necessary to do that), but they were comfortable and clung in all the right places, the high waist certainly does hit the waist dead on. There is obviously more length to these, but as a short person since these are a tight fit, it doesn't get under the feet or snag, so I'm not bothered, I usually try and move anything too loose around a bit. I did notice that there was a mild panty line with these, but nothing worth complaining about, I will just use seamless undergarments with these. The first pair I tried on was the grey pair, and they felt more relaxed than the black pair for some reason, not significantly, but it was noticeable enough once I put on the black pair which fit a little more tightly. Both pairs are noticeably soft, with a nice polyester/spandex mix that is very comfortable and pretty breathable. I have yet to try them when working out but I will be sure to update this review once I've washed them and tried them out (So I will review them to see if their quality changes with a mild washing and air dry) and then working out in them.

What a Surprise...Squat Proof! I liked these leggings so much that I ordered another set. They’re amazing! No need to look any further! These are the best! If you’re thinking you need some leggings, but you’re not sure which ones to get, these are them! Not because the first ones wore out, but because I lost so much weight, the first ones were too loose to run in! I kept them and use them to be lazy around the house! Very Impressed! first things first, im most likely the pickiest woman alive when it comes to leggings and how they fit me. i have wide hips, big thighs, and short legs. one problem i usually have is the leggings not staying up in the crotch area, with these leggings i have not had that problem these are super lightweight, but also give you a good amount of support, ALSO they do not roll down when you sit or crouch over which i'm so glad for. it's like they have the perfect amount of structure. the pockets, amazing. i always wanted to buy leggings with pockets but i didn't like how the ones from Aerie fit me, but these ones are 10x better in my opinion, and you get 2 pairs for roughly $12 each, you can't beat it. for reference: i am 5'4" , 195 lbs carry most of my weight in my thighs and legs, i have a slight tummy. my hip measurement is 47" also, and i purchased the XL size and they fit great so far, i will update if anything changes on that :)

I’m obsessed!! I’m so in love with Fengbay yoga pants!! They are seriously THE most comfortable leggings/yoga pants I’ve ever worn ever!! SOOOO SOFT I received these leggings today and noticed right away how soft they are! I love the pockets and they look flattering on! They're so comfy! *UPDATED REVIEW*** SO i exchanged the large for small, hoping for more compression and so they wouldn't fall down as much and the smalls are a WIN. They're so, smooth, and don't bag as much on the ankles which I love. *FIRST REVIEW*** These leggings are so soft and nice! My primary use for them is exercise (weightlifting). I thought they'd be more compressed, because that's how I like them but they're not. I decided to order a large based on everyone else's review that weighed them same as me but I honestly could've gone with a medium! I have narrow hips but big legs. And I'm 190lbs and 5'5". I do notice that they're a little bit long, some excess fabric bunches up at the ankles. And they do fall a tiny bit! I honestly wished I would've had these as maternity leggings, lol. I'll come back to update and see if they shrink in the wash, and how they hold up.

They run a bit small...I won’t dry them. These are the three colors I ordered for me. I only gave 4 stars because they don’t fit as expected, they run small. I bought this size, 2X because a girl gave a review with pictures that says she weighs 285 pounds and they fit her well. I’m 40 pounds lighter than her and they’re tight on me. I’m not going to send them back because they’re not too tight to wear, I just wanted them to fit well...not too snug. Since they’re supposed to help me on my weight loss journey I will just keep them. The material feels very soft, they are not see through, the pockets are big enough for a smart phone. These will be great for my type of exercise which is walking. I bought two pair for someone for Christmas and she loves them, says her size fits great.

Obsessed!!!!!! I don’t like putting pictures of myself out there, but if it helps a fellow big person, I’m happy! We all carry our weight differently, but for reference I’m 5’ 4” and 260 lbs. The fengbay pants and capris are the exact same thing just one is longer than the other. That’s it! They are great at keeping you held in and comfortable to wear all day long! I literally live in these things!!!! They have pockets too!!!!! Long enough to fit a smart phone! No tiny useless pockets! Thank you!!! They’re high rise so I don’t have flub hanging over my pants! The fabric does not leave you sweaty & gross, but not so thin that it rips the second you wash it. I’ve had 2 pairs of capris for over a year and they’re still in great condition.

Snug and Comfy According to reviews I felt a size small would fit, wrong, it was too big at the waist, had to return and size down. I’m 5ft 112 lbs and an XS fits perfect. I feel like the waist band hits at an odd spot cuz it’s right on my belly ring, would have liked it a tad shorter. (But I have a short torso) so sometimes I’ll fold it down. There’s a pocket on each side which is nice, fits my iPhone just wish it were a bit higher on the hip, I get nervous it’s going to fall out when running though. And a key pocket on the waist.

If you look very closely you can see a little sweat, but really not that noticeable. Of note, they don't breathe very well and a little hot. I wouldn't say they're compression and do slide down slightly while moving. I got a small and my measurements are: 26" waist, 38"hips and I'm 5'1. I probably could have gone with an xs.

Too short for tall gals! I bought the 2-pack (black + blue gray) They are basic compression tights, but don’t help me look thinner, unfortunately… my belly and big bum are still noticeable, lol. As you can see, the bottom of pant sits 2” above my ankle bone, yet all the models in every photo shows their pant leg covering the ankle bone. They clearly use short models :) Too bad the size chart doesn’t show an inseam dimension, which would be super . It feels funny having so much ankle showing when I walk/jog — These are the shortest yoga tights I’ve ever bought in 20 years. I hope this company considers TALL sizing even at a higher price point.

DREAM LEGGINGS Oh my god. Ya'll. These are magnificent. I'm 5'2" on a good day and weigh maybe 130lbs, so I got them in a size small. They fit PERFECT. Just tight enough I feel secure, but not so tight I feel like I can't move. The compression holds everything in without feeling like I'm dying or giving me a muffin top. It feels like I'm wearing a thunder jacket. The waistband is bigger than my hand!! Buttery soft and stretchy. Pockets? Inside AND out. The side pockets can fit my whole iPhone XS Max without it looking like I have a tumor. These make me want to go run three miles and then fight someone. Definitely will be buying again.

These are - by far - the best yoga pants on the planet! I probably have 8 pairs of the full length yoga pants, 4 pairs of the running shorts, and 2 pairs of capris length. Super Comfy Running Leggings Wow, these are nice and thick. They’ll be great for the winter! But still not too hot more that it’s warm. I’ve run in both pairs and would definitely order again. I am 5’2 and 165lbs and I ordered an XL. These aren’t too long and give me the stretch I need in my hips. I have an hourglass shape so I was worried the high-waist would have too much material but it’s perfect! Edit 9.26.20: I bought more! These are truly my go to leggings. So comfortable. I actually gave my first two pairs to my partner because they would wear them to work out and let them sit 🤢 and now they have that sweat smell even after washing. But obviously if you’re on top of laundry you should be good!

These are great, but I recommend sizing up!! I'm in love. I've already ordered 4 more pairs. I work inventory at a warehouse and am constantly up, down, and walking around and these have been awesome so far. The pockets are big enough to hold my phone. I have noticed that if I'm moving a lot, when I grab my phone, there is a little moisture on the screen from sweating. It's not a big deal, I just resorted to switching pockets every so often. I generally wear a size 14/16 in pants and 5'3", but ordered an XXL and they fit perfectly. I'm not constantly adjusting them either like I have to do my other leggings. I tried them on, then washed cold and dried warm and I haven't noticed any shrinkage. The quality feels great and hopefully they hold up. These will definitely be my go to leggings.

Great pants but lenght is a little short These heated pants are nice. They are heavy duty and well lined. They seem to be good quality. The pants have back pockets, front pockets, and side cargo pockets. The battery pack sits in the left cargo pocket. The power, location, and temperature controls sit on the left front pocket.I really like ability to choose which area(s) you want to heat (belly, knee, or back). I was excited to see the option to pick different temperatures for each area as well. The pants heated up fairly quickly and got quite warm. The battery pack came fully charged. It also has 3 dots for a battery indicator so you can keep an eye on your battery power remaining.I did reduce my review by 1 star for 2 reasons. 1. I don't like the front button closure. I would have preferred a button or snap that would stay better. 2. I am 5'6". I ordered small because that is what I usually wear but I have to wear them with a droopy crotch area to barely get them long enough. They would be capris if I wore them pulled all the way up. Longer is better especially if you want to pull these on over boots. I would prefer extra coverage.

Perfect for Petites & Versatile I work in education and they are allowing us to wear scrubs this year so that we can wash our clothes on hot and more frequently due to Covid. I really hesitated since I didn't want to look like I worked in a doctors office at school. I also wanted to try joggers since I am a petite person and typical scrubs make me look like I am wearing pajamas. I tried several joggers and these FIGS were my absolute favorite! I ended up going with XXS Petite (107lbs. 5'2). I purchased these here and also other colors from FIGS online. I love that I can wear t-shirts with them, but also dress them up. They will still be worn once we are no longer allowed to wear scrubs since they look more like cargo joggers. Easy to wash, comfortable, handy pockets (for school keys, phone, etc) and very versatile!

Awesome, but be wary of the sizing!! Okay, so the reason I’m writing this review is because these leggings are so awesome, however the sizing is so weird! I got two sizes because I wasn’t sure, and I’ll be keeping both, I’m usually in between a medium and a large with leggings depending on the brand. I am a size 9 or 11 in jeans, I weigh 170lbs and I’m 5’6”, so I’m not always sure if it’s a new brand I’m trying. I got both sizes of the black, medium and large, I tried the medium first, and they fit amazingly and are SO comfy and have just enough stretch and I could tell I’m going to work out in these too! Then I try on the large and they’re actually TIGHTER than the medium?? I had my boyfriend check the size tag on the back just to make sure I wasn’t losing my mind and sure enough, they were in fact the large? So how is a large tighter on me than the medium?? I’m not really sure haha but either way, both fit, both are comfortable and both make my butt look FAB. Like, covers all cellulite and shows off all my curves like no other leggings ever has! I havnt worked out in them yet, or done much other than stare at my ass and show my boyfriend over and over how good it looks hahaha! Anyway, just keep in mind the sizing issues it looks like I’m not the only one having issues with the sizes but I highly recommend these leggings and will 100% be buying more!! I hope this helped some of you trying to pick sizes out! I was nervous because people were saying they were like 140lbs and getting the medium, but they fit me great and I’m 170lbs and 5’6”!!

I'll continue to get my leggings from Kohl's/elsewhere. Def not worth the hype. I heard the "real ones" are of polyamide material and so maybe those are better, I'll try searching those up but I wouldn't recommend settling for these tbh.

They are decent… UPDATE- So I decided to give them another try lol. I ordered the style on this listing without the pockets in size small (one size down from the size I originally ordered) and they fit much better and I like them better overall. Still keeping my star rating at 3 stars because they aren’t as opaque as I had hoped, like I mentioned in my original review. I do like them much better though without the pocket (weird seam in front) and in a smaller size. Ugh I had such high hopes for these leggings! 😒 They are just ok. Not bad, but decent. For reference I’m 5’4” 136lbs. I ordered size medium. It fits but feels a little loose all over for a legging. I wish I would’ve gotten a size small. I shouldn’t have listened to the reviews that said size up. The length is too long for me it bunches at the ankles and I didn’t find that flattering. The material is really soft and stretchy. I got the ones with the pockets and the pockets hold my iPhone X snugly. I don’t know how it would hold a plus because my phone is pretty snug in there. You could probably get it in there because the material is stretchy but it would be tight! There are two vertical seams (coming down from the pockets) going down the front of the leggings that I didn’t care for. The “butt scrunch” part was just like advertised. It did not give me a wedgie, lol. I was worried about that. 😆 The area where these leggings let me down the most is they aren’t as thick and opaque as my other amazon leggings. 😞 They aren’t necessarily super see through or anything but in comparison to my other leggings it’s a let down. The texture definitely helps disguise whatever you are wearing under the leggings, but I just imagined they would be completely squat proof. They are only sorta squat proof. They are fine bending over to pick something up but if you are really doing squats I would worry about panties showing through. I’m Keeping them because they are very soft and comfortable. I’ll probably wear them around the house and with longer tops. I had planned using these for working out but I won’t be doing that. I would rate them 3.5 stars out of 5 if I could because they aren’t a total let down, I just had slightly higher hopes for them. If you don’t mind the bunching at the ankles and the fact they aren’t 100% squat proof then I say go for it. They probably are better quality than a lot of the cheap “tik tok style” leggings out there.

Great quality, not that enhancing if you’re lacking in the behind region Don’t let the username fool you, I took advantage of my parents prime membership to order these after canceling mine lol! I did have high expectations for these leggings. I will say the quality is superb, they’re very thick and at first upon inspection they looked like they were going to be see through, but the texture keeps them from being as such. I did follow what people were saying about true to size, but I’m not a small girl at 5’6” and somewhere between 155-165, I honestly don’t know. I will say I do have big hips, but smaller legs and a flat butt, genes are my kryptonite. I ordered a medium, and I actually would’ve been fine with a small to be honest, maybe my butt would’ve looked a little better, but if you’re a girl like me who has a pancake butt, don’t expect a magic lift. In my pictures I’m wearing butt enhancing parties and a waist trainer and it’s still looking a little lacking in what I want. Don’t get me wrong, I do my squats to try to achieve that coveted booty look, but I will say, don’t expect some magic leggings to help you get the look if you don’t have them. I will say I do like the leggings a lot, and they make my legs look great, but I just didn’t get the booty look I was hoping for 😭 still four stars for the quality. I would give 5 for that reason, but I’m deducting a star for what I was hoping for. All in all, they’re still quality leggings and I will wear them a lot, but I would definitely order smaller next time.

Nice concept. poor execution. I already have a nice bum and the texture and rouching along the bumcrack do not disappoint in emphasizing that particular feature; however, I have several complaints/suggestions for improvement: 1) I got black and they are totally see through when stretched in the slightest. I’m 230lb, 5’6” and got the XL. They are not too small at all. If anything I’d like them to be tighter. 2) There is ZERO compression, shaping, butt lifting, tummy control whatsoever so unfortunately it empathizes all the belly and “front lumps” too if you know what I mean. Due to sheerness I wouldn’t dare size down. 3) it has a thin 1/2” elastic at the waist top. It stays up fine, but I personally hate this. It gets all twisted in the wash and makes unflattering belly lines. 4) the seam along the crotch where the two legs are sewn together is so poorly done I’m afraid these won’t last long at all before getting a hole in a delicate area. I would NEVER wear these in public!! Overall love the concept but would rather these in a fabletics Mia legging type quality.

I can't wait for the "high waisted" trend to be over These arrived on a cold night and I immediately put them on. The fabric is thick and soft but not stiff – perfect! But the fit is terrible. I am 5'2" and about 140 pounds. If I roll the waistband over and pull the front up underneath my ribs the crotch is where it is supposed to be. Worn like that the length is perfect but the pockets are covered and rendered non-functional. The pants seem to have been made for someone with a Long torso, very poochy stomach, a completely flat butt, and short legs. If I do not roll the waistband over and let them fall naturally the crotch is about mid thigh and the thick seems cause chafing when hanging that low There is about 3 inches of extra 'rise' in front - the fabric gapes out at the stomach when sitting. I read lots of reviews and ordered a large. I think I would've been happier with a medium but still the fit would be way off for my body.
