Great pants Pros: I love the color, fit, and the pockets! It is high rise cut (waist hits above my bellybutton). The picture on Amazon shows the pant length way above the ankle, almost a capri length, but I'm 5'6" and the length hits right at my ankles (maybe I'm short?). I actually like that it's longer on me and not capris. The butt and leg area are loose on me, but not too baggy. The material is stretchy and stitched nicely. I think it is very versatile and would be great for work or to go out. Cons: The description online shows that it is drawstring, but it is full elastic waist with removable belt. The fabric is also very lightweight. That might make it more comfortable to wear, but as other buyers have mentioned, you can see any bumps from your undergarments, so just be aware. Conclusion: I like these pants enough to buy in other colors.

GREAT value! Wow, great bang for your buck! I ordered these at the last minute when I saw that there would be significant rain on my multi-day hike. I wore them over my hiking pants, between 4-8 hours a day, hiking in the rain for 4 days and they stood the test for sure. At first they felt a bit restrictive (but you're wearing 2 pants, so come on), then I got used to them; they were definitely breathable as well.I knock off a star for a couple of reasons: 1) you have to take your shoes off in order to put these on-- no wide pant cuffs; and 2) no pockets. The pants do seem to be "stitched" together in the seams by tape-- I'm not sure if they're sewn underneath and the tape is for reinforcement. If these pants would have ripped in the butt, the majority of my lower half would still have been dry. If they were to have broken on me on the 2nd or 3rd day I still would have gotten my money's worth because I really put them through rough conditions for a significant period of time. If you don't plan on tons of outdoor activities in the rain and don't want to invest in super expensive pants, then these are for you.Lastly, I'll share that I'm approximately 130 lbs and a size 6-8. I bought a small, and they were just right. If I were about 5 lbs heavier or wearing insulated pants, they would not have fit and would have to size up. Hope this helps!P.S. to the people who say these are not fashionable.... who ARE you?! They're RAIN PANTS!

Good quality and comfy! I have these in black coffee (thick), beige (thin), and black (thick), all ordered in XS short. I’m 4’10” and had to get the black coffee hemmed 2” and the other two hemmed 1.5” to get them to the height seen in attached photos. My waist is 26-27” and hips are 36-37”. These fit me really well. The back has stretch to help with fitting. For reference, I’m wearing slingback pumps with a 2.5” heel and new balance 530s (~2” heel). The pants go well with a variety of looks. You can dress up and down as you want. Really good value for money. When I took the pants to get tailored the tailor complimented the quality and asked where they’re from! As for the difference between thin and thick - yes, the thin version is thinner, unsurprisingly. The thinner pants feel more like spring/summer pants and are a bit more breathable, while the thick pants have more weight to them. They both feel pretty soft to the touch. The thin pants have a metal clasp and the thick pants have a button closure.

Wow, super impressed I wear professional dress for work and have lots of dress clothes. Took a chance on these and I’m so glad I did. They feel and look very high quality. The XL long in dark green is what I got. The color is rich, the fabric is super thick but still drapes nicely because it’s kind of a smooth/slippery/dressy weave. I’ve never had this particular fabric but I like it for dress pants. I’m 5’7. Not sure if the wide leg and pleats would work as well on shorter people, but in size long they drape nicely, especially with a bit of a heel. The color is rich. The length is long without being ridiculous. The pleats camouflage the hips and the elastic in the waist means no gap, which is usually a problem for me. If you are wide in the waist it might be too tight. Love the options for thick or thin material. Overall I’m really impressed and plan to get a couple more pair in other colors. *I think they actually look better in person than in the photo I posted.

Differences between fabric styles is important! I bought two pairs to try on. My body: 5’2”, 130lbs, 29” inseam, Typically Size 2-4/XS-S. Pair 1: Army Green, Size S, Regular Length, Thick Fabric. Pros: Standard “dress pant” thickness, great length for me to wear with either flats or small heels/wedges. Beautiful green color, nice earth tone. Cons: Tighter in the center, felt like they hugged my body between the inseam and belly button tighter than the Khaki pair. Only two front belt loops, no back belt loops. Button front closure, not a “hidden button” like the Khaki pair. Would prefer to wear a belt with this pair to disguise that. Pair 2: Khaki, Size S, Shorter Length, Thin Fabric. Pros: Loved the thin, moveable fabric. Pockets and elastic waist sat flatter than than on the green pair. Hidden button closure looks better than the exposed button. Stitching looks better quality. Cons: Too short for me to comfortably wear with the wedges. Khaki color is not as flattering on me as the green. Overall: If the Army Green color was in stock in the thin fabric in my size, I’d order that instead. I much prefer the hidden button closure of the thin fabric and the structure seems to be better quality. They also look like a higher end product in person. However, due to the length and color not being right for me, I am only keeping the Green color and wearing those for my event instead of the khaki. I would recommend these pants overall and they are a great item to have in the closet! The biggest downside is that there are only two front belt loops. For the thin fabric with the hidden button closure it is less of a problem, but the exposed button really could use a belt to pull the outfit together. Overall I didn’t notice a size difference between the two, but the thin fabric felt like it was more flattering. I’ll probably wear shaping shorts under these just for my own comfort. Definitely need to wash (instructions say cold, gentle cycle or soak for a short time, hang dry) and steam out the wrinkles from the packaging before wearing!

So chic and classy, perfection!!! PETITE FRIENDLY I am 5' approx 125lbs. These fit so perfect! I got 5 colors, ALL IN SMALL REGULAR. I got the: Off white thick Black thick Khaki thin Pink thin Black coffee thick I'm glad I went with my gut/some reviews & didn't get 'short'. They all fit the same for the most part. Perfect around my waist with comfortable stretch left, but it wasn't loose it was fitted stretch left if that makes sense? Length was good on all of them except for the black coffee, that one needs to be hemmed and is super long. The rest fit perfect WITH heels on, which was my intent. If I want to wear them with any other shoe I will need to get them hemmed 100%. As far as thick vs thin goes, they feel like regular pants, neither thick or thin, I would have to wear the opposite to tell the difference. Although, I did wear the 'pink thin' out and you could see my cellulite LOL so maybe because it's thin. The black coffee is thick, the only one that does feel a bit warmer to me. The rest I would consider regular pants. The red heels in the photos are kitten heels, so not that high and the pink pants were above the ground. I tried to be very detailed! I hope this helps you! I definitely recommend so classy and chic!!

Quality, Comfort, Value —💯 These pants are fantastic! I was so surprised at how much I love them. They are soft and oh-so comfy! I got the lightweight version and they are exactly that—lightweight, yet not see through. Absolutely perfect for summer! They look so much more expensive than what they cost. Thrilled to add these to my wardrobe. Don’t know about durability yet as this is my first time wearing them, but they seem like they’ll be ok in the wash. My only CON—and not a dealbreaker—is that there’s this funky closure. There’s a normal tab closure with a hook & eye, but then there’s a faux belt with the double rings where you pass the fabric through and tighten it by pulling the end—I hope that makes sense. Anyway, it was only on the front by the tab closure and cheapened the pants significantly so I removed it (of course before I thought to take a pic). Plus, idk how I’d wear a belt on top of it. Idk why the manufacturer would put that there. It’s not shown in any picture either. Again, not a dealbreaker, but wanted to mention it so it’s no surprise like it was for me. Here are my stats...I’m 5’4” and I’m 135 lbs. My weight is pretty evenly distributed, so I’m a natural hourglass. I’ve got a slight mom tummy going, but it’s not awful. My measurements are 39” (or 34DD), 29”, 39”. I ordered the SMALL in lightweight white. I hope you found my review !

Good quality and comfy! I have these in black coffee (thick), beige (thin), and black (thick), all ordered in XS short. I’m 4’10” and had to get the black coffee hemmed 2” and the other two hemmed 1.5” to get them to the height seen in attached photos. My waist is 26-27” and hips are 36-37”. These fit me really well. The back has stretch to help with fitting. For reference, I’m wearing slingback pumps with a 2.5” heel and new balance 530s (~2” heel). The pants go well with a variety of looks. You can dress up and down as you want. Really good value for money. When I took the pants to get tailored the tailor complimented the quality and asked where they’re from! As for the difference between thin and thick - yes, the thin version is thinner, unsurprisingly. The thinner pants feel more like spring/summer pants and are a bit more breathable, while the thick pants have more weight to them. They both feel pretty soft to the touch. The thin pants have a metal clasp and the thick pants have a button closure.

Wow, super impressed I wear professional dress for work and have lots of dress clothes. Took a chance on these and I’m so glad I did. They feel and look very high quality. The XL long in dark green is what I got. The color is rich, the fabric is super thick but still drapes nicely because it’s kind of a smooth/slippery/dressy weave. I’ve never had this particular fabric but I like it for dress pants. I’m 5’7. Not sure if the wide leg and pleats would work as well on shorter people, but in size long they drape nicely, especially with a bit of a heel. The color is rich. The length is long without being ridiculous. The pleats camouflage the hips and the elastic in the waist means no gap, which is usually a problem for me. If you are wide in the waist it might be too tight. Love the options for thick or thin material. Overall I’m really impressed and plan to get a couple more pair in other colors. *I think they actually look better in person than in the photo I posted.

Differences between fabric styles is important! I bought two pairs to try on. My body: 5’2”, 130lbs, 29” inseam, Typically Size 2-4/XS-S. Pair 1: Army Green, Size S, Regular Length, Thick Fabric. Pros: Standard “dress pant” thickness, great length for me to wear with either flats or small heels/wedges. Beautiful green color, nice earth tone. Cons: Tighter in the center, felt like they hugged my body between the inseam and belly button tighter than the Khaki pair. Only two front belt loops, no back belt loops. Button front closure, not a “hidden button” like the Khaki pair. Would prefer to wear a belt with this pair to disguise that. Pair 2: Khaki, Size S, Shorter Length, Thin Fabric. Pros: Loved the thin, moveable fabric. Pockets and elastic waist sat flatter than than on the green pair. Hidden button closure looks better than the exposed button. Stitching looks better quality. Cons: Too short for me to comfortably wear with the wedges. Khaki color is not as flattering on me as the green. Overall: If the Army Green color was in stock in the thin fabric in my size, I’d order that instead. I much prefer the hidden button closure of the thin fabric and the structure seems to be better quality. They also look like a higher end product in person. However, due to the length and color not being right for me, I am only keeping the Green color and wearing those for my event instead of the khaki. I would recommend these pants overall and they are a great item to have in the closet! The biggest downside is that there are only two front belt loops. For the thin fabric with the hidden button closure it is less of a problem, but the exposed button really could use a belt to pull the outfit together. Overall I didn’t notice a size difference between the two, but the thin fabric felt like it was more flattering. I’ll probably wear shaping shorts under these just for my own comfort. Definitely need to wash (instructions say cold, gentle cycle or soak for a short time, hang dry) and steam out the wrinkles from the packaging before wearing!

So chic and classy, perfection!!! PETITE FRIENDLY I am 5' approx 125lbs. These fit so perfect! I got 5 colors, ALL IN SMALL REGULAR. I got the: Off white thick Black thick Khaki thin Pink thin Black coffee thick I'm glad I went with my gut/some reviews & didn't get 'short'. They all fit the same for the most part. Perfect around my waist with comfortable stretch left, but it wasn't loose it was fitted stretch left if that makes sense? Length was good on all of them except for the black coffee, that one needs to be hemmed and is super long. The rest fit perfect WITH heels on, which was my intent. If I want to wear them with any other shoe I will need to get them hemmed 100%. As far as thick vs thin goes, they feel like regular pants, neither thick or thin, I would have to wear the opposite to tell the difference. Although, I did wear the 'pink thin' out and you could see my cellulite LOL so maybe because it's thin. The black coffee is thick, the only one that does feel a bit warmer to me. The rest I would consider regular pants. The red heels in the photos are kitten heels, so not that high and the pink pants were above the ground. I tried to be very detailed! I hope this helps you! I definitely recommend so classy and chic!!

Super cute Cute versatile piece! I got them in two colors. One in the thin fabric and one in the thicker fabric. They fit and feel very similar but I do like the slightly thicker fabric just a little more. I’m 5ft, 168 lbs. I got an L short which fits perfectly.

Quality, Comfort, Value —💯 These pants are fantastic! I was so surprised at how much I love them. They are soft and oh-so comfy! I got the lightweight version and they are exactly that—lightweight, yet not see through. Absolutely perfect for summer! They look so much more expensive than what they cost. Thrilled to add these to my wardrobe. Don’t know about durability yet as this is my first time wearing them, but they seem like they’ll be ok in the wash. My only CON—and not a dealbreaker—is that there’s this funky closure. There’s a normal tab closure with a hook & eye, but then there’s a faux belt with the double rings where you pass the fabric through and tighten it by pulling the end—I hope that makes sense. Anyway, it was only on the front by the tab closure and cheapened the pants significantly so I removed it (of course before I thought to take a pic). Plus, idk how I’d wear a belt on top of it. Idk why the manufacturer would put that there. It’s not shown in any picture either. Again, not a dealbreaker, but wanted to mention it so it’s no surprise like it was for me. Here are my stats...I’m 5’4” and I’m 135 lbs. My weight is pretty evenly distributed, so I’m a natural hourglass. I’ve got a slight mom tummy going, but it’s not awful. My measurements are 39” (or 34DD), 29”, 39”. I ordered the SMALL in lightweight white. I hope you found my review !

cute slacks if you dont wanna splurge I'm 5'1.5 with a long torso so my biggest problem is the "high waisted" needs to go beyond my belly button. these pants are good if: - you're looking to not break the bank - need thigh/leg room in your pants - you have a long torso like me - need stretch to accommodate a growing body - you're over ~5'2 i returned them b/c: - material is not giving in my opinion, not terrible either just wasn't soft, comfy - the pants are kinda heavy - i wore a 1in heel with the pants on and it's still kinda long - didn't give me any body shape - not even close to the A&F Sloane pants / Aritzia dupe TLDR: not for the 5'1 girlies unless you want it a little long, decent material just not my preference.

I'm going to buy this in more colors! For reference, I am 4'10" and I bought an XS. These pants are a bit long when I stand barefoot, but are perfect when I stand on my tiptoes or when I wear heels. I think I will hem the bottoms just a bit, but otherwise I absolutely love these pants and I will be purchasing them in multiple colors. The scrunched waist band in the back grabs my waist and keeps the pants tight to my body. There is a lot of room in the thighs and I feel like I can move around! The material is thick enough that I do not have to worry about underwear showing through, but I also bought these in July in Southern California and am able to walk around in them comfortably without being too hot! I am so excited to wear these to work as well as to semi casual things.

Great price and quality This is my second pair of these trousers. Both in the thick fabric. This green pair I ordered a size L, the other pair I ordered in an XL. I’m around 180 pounds, 5’1. I have a very high waist and short torso and long legs for my height (note: my legs are still short AF, but since my torso is so short and my waist so high my legs at least look longer than my 5’1 height would make you think). The XL fit nice and were comfy and flowy, but they sat lower than my natural waist which annoyed me. The L fits exactly at my waist. They are a touch snug though, not uncomfortably so, but if there was a size inbetween the L and XL they would be perfect. I got the regular length in both sizes. The length in the L is perfect, they are a touch long on the XL since they sit below my natural waist but both work well with boots/heels. The XL works with flats/loafers if they have a platform sole. The only negative is that they don’t flatter my big bubble butt the way I’m used to with different style pants but the booty doesn’t ALWAYS have to be pippin’ I suppose. Regardless, love these pants. I haven’t tried the thin fabric style yet.

Super comfy and warm! These sweats are exactly as described. The material is a slick feel on the outside that I am not familiar with but they are definitely fleeced lined on he inside giving that warm feeling. I wore them with an oversized sweatshirt and sneakers and even though I was in a baggy look; I felt super cute and comfortable. My inseam is a 33. For reference. I am in the process of losing prednisone weight but still deal with an arthritic inflammatory disease. I’m 5’8 and 188#;. Longer legs with a shorter torso, a perfect fit is large or size US 12. Weight is mostly in tummy area now; 13# left to go. I still go one size up for extra looseness in clothing but it’s almost too big now to do so. Shoe size is about a 7 wide when inflamed. 7 medium when normal. US shoe size 7-8 is about normal depending on shoe. FYI: online purchases I try to keep rating simple. If I was in a local store and could hold and view it; 5 star would buy and take it home, one star would put it back on shelf and pick something else.#OnlyJesusSaves.

I'm 5'2", fluctuate between XS and Small. These pants after one wash hit right at the bottom of my heel without shoes, so no drag. Not too tight at the waist which I really like. Comfortable to wear even with any bloat. The fabric is not too thick or thin. Pant legs are wide enough that leggings could be layered underneath during the winter. The fabric is an athletic material, and stretchy. Pockets are decent size. You can see the shape of anything placed in the pockets, so not too much structure. Stitching is fine but not great. I expect these to fall apart within a year or two. As you can see in the 3rd pic, there's already some pilling and I've only washed these twice so far. Overall, these are petite friendly and ok value for the money. This is the first time I've bought from this brand. I'm not converted into a brand advocate and am not overly impressed. Won't go out of my way to look at their other offerings. But if I want another pair of these or come across something from this brand, would also be fine with buying. Content with this purchase.

Oh my goodness… First of all, the important part is I am 5’10” & 120lbs (in those photos), I bought a medium, tall, the 33” inseam, in white, khaki and black. And I am only wearing flats. Posting several pics to see the fit, plus for proof its not possible to take a bad pic in Europe. Anyway, I bought these to wear on our three week trip. I wanted something I could beat up but still look dressy & fabulous. Since we had never been to Europe and only brought a backpack each, I was really nervous about my clothes. But let me tell you, I would buy these pants all over again. I honestly felt like I one upped all the younger, high fashion girls in Milan and Capri. Also, my husband had me vacuum seal my clothes in my backpack. So they were (neatly) crammed into a super tight bag for three weeks. They went through six flights, unknown bus and trains and multiple ABNBs. Every time I pulled them out, they were wrinkle free. They are so soft, I actually slept in them, several times. They STILL do not have stretched out knees. They cleaned up super easy and air dried every where we went (cuz they don’t have dryers in Europe). I wore the WHITE in the RAIN on the streets of ROME (i feel really spoiled saying that) and figured that would be the ultimate test. I honestly figured I would be throwing them away when we got back. After 19,000 steps in the rain, the bottoms where they hit my tennis shoe heel had some discoloration after I washed them. But there has been no shrinkage and I still wear them. I wanted a loose, classy, palazzo look and the medium, tall did exactly that. I bought the small, tall and it was too tight on my bottom for what I wanted, so I gave them to my daughter who is about five pounds smaller. But the smalls did fit. The medium doesn’t feel like they are going to fall off, I felt confident in them everywhere we went. And the hips don’t look like they aren’t cut for a girl my size. I’ve purchased much more expensive pants and didn’t get 1/2 of what I am getting out of these. My only wish is they made them with a sewn in front seam and a non-yoga unwrapped waste for a more professional look. If they did, I’d probably buy the company.

I'm 5'2", fluctuate between XS and Small. These pants after one wash hit right at the bottom of my heel without shoes, so no drag. Not too tight at the waist which I really like. Comfortable to wear even with any bloat. The fabric is not too thick or thin. Pant legs are wide enough that leggings could be layered underneath during the winter. The fabric is an athletic material, and stretchy. Pockets are decent size. You can see the shape of anything placed in the pockets, so not too much structure. Stitching is fine but not great. I expect these to fall apart within a year or two. As you can see in the 3rd pic, there's already some pilling and I've only washed these twice so far. Overall, these are petite friendly and ok value for the money. This is the first time I've bought from this brand. I'm not converted into a brand advocate and am not overly impressed. Won't go out of my way to look at their other offerings. But if I want another pair of these or come across something from this brand, would also be fine with buying. Content with this purchase.

Petite friendly! Comfy and flattering! Are you really short and hate having to hem pants? Are you slim thicc or carry your weight on your lower half? These are for you!! I’m 5’0” and fluctuate between 110-115. I ordered these in 29” XS and they fit like a GLOVE. No weird baggy crotch and THE perfect length to wear with my gym shoes without dragging. Fits my waist AND my large rear end… I think my butt looks cute in these but since I picked a light color, I can see dimples… I imagine this would not be an issue with darker shades. To be totally fair, I have not yet worn these extensively so I will update later if need be but I had to hop on here and let the really short ladies know to order these!!! I DREAD having to take stuff to the tailor and always pray I can find a pair that fits in the waist and in length… Hope this helps! **Updated original review to add pic of the back of the pants. Still loving these pants! I wash with detergent (no fabric softener) and hang them to dry!

Oh my goodness… First of all, the important part is I am 5’10” & 120lbs (in those photos), I bought a medium, tall, the 33” inseam, in white, khaki and black. And I am only wearing flats. Posting several pics to see the fit, plus for proof its not possible to take a bad pic in Europe. Anyway, I bought these to wear on our three week trip. I wanted something I could beat up but still look dressy & fabulous. Since we had never been to Europe and only brought a backpack each, I was really nervous about my clothes. But let me tell you, I would buy these pants all over again. I honestly felt like I one upped all the younger, high fashion girls in Milan and Capri. Also, my husband had me vacuum seal my clothes in my backpack. So they were (neatly) crammed into a super tight bag for three weeks. They went through six flights, unknown bus and trains and multiple ABNBs. Every time I pulled them out, they were wrinkle free. They are so soft, I actually slept in them, several times. They STILL do not have stretched out knees. They cleaned up super easy and air dried every where we went (cuz they don’t have dryers in Europe). I wore the WHITE in the RAIN on the streets of ROME (i feel really spoiled saying that) and figured that would be the ultimate test. I honestly figured I would be throwing them away when we got back. After 19,000 steps in the rain, the bottoms where they hit my tennis shoe heel had some discoloration after I washed them. But there has been no shrinkage and I still wear them. I wanted a loose, classy, palazzo look and the medium, tall did exactly that. I bought the small, tall and it was too tight on my bottom for what I wanted, so I gave them to my daughter who is about five pounds smaller. But the smalls did fit. The medium doesn’t feel like they are going to fall off, I felt confident in them everywhere we went. And the hips don’t look like they aren’t cut for a girl my size. I’ve purchased much more expensive pants and didn’t get 1/2 of what I am getting out of these. My only wish is they made them with a sewn in front seam and a non-yoga unwrapped waste for a more professional look. If they did, I’d probably buy the company.

The G4Free AiryBlend Wide Leg Sweatpants are well made and very soft and comfortable. I really like the fabric, it has a nice drape to it that makes the fit very flattering and it doesn’t show every little bump and bulge. The fabric has a very slight shine that elevates the look of these pants. All the seams are neatly finished both inside and out. These sweatpants are a step up from your average sweatpants and to me, they seem more refined and slightly dressier. You could wear these pants anywhere. They are not just for the gym. There is a cinch cord at the waist that works so much better than the usual ties. The cinch cord allows you to quickly and easily adjust the size of the waist and it holds firmly in place. The pants are just the right fit, not too loose or too baggy. I ordered the 31" inseam and the pair I received is actually a 32" inseam. I like that these come in regular and tall sizes because we all know that one size does not fit all.For reference, I am 5’9” and I weigh 195 lbs. I normally wear an XL and I ordered these in an XL. These pants fit me well but the waist does seem a little smaller and tighter than my usual XL sweatpant's waist. But they are still comfortable enough to wear all day. The length for me was perfect and they don’t require any hemming. These sweatpants are well made, look great and feel great.

Good Quality, Well-Made, Will Keep You Warm & Stylish! At 69, and after a long illness that left me with no meat on my bones, I'm freezing when it's below 75°F. I got these fleeced-lined pants to survive Vegas winters - yes, it gets cold here too! These arrived faster than my need for an extra blanket, well-packaged and looking like they could take on snowstorm (or at least a stiff desert breeze).First impression: the pants are excellent quality, with strong stitching and soft fleece lining that'll keep you toasty through autumn and winter. The straight leg design is a dream - comfortable, stylish, and not too tight (although I could've gone for a snugger fit with all the weight lost - working on getting that back, thiugh!).Two back pockets? Fake, but hey, they make my backside look great! Two deep side pockets? Real and big enough for my phone, keys, and maybe even a snack or two. These pants can go from grocery run to fancy dinner without missing a beat, making me look trendy and pulled together, no matter what the occasion.At 5'4" and feeling more like a shrimp than ever (hovering around 100lbs), this 31" insem is perfect - I've got long legs, and these pants know how to show them off. All in all, they're worth every penny for the warmth, comfort, and versatility they bring. Highly recommended for anyone - no matter your size or how cold you get when the thermostat dips below "mild."

So comfy.. and works for any height! For starters, I’m super excited the 33 inch inseam is actually long enough for tall girls! I am 5‘10“ tall and almost never find pants on Amazon that go to the ground. These are really comfortable a nice medium weight and really soft lining, which also makes them hide lumps and bumps better than most lounge pants. There are seams and pockets, almost make them look like a slack, but personally, I think the material gives them away as an athletic style pants instead. I plan to wear mine when I am working remote in case I stand up on a video call for some reason. For reference, I ordered a size medium in the 33” inseam and they fit perfectly. I am 5’10”, as I mentioned previously, 167lbs, 29” waist, 40” hips and typically wear either a medium or large or around a size 8 or 29/30 in pants.

Leisure Wear Only Got these royal blue leggings to wear to watch Football on Sundays, but they’re just a miss. They are soft. However, they are thin enough to show off cellulite and could be see through depending on your undergarments and how stretched the fabric is. The size Small has too much extra fabric in the crotch area for my 122lb body = camel toe if I put my legs together. This is immediately out of the package, before they get stretched out, so it would only get worse. This is really minor, but the pockets are already starting to flip out away from the body, right out of the package. I’ve bought other $20ish leggings from Amazon that have been great but they didn’t have the color I wanted. Could keep these for lounging around the house in or wearing as long johns, but I know I’d get annoyed with the inevitable sagging. So, I’ll be returning them.

The PERFECT yoga pants! These are THE perfect yoga pants. I own 7 pair (2 being the Capri pants). From home to gym, these pants are great! No see-through, no fade, perfect fit, love the pockets. I love how it hugs my tummy. I believe it says line dry, but I have no issues putting them in the dryer on low heat. I recently lost 50 lbs in 4 months going from 170 lbs to 120 lbs. I’m 5’4” in height. When I was 170 lbs size Large fit best. I’m currently in a size Medium at 120 lbs, but I can tell that they are getting slightly big and I may need to size down to Small. Hope this helps! You’re making a great purchase!

Soft, great fit, good coverage & form-fitting pockets They look great on me and are really soft. I’m 5’4 and I range between 115-120 lbs. I didn’t have high expectations. They fit perfect, but are slightly on the smaller side, so before the wash — they seem perfect, but I am worried about them getting slightly smaller after the wash/dryer. They would probably still fit, but this is to keep in mind for anyone who may be the heavier side. My favorite part besides the softness / comfort is how the pockets stay in position whether you just had a lot of stuff in them or not, which is great if you don’t want that heavy pocket look for when you don’t have anything in the pockets. I’m definitely going to consider ordering more black pairs & their other colors! I haven’t tried working out in them, I just planned on wearing them around the house or to do errands outside, but they’re nice enough to wear with an outfit if you wanted to.

I'm not crazy about them at all 😒 I ordered these because I was looking for a good workout legging with the side pocket for my phone. An influencer that I follow suggested these but I don't like them at all. First, they had a gross smell out of the package 🤢 They run a little big, like I know they're high waisted, but I can pull them up to right under my boobs. Makes me feel like Urkel 😒 I ordered a 2xl and should've ordered the xl, but you never know how clothing is gonna fit when ordering online, and honestly, things are usually too small. And I'm short to begin with (5'3") but these are ridiculously LONG! I had to roll them 3x. Also, they are a little see through in the bum, so that won't work for the gym. The side pockets are very weird, I've never seen one like this before. It goes down into a V, and gathers annoyingly at the bend in my knee ( see 2nd photo). I don't care if these are Callie's FAV leggings, they are terrible! I don't recommend at all.

Super high waist, buttery soft For $10 a pair in a three pack, I’m not complaining too loudly. Pros: For a pale blue, you can barely see my black with lemon panty when I squat. But not when I’m standing which is surprising. They are so soft!! The fit is incredibly generous- I’m 5’6, just shy of 180 and have the L/XL. I could still fit another 30 pounds in here easily. No muffin top when I fold the waistband down. Good length. I have longer legs and these still hit mid-calf, which was the goal. Cons: These will snag very easily on uneven or rough surfaces. These do not stay up while running etc, but maybe just because they are not snug on me. Pilling is a guarantee. These are really made for walking to the store, working from home, being cute under an oversized sweater or hoodie. These are NOT suitable for working out. The waist comes to my bra. That’s not high waisted, that’s a catsuit! But the band folds over nicely which is how I wear them.

Too wide in hips and waist My daughter and I were debating whether to order these in my daughter’s regular size small or go down a size and order x-small. She is 5’5” 115 lbs and wears size small in jeans. According to the included chart these numbers put her in size small. However, her hip and waist measurements put her in size x-small according to the chart. Ultimately we decided to get size small because we have a couple of other pairs of jeans from this brand and size small in those fits her great. Unfortunately, these pants are too big for her in the waist and the hips. In the pictures I took the frontal view looks great but make sure to look at the back view picture too, so you can see how my daughter tightened them with a shoe lace to make them wearable. The fabric content according to the tag is 65% cotton 20% polyester 15% spandex. I’m not a fabric specialist by any means but 15% spandex? I have never seen jeans with such a high spandex content. And wouldn’t that make them very, very stretchy? These jeans however are not very stretchy. They are definitely not all cotton because they are soft for denim and have some give but I would not describe them as stretchy denim. All in all, the style is trendy, the workmanship is good, but size wise I wish they were running more true to the standard size small jeans.

Not true to pictures, but love what I got more than what I was shown! What arrived is PERFECT! But not at all like the photos shown, so I wanted to leave this review. Please excuse the wrinkles -- the pants are straight out of the packaging. • It's ankle length (which might be because I'm on the short-side, at 5'3"). • The fabric is thicker than cheap Amazon products. It still has a lovely flow as you move and is light for summer. Most importantly, it's not at all see-through!!! • The color is a slightly more vibrant blue than jeans, so easy to style with. I find it works with my tops from multiple seasons (spring/summer/fall). Could wear leggings underneath to keep enjoying these pants in colder seasons. • The waistband is perfectly comfortable. It doesn't pinch at all, and smooths out my midsection to make it very flattering. • The pockets are great!! My whole phone fits inside and you can't even tell my phone is there. I've already ordered a second pair. Hope this review was and you like them, too!

Ugh so cute and comfortable Photos don't do them justice- it's all in the movement! These pants have a heavy drape and flow beautifully like a midi skirt, while keeping my inner thighs covered for the added feeling of security pants provide, and to prevent chub rub! I ordered these in size large and they fit me perfectly. For reference, I am 5'5", and I have a 31" waist, and 45" hips. Very pear shaped with thick backside and legs. It's next to impossible to find pants or bottoms in general that fit and flatter me, and I hate showing off my legs, even in the heat of summer. These pants are the perfect solution! They're so freakin' breezy and comfortable to wear, and I love the flat waistband. (It's so hard to find a nice-fitting waistband without a drawstring!) I have machine washed these and hung to dry, with a tiny bit of a fluff up in the dryer once dry, and they didn't shrink at all. There were a few loose threads that I need to cut and fix, but I'm just so happy to have found comfy and beautiful summer pants, that I don't even care about small details like perfect quality 😂😂. I'm going to buy these in every colour, and become "that girl". (You know, "that girl who always wears those loose pants"?) Summer is going to be so much more bearable now. Yay.

I purchased the size small. It fits comfortably around my 27.5" waist, but I have to shimmy a bit to get the waistband over my 36.5" hips. I have a 30" inseam making the pant leg end in a good place. The cotton/poly fabric was a bit course (not scratchy) because of the higher cotton content, but as I have been wearing it and I think with washing it will soften up more. I have had compliments on the culottes so, I think it was a really good choice. I enjoy wearing them.

No longer as good I originally bought a pair of these pants in dark gray in 11/2020. The quality was good, they’ve held up to washing, and the material was on the thicker side and a soft fleece type. I tried ordering a new pair in 10/2022 in light gray, but the pair I received seemed so much thinner than my old pair, and seemed to have a cheap/flat sherpa-esque lining (but not like the products they have that are actually advertised as sherpa—I have no complaints about Gihuo sweatshirts for example! So I suspect the sweatpants that are advertised to have sherpa lining are all right, those are just too hot for my legs). I thought maybe it was a fluke, some companies like this accidentally mail the wrong model when they have a lot of varieties. I tried ordering these pants again in 01/2023 in dark gray, to compare to the pair I already had, and brown. They definitely are not the same anymore. The new pair was much thinner in material and didn’t feel as well-made as before. The new pair also had that flat cheap fur-like lining (think like, if you got “warm” leggings from Five Below). Even the string ties seemed a flimsier material. The brown pair mysteriously seemed somewhat better made, and slightly better feeling on the inside (less fur-like, but still not as thick material as the old dark gray pair I have). I’m pretty disappointed honestly, and wish I just got multiple pairs earlier. I would not recommend these pants as they are now. Gihuo’s sherpa-lined sweatshirts are great, so those pants that are also sherpa-lined may be a better alternative, but I just wanted house pants for the winter and I know those will absolutely roast my legs. (Side note: I ordered these in Medium, but personally, I think Small would’ve fit better if I were keeping them. 5’2” 135 lbs, hips are around 39”)

Warm and flattering Very thick, warm sherpa. I’ve only tested it in 25F weather but my legs stayed perfectly warm. While still being soft and comfortable, these have enough structure to still be somewhat flattering (not why I bought them, so a good plus). To be clear, these will still make you look 10-15lbs heavier, but for me the warmth is well worth it! At 5’2”, 26.5” waist, 37” hips, a size small fits well. *edit: after a year of use, I am very happy with these. They’re not too warm to lounge around the house in (and save a bit on heating costs) but still kept my legs cozy in -1F (-27F with windchill)!

Warm, soft, stitched well, comfy, length not accurate For reference I am 5 feet 11 inches and 168 pounds. I ordered a medium size. Positives: *These are warm-I live in an area that gets down to the teens in Fahrenheit in the winter. So far my mornings have been in the twenties and these joggers keep me warm while I walk my dogs. *Soft- I have mild sensory issues and the lining is incredibly soft to me. The lining is soft and I believe will wash and dry well over time. I washed and dried before wearing. (I use white vinegar as fabric softener in the wash and dryer balls in the dryer.) I’m not sure how they would hold up if you used traditional fabric softener though. *stitched well- Only washed once but the stitching seems to be done very well. I think these will last quite a while. I like the zipper pockets as well. *comfy- They’re not too tight around the stomach. They feel comfortable to sit in. They do add a little bulk which is to be expected with the lining being so thick. The mediums are snug fit. If you want a more loose fit I suggest ordering a size up. Con: the length is not accurate. For a medium pant, the sizing says the length is 38-ish inches. I measured the pair I received at around 37 inches (see picture). If you’re not tall then it won’t be an issue. However, being 5’11, they shorter than I like. I still wear them as they are long enough to tuck into a tall boot. I am ordering another pair in a large and with the elastic cuff (not wide cuff) so we’ll see what kind of difference that makes.

Cozy, warm, TINY WAIST, very short- for PETITES only TINY WAIST. According to the description on Amazon, a size medium is a 29”-30”. These pants, medium, are between 23” and 24” waist, usually an extra small. Also, I just washed and dried them, thinking maybe the elastic would expand a little. Now not only are they even tighter in the waist, they are now 3 inches shorter. Great idea. Terrible product in terms of usefulness. If you want to order them, I would suggest two sizes larger than your normal size, understanding that the legs and hips will be baggier, but the waist and length may be better. Positives: So cozy and warm. UPDATE: I returned the medium and just received the large. Ordered the style with the zipper pockets, hoping it would be a larger more comfortable waist with a slightly different style. Nope. Baggy, dropped crotch about 4" down, maybe 1" larger waist. Very odd cut, to me. The zippered pocket style is stiff where the little plastic zipper is, bulky, just so disappointed because the fabric is GREAT, so warm. Then ordered another pair, very comfy. However, do not order style #2, jogger, if you're over 5"4"- VERY short., especially after washing and drying. The key is to order exactly the right style # and size, and they're fabulous. Then ordered XL full leg pants. Quite large, except again, the waist is so tight. Very dissatisfied and finally threw them away.

Great Fit and Extremely Warm! Love these Sherpa Lined Pants! Bought these Light Grey Sherpa Lined Pants for my 79 year old Mother who has Neuropathy in her legs,Her legs stay cold all the time so I got these hoping that they would help. My Mother absolutely loves her new Sherpa Lined Pants and says that they definitely have helped keep her legs warm especially when she is out walking her little Dog. I was concerned with what size to buy her with all the conflicting reviews about the sizing but I decided to buy her a size small and they fit her well but are a little long but since they have the elastic at the bottom of the pants it doesn't seem to be a problem for her. For information purposes on her height and weight, she is 5'0 tall and weighs 112 lbs and the size small fits her good. I was so happy to see that these pants fit her so well and are keeping her warm this Winter.

Stretchy, comfy, flattering, hides mom pooch, what else can I ask for??? Very stretchy and comfortable pair of leggings! They are on the warmer side so I wouldn’t wear them once it gets hot, but they are so flattering! I am 5’2”, weigh around 125lbs, and usually wear a size 2 or 4 depending on the brand so I got these in a small. These are petite friendly! I don’t need to fold them up on the bottom because the little bit of wrinkle is not enough to make me want to fold them. If you’re tall, then these leggings would be more ankle length though there is a lot of stretch! They are reflective, but I don’t have to worry about my fingers making weird stretch marks on the material while pulling them up. I can wear underwear and it doesn’t show up very much though a thong would definitely be a better choice. I also own the faux leather leggings by Spanx and honestly I feel like these fit more comfortably... not to mention are way cheaper! For reference, I’m a small petite in spanx and the top of those rolls down quite a bit due to my mommy pooch. On the contrary, these leggings don’t roll down and are very forgiving on belly fat. Compared to Spanx, these also have more stretch. They go up to cover my bellybutton and the effect is like wearing shapewear! If you have a mom pooch or wider legs, order your usual size. If you have slim legs (despite having a belly pooch), you can afford to order a size down. My mom tried these on and she’s a reverse triangle shape (large top and small bottom) and usually wears a S or M. For these leggings, she tried on my size S and I thought they were too big on her because they were a bit wrinkly by the legs. I plan on ordering her an XS. The upper waist part of the S fit her just fine but with the level of stretch this has, she’ll be ok in an XS too. In my opinion, you want them to stretch tight to get that sexy reflective leather look! Overall, I am super pleased with these leggings! They are not see through AT ALL, and have a layer on the inside for warmth and comfort. They hold my mom pooch in to the point I can tuck a shirt in at the front and not feel self conscious, and they make me look super shapely and sexy. It’s a win!

Bought these for a concert. I ordered my usual size small (148# 28" waist, big thighs lol) They are very difficult to put on, but once you do they look great. As someone else suggested they go on better if you pull them up like pantyhose. They are smoothing and hide any flaws :) Once you get them on they are stretchy but have great compression. The light felt lining feels nice. They are not at all see through and seem very durable. They do seem to run a tad smaller than regular leggings so you may want to size up. I'm keeping the smalls because they suck me in more Lol. They make this almost 50 year old lady feel sexy!

They feel expensive and are perfection! I ordered these pants in black last year and loved them so much. Came across these in this merlot color and thought I would give them a try. Absolutely love them just as much as the black ones. They are controlling yet stretchy. When I wear them to work I would always catch on to the dishwasher (I bartend) and would fling me back. Never tears scratches or rips. They have a good material in the inside where it’s not leathery but more like a cloth material. It’s not a liner but just how the material on the other side is. You can flip them on the waist if u don’t want high waisted. These come up to my waist around my belly button. I’m 5’7” 135 pounds and these are a small. Update: love them so much had to add the brown pair to my collection. I have the black red navy blue and brow. I bartend in these and always get caught in corners and slingshot back with no scratches or tears. So impressed and get a ton of compliments. As stretchy as they are the compression is impressive. My co workers are all different sizes and shapes and love theirs just as much! Worth every single penny. 🤎🤎🤎🤎🤎🤎 Another update; bought them in green too. Love them! They’re the most comfortable brand of “leather” leggings. My only complaint would be the waist area gets sweaty but no other places. Im more than ok with that. So many compliments. I hear people spend near $100 for the spanx brand. I haven’t bought them before and have no reason since these work perfectly for me.

Im 5'6 230ish lbs. Wide hips, not much butter but a belly. Normally size 18 pants. I've bought these before in black and just purchased the brown and red. I love them. I got the xx large and they're perfect. I have a tummy and thick thighs with pretty normal size calves. They come right to the belly button and don't roll down and don't have a ton of wrinkles from being too large. They're tight enough to smooth and flatter but not show every cellulite dimple! I haven't worn the new brown and red ones yet but they're the same as the black ones I bought a while back and are sweaty in warm weather but comfy in the winter.

I ordered 2 sizes down to get compression I’m 5’3” about 140. Normally I order down a size in leggings because I like the tight compression feel. My recommended size on Amazon was the medium. So I ordered these in small. They were a tad loose around the ankles so I returned them and ordered them in the XS. The XS is what i have pictured. I think my waist is about 28” right now. They fit like a latex glove. They don’t show any thing. Total confidence booster. I went back and ordered them in the blue and black. The navy color is to close to black for me to keep them. I had no trouble getting them on, plenty of stretch. But do not try and put these on after lotion lol. These will keep you looking warm and sexy. I have them with a tee and tennis shoes but I’m sure the black ones would look really cute with a crop top. They suck you in tight like that famous brand that’s $100 per pair. Get em if u like em!

Almost perfect... Ok... I’m so torn on these. I love the way the look, I love the way they feel. Very comfortable with lots of Stretch. Im 5’8 and they did reach my ankles no problem. Sizing is a little interesting. I’m 36’ bust 28’ waist 36’ hips... according to the size chart I should have got a medium but I went with a small. My biggest issue with these pants were the seams. The stitching is so poor! It’s literally coming apart at the seams. I purchased them right before an upcoming event so I literally had no time to create another outfit. I have a super handy husband and he re sewed some of the seams for me to give it extra support because I was literally afraid to leave the house because I thought they would just burst while out in public. I wore them to the event and when I took them off... again the threads were coming undone... if these were just properly stitched there wouldn’t be any issue. First time in leather leggings sooo I think I’m just going to opt for the spanxs version so I know quality will be on point. Hope this helps.

Spanx-like leggings for a fraction of the price! I’m 5”3’ and around 140. Athletic build. I ordered a size Small. Very very slight castor in the front, but it’s not noticeable and will probably disappear when i actually wear them. Holy moly ravioli. BUY THESE NOW. I have very little texture on my thighs that I was afraid I’d see when wearing these… SMOOTH, AND JUST THE RIGHT AMOUNT OF SHINE! They’re like Spanx for pants. I have worked in couture boutiques on 5th Ave in NYC so I know the difference between inexpensive and cheap. These are excellent quality and they hold everything in place without a dimple in sight! Update:: I work retail and it was very busy the day I wore these, and they were HOT. Just realized they were fleece lined. So if you are going to a club or doing something where you’ll sweat, these will either make you feel nasty or really, really good because you’re sweating out any fat you have winkwink. Still love em! Don’t hesitate!

Too pleather-y looking for me.... I really wanted to love these leggings, the reviews were hit or miss but the price was right so I just went for it. And unfortunately I made the wrong decision bc they just weren’t doin it for my 5’3 135lbs body... I ordered a medium & they were def too long (but most pants r at my height😕) and too tight in all the wrong places! Plus I noticed that the sheen/shine intensified too much while stretched against my body, so that nice matte leather look, turns into cheap plastic pleather real quick! I ended up returning them & the seller was nice enough to give me a full refund which I didn’t think twice about & I put it toward the so called, “Holy Grail” of faux leather leggings... SPANX! Of course I paid about $85+tax for them but I learned long ago that u def get what u pay for & those extra $$ are usually put toward making your ass look phenomenal! Which is always something I’m willing to pay extra for😉 lol!

Chanced it and very happy I did! Ordering online is a hit and miss so I was very skeptical. After reading many reviews I decided to give it a try. I'm 5'2 and 3/8 haha, 145lbs, measurements for my waist is 32in and 39in for hips, mom of 5 with my youngest being 5mo. I was unsure what size to get so I chanced it and got a Medium. They are very comfortable and easy to get on and off. Mine had 0 " funky " smell like I read on other reviews. I haven't wore them out so I'll update on that later. They are form fitting but not too tight and stretchy and I had no camel when I pulled them up higher over my love handles lol. They are perfect length for my short legs, even with 6in heels were great! They will show a panty line so if you're not into that wear a thong or nothing at all.

Other than having to size up, awesome pants! I'm 5'5" and 125 lbs. I ordered the black color first and then returned them for the brown, so I'll start with the black. I cannot stand super-tight waistbands so although a size small sometimes fits, I pretty much always go with a medium in leggings. The size medium in these was waaaaaay too tight. I was able to get them on, but they were really uncomfortable. Yes, they had a horrible smell. Is that a reason to give a bad review? No. The smell will dissipate in a few days or you can just wash them. It was almost gone by the second day I had them open. I personally don't feel you should listen to reviews that are negative because of a temporary smell. Okay, now for the brown pants. I returned the black and decided to order the brown in a size large. I LOVE them. They're expensive looking and soft on the inside (see pic)! They sit very comfortably on my waist aside from a teeny tiny bit of extra room in the back, but not enough for me to care. They're a tad bit squeaky between the thighs, but again, not enough for me to care. I feel like these are going to go with everything I wear this winter and I can't wait! And for whatever reason, the brown did NOT have a horrible smell :)

Relaxed fit, runs a bit loose and long Ordered two pair of these, an 8L (recommended by Amazon) and a 6L (because 6 is my usual size in pants). For reference, I’m 5’7” and 125 lbs, abt a 37” hip. The 8L were big, boxy, too long and too loose (wrong size); the 6L were a better fit—definitely not tight, though at least 4” too long. Returned both these Long-sized Amazon orders, and since Amanda regular 6 was not available, I ordered a pair in that size elsewhere. The reg 6 fits better, though still longer than other regular-sized jeans I own (a plus—can launder without worry). The Amanda style is a more loose-fit style than skinny jeans, and definitely look looser on me than on the model. But they are a higher-waisted, stretchy style, esp good for when you’re going to be doing a lot of sitting (eg. travel). *UPDATE: The 6R seemed to stretch out and get even looser after a few days of travel wearing last week. Based on reviews by others my size or larger, I ordered a size 4R (Zermatt), even though Amazon was now suggesting I order 6L. The 4R is actually the best fit in this Amanda style, and is still plenty long. *PHOTOS are of the 4R. Third time’s a charm! (These pants did need to be washed right away due to strong chemical smell.)

Jeans fit great, color does not match image These are the same color as the Park City color, except these have gold stitching. I would keep them if I didn't already have the Park City. I ordered these because I wanted them in a very dark wash, like the picture indicates. Unfortunately, the color is wrong. Aside from that, I love the fit of these jeans on my curvy figure. I'm 5'8" and the length is perfect too! They fit me in the waist and the hips, no gapping at the back waist, which is a miracle for me. I wish I could trust the images to show the correct color. I would love a dark wash in these.

Great jeans... but don't do what I did! lol! Returned another brand to try these. Bought them in white. I'm 5'6.5 and 138 pounds (feel my normal weight should be 130 to 133... I'm working on it, lol!) Anyway, I bought the size 6 short (I'll explain the short part at the end). I could button them, but when you run around in leggings most days, normal pants can feel a little too snug. I thought, "I know! I'll wet them and stretch them over a chair!" No! Bad idea! the next day, they were huge! I wore them and had to pull them up constantly, lol! So I wet them again and put them in the dryer. Fortunately, they tightened up - but that's the point. JUST wear them! Sit in them, walk around. They will loosen up on their own and feel fine! I even thought the thigh felt a little too snug... no... they're fine. Just needed to break them in!The reason I bought short (I normally don't) was just because I heard they ran long... and in the summer, I like to roll up my jeans - especially white ones - just for the look (I like them casual and relaxed) - so I thought if I get short, less rolling and I was right. They're just what I wanted!

True to size After reading a lot of reviews, I took a chance and ordered the pants. I have an apple shape with no butt, so it's hard to find pants that will go around my waist. These fit great! I'm 5'9" and 183 lbs. I bought 18 long and I'm very had happy. If I had a smaller waist, I would have bought smaller size. I like the give in the stretchy fabric. Very comfortable.

Buy these Jeans cause They're Great I bought a size larger than I usually wear because I like wearing roomy jeans. A size 14 would have been more snug and fit perfectly but I like clothes that are comfortably loose. I am 5'7. I immediatly tried them on -- perfect fit for my taste -- then I put them in the washer first and dryer 2nd. They didn't shrink at all! The dryer (with a dryer sheet) actually made them even softer. The color is dark blue and they didn't fade onto the other clothes in the washer at all. A white pillow case came out white! They are comfortable and when I stand up the legs touch my shoe tops. The picture was taken while setting with my legs stretched out.

Perfect in every way! I bought a size 4 in Stone. I'm 5'6"" and 126# and generally wear a 4/6-Sm. This is a great true fit, very true to size. It's not too big and not too baggy. The pants are soft and not scratchy. I'm so happy with them I've already purchased two other colors.

Way better than I had expected Usually I’ll buy some fashion nova jeans in a 15 for $30 and they’ll show up a little tight but stretch out in a few hours. Few months in they’ll tear at the thigh anti-gap lol. A coworker convinced me to get jeans at Target in July. Got 3 pairs of “Levi’s” in a 14 (because they’re stretchy) for like $40/ea. Literally wearing these things to work every day because that’s the only thing I can do through this apocalypse. Some how a pair of these garbage pants ripped in a month. A MONTH. So. I’m on amazon and I see these here bad boys on sale for $9 and I figure I should honestly just buy six pairs and call it a night. Just in case though I upped it to a size 16. If they run small, they’ll fit. If they run regular, I’ll wear a belt. Well guess what. They run regular. The delivery lady Just dropped them off and I pulled one on and these bad boys are so comfortable, but I ordered them big lol. Anyway, I’ll wear a belt to work. I got two pairs but I came back to buy two more, but the sale is over, sad day, but not that sad. They seem way sturdier than the ones on fashion nova AND these pants not only have 4 1/2 Real Pockets but I can also fit my entire hand in the front ones. Heck, I can fit a couple TV remotes in the front pockets. I’m definitely coming back, and even though the $9 sale is over, full price is still a steal compared to Target’s “Levi’s” For reference I’m about 5’5 and 197lbs this week but I’m kindof dense in the bones. A lot of my weight is butt, a lot of it is thighs...these jeans don’t quite do the butt justice but that’s definitely on me because I purposely ordered them too big haha if I figure out how to update this review, I’ll edit to add pictures when I buy the right size

Love Gloria Vanderbilt Amanda jeans , I have worn this brand for years . They fit my body better than any others . I have another patterned jeans from GV and have been searching for another. Finally found it on Amazon got them by using try before you buy. On receiving them, I tried them on immediately sent them to the washing machine turned wrong side out and wash them and dry them. I tried them on again and they were still perfect. No shrinkage immediately notified Amazon and Purchased absolutely love them and get many compliments whenever I wear them I am 5 foot six and 165 pounds I purchased a 12 short and it works for me.

Dont Think This Style's Good for Petites/Shorts, Otherwise Nice Im 5'5" a little over 35.5" in waist, 45" hips, 24" diameter thighs and 16" diameter legs/calves, no thigh gap. What drew me to these pants were that they were high waisted and on a lightning deal for like 20 bucks. I didnt think much about the cut beyond the waist but figured tapered legs would be attractive. I think the deal breaker for me is the style. They look kind of like straight legs on me, and since I have large thighs it's just more fabric than I want in the leg area. I dont like how the fabric bunches behind my knee and though this isn't entirely on the jeans I don't think they're doing my bum any favors. I did go a size larger than i probably should have with the 16, I just hated the idea of the top of the jeans pinching my stomach. I also got the regular instead of the short but I dont think the tapers sharp enough for the extra length to make a difference here, meaning to give shape to my legs. I think the taper's only really attractive if your thighs arent super disproportionate to your legs. Maybe like 2 or 3 inches max wider diameter in the thighs in relation to your calves. At least for the fit I want. I was gonna send these back in exchange for some 14's but I think I'll have to go with one of the other cuts to get the fit I'm looking for. Besides the fit I think these were a good buy for the price. Nice stretch and color. not as thick as some denim blends, a bit thicker than like denim leggings. Comfortable to move around in, liked fit at the waist. Better than most things you'd find between 20 - 30. If I had to recommend based on fabric and comfort vs leg fit I'd recommend.

Fit is great, continues to look NEW Great jeans, I have purchased several times. After washing they still look NEW! At 72 it is difficult to find jeans that flatter but these definitely do! Try before you buy if not sure if size is a great feature. These jeans are not so heavy or light that they cannot be worn in ALL seasons. Sizing chart is accurate and true to size. They do not shrink when washed nor do they fade! At 5’5” the short still works for me ending just above my shoe heal. Average requires a 1 inch hem or simple turn up! (Yes, that fashion is coming back for jeans) Enjoy your Vanderbilt’s, I know I do!

Decreasing quality I love the Amanda jeans and have worn them for years. I bought the lighter Amanda jeans from this listing two years ago, and the darker Portland Wash jeans last week. The finishing on the new pair is so much more raggedy than the older pair after many washings! The fabric content is slightly different: the lighter pair is 73% Cotton, 25% Polyester, 2% Elastane and the darker is 72% Cotton, 26% Polyester, 2% Elastane. The first is made in Jordan, the second in Kenya. I did buy two other pairs in the two year interim, and I returned them as the “fits at natural waist” was uncomfortably well above my belly button. The pairs I kept both needed to be hemmed, but I’m used to that. I’m 5’4” with a 29” inseam. The Short length is too short at 28”, and the Regular length is too long at 30”. This pair of jeans fit comfortably and the darker color is more dressy, which is what I wanted. I’m glad I have a pair I can keep!

Could be worse for the price so I’m going to keep them

They ok I guess lol kmart Gloria Vanderbilt my mom used wear these back in the day . I miss Kmart so much . They are charcoal black. darker than the picture. They are a thin material . Soo Comfortable and lookin and feelin fantastic!! I have always bought my pants in a box store. After having four kids, and now going through the beginnings of menopause, buying clothes is not my favorite thing to do. In fact, I downright loathe it. All my jeans have become too tight now, so I’ve been wearing a lot of sweatpants and t-shirts which makes me feel sloppy and like a dirtbag when I’m out and about. When you look good and are dressed decent, you just feel good. I am sooo glad I found these jeans!! I have come to terms that I will never be a size 5 ever again, and so finding the right fit and comfortable jeans have just been almost impossible until now!! These are so dang comfortable, but look good and do a good job being flattering to all my lumps and bumps! I washed these first thing, and they are just so soft and feel beautifully already broke in! I’m 5’5 and these are the perfect length. Not too short not too long. I would call these more straight legged though. Not a super taper which I hate the struggle of taking super tapered pants off. I am just so ecstatic to take a chance on these jeans and now I feel like a million bucks! So much , I bought 3 more pairs!! Update- back to buy 2 more pairs, they are that good! Wash great and just get softer Another update- here to buy some more of these jeans. I’ve read a couple of negative reviews, but for most people they are going to love these jeans. I haven’t found a more comfortable jean that moves with you like these do and fits so well. I’d say for pple 5’3 to 5’5 just order regular sizes, not petite. Anything taller than 5’5 order tall or long.

Big waist gap Size 22 Plus - regular length - Black 5'2" - 235lbs - short torso - large booty I've got what I call a " booty shelf". My bum sticks out a lot, and you could almost set stuff on it. So to be fair, a lot of women my height and weight may not have this issue... The waist gap is big with these jeans. No way can I wear them. When they slide down, which they will inevitably do, they'll still show way too much booty/undies, especially if I bend over and/or my shirt rides up. The regular length is long on me, but that's not unusual. Otherwise the fit seems fine in the hips/thighs. I'm returning them, so of course I don't know how much they'll shrink in the wash. These will probably work great for people without a booty shelf. Since I can't wear them I'm giving 4 stars, but for a different shaped body they could easily be 5 star jeans.

So nice, but too big These pants were super soft & were the perfect length. Usually when I get “short” pants they hit right above my ankle even though I’m just under 5’2 smh. These did not, but they were way too loose in the waist & gave me a bit of a pooch in the crotch area. Because of the softness and stretch, these pants would most likely become larger after a wash instead of shrinking. I got my normal size 16S Plus (couldn’t beat $10!), but need the 14S and they aren’t even available at all. Find them in your proper size and you won’t be disappointed 💕

Extremely comfortable but stretchy, stretchy These 22Plus, Gloria Vanderbilt jeans have a huge amount of elastic give which does not allow it be body conforming in the places you want them to be. For example, they do not help you look shapely in the tush, hips and tummy. I am 5'8, 260 pounds with a generous tummy. The jeans do not hide much tummy nor subdue hips and are very loose in the bottom side of the rear, & legs due to the stretch. Without a body shaper on they are a tad loose everywhere. With a shaper on, they are tighter but still comfortable. Yet to me, still not the most flattering look. They fit a tad bit like mom jeans in my opinion. I almost exchanged them for a 20 Plus just to get a more flattering silhouette. But I decided against it as one thing that they definitely are is the most comfortable jeans I ever wore. So, I decided to keep them as I am of the age (56) that comfort trumps style. My first designer jeans at age 14/15 in 1979 were Gloria Vanderbilts (see me in them in 3rd pic) . So buying these were like visiting an old friend that you greatly missed. If nothing else, I am decidely loyal to old friends and our fond memories.

So glad I bought these!!!! I work outside for a small delivery company( starts with an A) and wanted something to keep me warm and dry on rainy days. I am very happy with these pants. I worked 5 days last week, and I wore these for 3 of those days. The temp was 40-44F for a high and 30s at night. I wouldn’t want to wear them if it were any warmer though. But that’s just me, I get heated quickly when I am constantly moving. I work 10 hour days and deliver around 100+ packages each shift. They keep me warm and dry for sure. They are also comfortable. I am 5’5” between 145 and 150 lbs, and I am wearing the medium size. I can’t think of anything negative to say about them. I am most certainly getting me another pair to put into my work rotation.

Warm and much better than I anticipated them to be I ripped my ski pants while skiing in CO last week and needed another pair dropped to me in an Amazon box that weren’t the expensive ones at the resort. I ordered these and was pleasantly surprised and impressed by how warm they are and how well fitted they are. I’m 5’8” and 180 lbs and a TTS 12. I ordered the XL and they are baggy on me without layers on. Layered with fleece lined leggings, they fit perfectly. I’m going to buy a size large to wear when skiing above 37 degrees and I don’t need layers. They are also the perfect length for me (I have a 32.5” inseam).

As advertised I was looking for a pair of warm, wind/waterproof pants to pull on over leggings for walking the dog. Didn't want large bell bottoms. Didn't want bulky ski pants (already have those). These are definitely warm and wind/waterproof, but not really for wearing over leggings unless you order up a size. They would be fine over a pair of lightweight thermals though, for extra cold days. I got the deep grey which is more a slate blueish than dark grey. They fit like a cargo pant. I have decided to keep them since winter walks here in NH can be darn cold! I am 5'6", 140 lbs and got the medium. They fit perfectly.

Perfect Snow Pants! I love everything about these pants...the style, color, fabric, warmth and how well they are made. I ordered them for Alaska, but I wear these all of the time because they’re so cute.Directions say to hand wash, but I just set the washing machine to COLD delicates and then I hang them to dry.I’m 5’4” 118 pounds. I had originally ordered the xs, but had to exchange for a small. The waist was too tight for me. The small is PERFECT.Update: I was able to use these in Alaska in -25 to -40 while mushing & viewing the Northern Lights. I DID wear thermal underwear AND a synthetic base layer underneath that was also fleece lined. These pants are still great, but for extreme cold, you can’t wear them alone. I have worn them in the teens on up, without any additional layers.Update: Also wore them in Alaska in September 2021, hiking in rainy 30 degree weather. Still love them!Update: As of Nov. 2022 they’re still great! No holes & I still wear them all of the time. 👍🏼👍🏼Update: It’s January 2024 & they’re still going strong!

Comfy, flattering and waterproof! These pants are super awesome! They really are waterproof and this is after me playing in a very wet snow with my kids. The water beads up on them. They are fleece lined and warm as well. We are in the mountains so it’s gets pretty cold (we had -14* here recently) and with layers under these were great. I’m very happy! I’m 135-140lbs, 5’-6” and I bought a medium, which leaves room for layers under but if you want a more tight fit I would size down. Im between a small and a medium and could have done a small but am happy with the layering room since I’m using them as snow pants primarily.

Great Pants, but runs Really BIG! I'm 5' 2" 110 lbs - ordered a size 2 (XS) in gray and they were really big (more like a large) so I had to return them. I ordered these recently and have worn them out hiking twice so far, in 25-35 F temps. They are VERY warm and I did not need long johns on underneath at those temperatures, though i suspect when it gets below 0 F I will. These are so well made, very comfortable. I am 5 ft 5.5" and 114 lbs and the small fits me well, though snug in the butt area as I have a naturally large butt. The elastic allows for a little wiggle room. The pant legs are slightly tapered but this works well for tucking these pants into boots. I also love the side pockets on the pant legs for little things like compasses to stash. I would highly recommend these! I was even comfortable enough to do a little dance improvisation and posing in these. :)

Do not recommend So I waited to write my review after actually being able to use it in the snow. (Snowboarding) 1. Not warm- I needed 2-3 layers of thermals just to wear this on a December day in Big Bear, CA. I can only imagine if wearing these higher up north. 2. The (butt) pockets have a small velcro closing, which allowed for snow to get in the pants. (Picture attached) I think it it were zipped completely, it would have held the snow out much better. Not to mention, that the lining was super thin and does not keep the snow out if Velcro is undone— which was difficult since I would fall on my butt when trying to snowboard. So I was freezing after about 1hr, on the slopes (I.E: sliding/falling on my butt) and eventually snow got it, and wet my under layers. I don’t personally recommend because the intended use for the product was not met. Otherwise, it’s aesthetically nice. The fitting was good, I’m 5ft and got a size Small, pretty long— could have went smaller if I wanted to be more snug.

Don’t buy if you’re tall I’m 5’10, 140 pounds and I bought a medium for the length. They fit great at the waist, though I think I could fit a medium. These left plenty of room for another layer. However, they were 4 inches too short. So disappointing. The color is gorgeous!

Runs small These are a great Tripp pants dupe. They are made of cheap fabric, but they look the part. I bought them for a Halloween costume. I ordered size Large. But unfortunately these run small and have zero stretch to the fabric. I usually wear size XL in bottoms, so I knew Large would be a bit snug, but these fit more like size Medium to me and could barely get on. I measured 16” across the waist when laying flat to help anybody else trying to get a size comparison to a current garment they own. The leg length was great, however. They are long enough on me and I’m 5’10”. Just too bad they don’t fit my waist. Have to return.

Very impressive… I love these pants. I am 225lbs and 5’4. I maybe could’ve gone down to a large but the XL works and what you see here. There is elastic in the back. I guess I didn’t notice that.The material is a cotton linen blend. They didn’t have wash instructions. So I checked Google. (Gentle cycle cold or warm, no fabric softener, and air dry. If wrinkled you iron gently with something between the hot iron and the material).I purchased the grey and the pink purple. They had a great price for natural material. I’ve washed them and can’t wait to wear them. Yay!
For reference: I'm 5'-1" tall, 135-140lbs, 39-31-39 Pants size: medium Love these pants! High-waisted, lightweight pants perfect for office wear in the summer. I'm wearing 2" heels in the picture.

Very comfortable and has pockets!! Great variety of colors to choose from. True to size, super comfortable. I love the bow tie detail on the waist and pant leg. Stretchy, comfortable dress pants! I ordered these pants in large & in the apricot color. I love them!!! They are a great color, stretchy & comfortable! Nice pockets in the front & very cute bow details. I am 5’ 9” & 170 lbs and the large was perfect!!! I would definitely recommend them!

Stretchy, comfortable dress pants! I ordered these pants in large & in the apricot color. I love them!!! They are a great color, stretchy & comfortable! Nice pockets in the front & very cute bow details. I am 5’ 9” & 170 lbs and the large was perfect!!! I would definitely recommend them!

Super cute but hear me out! I NEVER wear things like this because of my stomach pouch but I figured to broaden my horizons with a new outfit. I love it! I’m 164lbs, 5’4, and I bought an XL (fits just right, anything smaller and I wouldn’t have been able to get into it). Absolutely cute with a black shirt, BUT guys, they are super thin. I wore a bodysuit with this and you can clearly see the lines from the shirt. So one picture is stretching my butt out a little, and the other is just me standing relaxed. You can see even more if you squat. I mean… this outfit makes me pretty confident and I like it, even with the thinness to it, so I’m keeping. I even wore it out like this. It’s probably just me that overthinks the “panty lines” because it doesn’t look as good, but what can you do. I feel like that’s every outfit.
