Dressy yet casual Bought these in Fuchsia to wear to my office job. Can be dressed up or down as needed. Material feels durable with a little stretch to it. The waistband in back expands or contracts for a more versatile fit. I am 5’9” and almost 150lbs and the medium is a good fit. Runs a little short on tall people, but can be remedied with a higher boot so they won’t look like high water britches.

obsessed! Ok, first off, for reference I am 5’2.5” 195lb and Igot an XL. If you want a looser fit, size up. These pants aren’t unbearably tight but they are fitted. I have hips and a bit of a butt and I had to hop to get the elastic waist over my humps BUT once i did baby these pants had the body bodying ok? Love love love these. The belt is detached which is great because as pictured, I changed mine to a stretchy belt that really helped the high rise of the pants cinch my waist in. The pants are a light material but aren’t sheet. Obviously not particularly expensive feeling but not itchy or irritating. I worked a lengthy shift on a retail job when i got them today and I was comfortable in them all night. I ended up snipping the ties at the ankle; didn’t like that feature. but overall i like them. I believe they’re worth it for the price.

AMAZING for the bigger booty girls with long torsos (: Alright ladies. If you have a larger bum/legs then these are for you. I have always wanted a pair of pants like these but the waist is always too large or the thighs/calves are too tight. However, these pants are literally perfect. I got a medium and I am 138lbs, 5’6”, pear shaped, and have a super long torso and short legs. Most high waisted pants hit me just below my belly button but these hit above! They make my torso look shorter and my legs look longer, really balancing my proportions out more. The stretch is perfect, super comfy but still has structure. And the most important part… I don’t feel uncomfortable having my booty show at my job. I work at an elementary school and often feel as though I need to cover my bum because most tighter pants really show it off lol but these are the perfect material that gives me shape but doesn’t put my whole body on display if that makes sense (they are perfectly work appropriate). Lastly, I didn’t love the bows on the ankles, since there’s already a bow on the belt, so I literally just cut them off and you wouldn’t be able to tell at all - especially from far away. Now they’re super cute cigarette style pants (: If you’re considering these pants, just try them!!! So worth it.

They do have some stretch but holds shape well too. My biggest concern is I do have some hips and since these are only pull on with out any kind of zipper I wonder if I can get the small on.

Good fit, good quality I read various good reviews before ordering, and I'm glad I did. I really enjoy these pants. I am 5'7" and about 185 lbs, normally a size 12 pant, and I ordered an XL. They fit really well. Maybe a bit big in the waist, but I didn't want anything smaller to be tight on my thighs/behind, so I'm really happy with the sizing. The pictures don't really do justice, and I don't love this top with these pants as much (it's a looser fit, and I need something that tucks in better), but I am so pleased with these pants. I haven't washed them yet, so I don't know how they wash, but other reviews have said they wash good. If I had one complaint, it'd be that they're a touch long for me, but I just tied the bottom bow a little tighter so they don't fall down. The length would be perfect with sandals. I also LOVE the pockets. They are big enough to put my phone in (iphone 7) AND to put my hand in at the same time. Overall, the material lays/wears in a flattering way, and I love that. I couldn't be happier with these pants.

Yeah, go head and buy them sis…but my thicc girlies need to size up For reference, I’m 5’5, 180 lbs with a short torso, “shmedium” hips and nice back end. I’ll be buying another pair soon.

two different styles fit differently at waist I'm 5'4 and somewhere about 135-140 pounds. I carry more weight in my middle than my legs or hips. I bought the medium based on measurements and found the ruched waist too snug for comfort. I'm returning my medium to try a large instead. The hips weren't overly roomy, so I think I'll be good with the large. Will try to update! I bought these for an international flight hoping they'll be super comfy. EDIT : 5'4, 140lbs, size 6/8. I ordered a medium in the style without bows at the ankles. The waist was just too fitted for me for comfort, so I returned them for a large. I switched to the bows at the ankle style. What I didn't notice in photos, is that the ones with bows are only half ruched at the waist and the ones without have a fully ruched waist. Had I recognized that, I would have stuck with the original style, because going up one size was more like going up two with that variation. (see photos-- yellow is large, green is medium). The legs were too baggy now and I had ample room in the waist Ultimately, I'm returning both. The pants are soft and nice quality, but given what I just explained, neither fits. I bet a large in the bow-less ones would, but my trip is in 7 days and I don't want to order a third pair at this point. If you carry weight in your middle I'd say order your true size in the ones with bows, OR size up one in the ones without. They're cute pants, just not for my shape.

I am 5 ft 7, about 133-135 pounds (with a 26-27 inch waist), and a small fit perfectly. I have a pretty extreme waist to butt ratio (I saw some reviews that complained of a "dumpy" and/or "diaper butt" effect (LOL), and I had no such problem/have provided a butt shot for reference). If I'd ordered a medium, they probably would have looked dumpy, however. I will note that they are pretty cropped on me, given my height and my voluminous posterior. I bought them mainly to wear as spring/summer/very early fall pants, so that's fine with me, but any shoe situation involving socks would probably look goofy.

Easy care and fit These have a nice fit to them with the elastic waist and fall nicely at my ankle (5’3” height). I ordered a small and usually wear a size 4. If I were in between sizes, I’d order up a size. They wash nicely and don’t wrinkle, which is a big plus for me. They are a tad thin, so not for winter wear. And the thin material does make me feel a little self conscious about my rear end, as I feel like it shows panty lines and cellulite a little too easily. Not because it’s see through, it’s just a thin fabric. For me, trying to stay somewhat on trend with the “paper bag pants” trend, these were a good buy.

FAVORITE PANTS EVER!!!!!!!! I absolutely love these pants!!! I did a little studying on the size and fit, looking at other peoples reviews. I ordered a large, I’m 5’1 and I have to get more of my pants hemmed anyway since I’m so short but the length is perfect for me! However, if you were wanting to achieve a more cropped ankle and you’re short like me, I would suggest ordering one size down! I am usually a size 9 in jeans and business pants, and these fit me (not tight, and have a little give... which is how I like it I don’t like my pants tight around the tops of my legs! ) These pants fit just as expected but they are a stretchy material!!! Also, the waist can be pulled up to make it more high waisted (which is how I like it) . These pants are so comfortable, and lightweight! The material is a little thin so be cautious of certain underwear styles when wearing. These are definitely not a thick material if you are looking for such. I will be ordering more colors !!

Perfect Paperbag Pant Goodness was I pleased when I received this! The pant and belt came in a Grace Karin plastic product bag along with a Grace Karin thank you note! I am 5'1 and 128 lbs (pear shape) - I ordered the MEDIUM in Black. I wear this pant around my natural waist, and the length hits right around my ankles. There are no zippers or buttons - stretchy material (almost like dry-fit). Elastic cinched at waist where belt goes, looks very well seamed and remains classic looking. 98% Polyester and 2% Viscose. Super comfortable, and very breathable. Belt came with the pant and is a perfect length, plenty of room for a bow. I highly recommend this pant for anyone looking for an affordable paperbag pant that needs to move around a lot (very forgiving because of the stretch). I can wear this to the office, to the park, or to a nice dinner. Dress this up or down! Perfection!

SO HAPPY WITH THIS PURCHASE!!❤ I'm 5'5", 118 lbs. Normally, I would go with an XS, but upon reading the measurements on their sizing chart, I decided to go with a S (my waist is about 26"). True to size IF YOU PAY ATTENTION TO THE MEASUREMENTS. These pants are gorgeous! 😍 Great quality, material is light but not see-through (so great!). They don't restrict your movement at all, as they stretch so well. Very stretchy elastic waistband so it adjusts to your own body shape perfectly. They look exactly as they are pictured, so no surprises when purchasing. You can physically remove the belt/ribbon, and switch it for another belt/scarf/ribbon. Personally, I think it looks a little strange without one given that it has 3 very-visible wide belt loops. The pockets are deep enough for a phone, keys, or regular sized wallet. I measured them: they are about 4 1/2" wide, the length from the waistband to the bottom of the pocket is 8", and from the bottom point of the diagonal opening to the bottom of the pocket, you have about 3" of full covered security (nothing should fall out). Overall, I'm very happy with this purchase, and might even buy me some more in different colors...

Love love love I Love these pants they are so comfortable! The look so cute and professional. I am petite and it’s hard for me to pull of the paper bag pants. I feel these look great on my body type. I’m 5”2 and 120lbs athletic body type. My waist is 28 inches and my hips are 38 inches I really loved these. I actually first ordered in black and had to order another one in coffee color. The pants for me sit around the belly button so they are high waisted for me. They are stretchy and firm so they hold shape nicely. I like the fit of them I could of probably sized down even but I feel like medium fits great for this style of pant. However if you wanted the pants be less paper bag-ish you could probably size down for a slimmer look. I think you don’t need to size down tho because even tho they have that paper bag style these ones don’t give the penguin effect like other paper bag pants do sometimes on petite girls and the elastic and tie make it slimming. I say if your on the fence get it!

Stretchy and comfy I'm 5'4" and normally about 152lbs, but I'm 16 weeks pregnant and currently 162lbs so I sized up from my normal size medium to a large. The large fits me great and I feel confident I'll be able to wear these pants as my belly keeps getting bigger (maybe not in the third trimester). They're very stretchy like a jersey type material but very light. I did expect the color to be a bit more brown than pink so mildly disappointed in that but it's fine. Perfect for me as I'm not quite strictly in maternity clothes yet but my normal clothes are getting uncomfortable. I'd definitely wear these not pregnant too of course.

Like the look, hate the quality! I went one size up and ordered large, relying on the reviews that were mentioning that these pants run small. Should’ve bought my normal size, which is medium (US 6) in every single brand! They are true to the size! Large was too big and baggy, especially in the crotch area. The waist was a bit loose too. I exchanged them for medium and they fit perfectly (I’m 5’8”, waist 28, hips 40). Got them in ginger color, which is more like camel in the reality. However, the quality isn’t so great. The material is not soft to the touch, not breathable. Wrinkles easily and can not be ironed since it’s polyester and basically made of plastic and it melts and sticks even on lowest heat. Only steamer works, but takes a looong time. They can be worn for a couple of hours, but I can’t imagine having them on the entire day. If you tend to get hot or live in a hot environment, note that this fabric doesn’t let the air through and traps the moisture. Hope this helps.

Sexy, stylish, comfy! I wanted pants exactly as pictured- comfy, stylish, sexy, stretchy, and light enough not to take up much space in my carry on luggage. These pants check ALL of those boxes. FIT: I am 5'3, 110 pounds, and I bought XS. These fit like a dream (see photos). Fit just above the ankle. Tight enough to be sexy and stylish but loose enough to look classy. QUALITY: I was extremely impressed with the quality of these pants upon receiving the package. Purchasing clothes on Amazon can be a bit of a crap shoot, sometimes they aren't great but these are MORE THAN GREAT! The material is thick and stretchy. The construction is quality. STYLE: These pants are very in, I totally fit in when I wore these to a nice dinner in Europe which was exactly what I bought them for. They styled really well with a slouchy top tucked into them. I chose the dusty blue color and the color is perfectly true to the listing photos

Covers a lot if you get a bigger size. Oh boy, the model must’ve been like a million feet tall, and oh-so-skinny. But anyway, I knew they wouldn’t look like that on me, so I posted pictures. I bought two pair, a S and a M. Im returning the S and posted pictures of me in the M. I’m 5’2.5” and 116lb of after-baby mush. Don’t get me wrong, I worked hard to eat right & lose the fat, but did very little to gain muscle. What does that have to do with these pants? Well, it hides my jiggly, squishy tummy. I can’t say as much about my backside, but they sort of cover the range where the cheeks meet the legs. I’ve got kind of wide hips for my stature, and chicken legs to make them look bigger. So the pants do a good job. They meet my ankles, and will go best with flats (on me). Also, you can totally see the shirt line on the backside of the pants because of the material, and I (for the first time ever) can see why women in the 80’s bought those godforsaken tops that buttoned at the crotch. Ugh, I think I might need to find one now, because I like the pants.

Petite Friendly! I love these! I work from home, but still want to dress as if I am going to work but in a much more casual way, and these are perfect! They are super comfortable and the quality is really good for an Amazon buy. I am 4’11” 105 LBS - I got an XS (coffee color) and these fit me perfect. They hit right above the ankle. They fit my legs perfect (not too tight, but they also don’t swallow me (petite problems). I highly recommend these! I am about to order these in a few more colors. :)

Seriously so comfortable and cute!! I’m 5’7”, 210lbs, a size 14 in jeans, with an apron belly and I ordered an XL. These are PERFECTION. I bought them for a job interview and I’m obsessed. They’re probably the comfiest pair of pants I now own and they look GREAT. Seriously, I’ll be ordering in more colors asap! (These are the dark green, and the color is super pretty)

Yes, you should get them!! These pants are soooo comfy and can easily be dressed up or down. There is an elastic band with a decorative belt. No buttons or zippers. I was hesitant to buy these based off my short frame with wider hips and thighs. They have a bit of stretch to the material so they form-fit in some areas (but not in a bad way) and are looser in other areas. For reference I am 5’2 and around 135-140 lbs. I would order a size up if I wanted a looser look but I am content with the fit of a small. Almost looks like a fancy Capri. I roll the pants up about 2 times at the hemline and wear with wedges or heels (short girl proboems) and they hit right above the ankle. Super cute, would recommend, and now I want them in every color!!!! Definitely get some, perfect for summer especially if you don’t like wearing shorts :)

Some Guys Opinion Update: After my wife has now worn both pairs, there is a sizing difference. The pink pants are noticeably bigger and baggier than the black. She stands by her feeling on the pants. And if you are willing to deal with the fit difference, you should have a positive experience with everything else. Ok... So, I bought 2 pairs separately because one color was set to deliver 3 days before the other. Then they packaged and shipped it together anyway, so I was ready to be upset. Instead... They both got here on time and my wife was able to wear them at our daughters college graduation. AND SHE LOVED THEM!!! She loves the way they look, feel and move with you while on. She is ready to buy in other colors. She is 5’11” and bought a Large if that helps anyone with sizing.

So I was actually pleasantly surprised that these pants fit. I’m 5’9” and I typically wear an 18/20 in jeans and I ordered these pants in a 3x. They’re very soft and comfortable! BEST WORK PANT LOVE THESE PANTS! I bought them a few months ago for a teaching interview. I ended up getting 4 more colors since I was offered a job at a new school. Not what was advertised The top of these pants are "paper bag" but the bottoms are no way cropped or straight leg. I am 5'7" and these are long on me. I am returning them unfortunately. Maybe only for plus sizes?

Dressy yet casual Bought these in Fuchsia to wear to my office job. Can be dressed up or down as needed. Material feels durable with a little stretch to it. The waistband in back expands or contracts for a more versatile fit. I am 5’9” and almost 150lbs and the medium is a good fit. obsessed! Ok, first off, for reference I am 5’2.5” 195lb and Igot an XL. If you want a looser fit, size up. These pants aren’t unbearably tight but they are fitted. I have hips and a bit of a butt and I had to hop to get the elastic waist over my humps BUT once i did baby these pants had the body bodying ok? Love love love these. The belt is detached which is great because as pictured, I changed mine to a stretchy belt that really helped the high rise of the pants cinch my waist in. The pants are a light material but aren’t sheet. Obviously not particularly expensive feeling but not itchy or irritating. I worked a lengthy shift on a retail job when i got them today and I was comfortable in them all night. I ended up snipping the ties at the ankle; didn’t like that feature. but overall i like them. I believe they’re worth it for the price.

AMAZING for the bigger booty girls with long torsos (: Alright ladies. If you have a larger bum/legs then these are for you. I have always wanted a pair of pants like these but the waist is always too large or the thighs/calves are too tight. However, these pants are literally perfect. I got a medium and I am 138lbs, 5’6”, pear shaped, and have a super long torso and short legs. Most high waisted pants hit me just below my belly button but these hit above! They make my torso look shorter and my legs look longer, really balancing my proportions out more. The stretch is perfect, super comfy but still has structure. And the most important part… I don’t feel uncomfortable having my booty show at my job. I work at an elementary school and often feel as though I need to cover my bum because most tighter pants really show it off lol but these are the perfect material that gives me shape but doesn’t put my whole body on display if that makes sense (they are perfectly work appropriate). Lastly, I didn’t love the bows on the ankles, since there’s already a bow on the belt, so I literally just cut them off and you wouldn’t be able to tell at all - especially from far away. Now they’re super cute cigarette style pants (: If you’re considering these pants, just try them!!! So worth it.

Perfect and classy and fit short curvy girls! So I really love these pants. Very flattering on hour glass shaped women. I got them for an interview and I think they’re going to be absolutely perfect. I’m 5’1 super curvy and probably 175-185 (it’s been a while since I’ve weighed myself lol thanks quarantine 2020) and I ordered an XL in “dark green”. The dark green looked a little more teal and I was hoping it was so took the chance and was super happy I did. I took a picture outside so you could see color better. Its not like Christmas tree green it’s more like handmaidens tale wife dark green (sorry for the weird reference but ¯\_(ツ)_/¯) When pulled to be high waisted as intended they fit my legs and end at my ankles with just a little extra at the bottom so good for us short girls!! I am wearing the “Bishop” shirt from Target (available as of Jan 2021 online) and it works well for this as a crop shirt with puffy sleeves. Love that these pants have their own little bow/sash/belt! Also these are very comfortable so if you needed to have something you could wear that could survive literally running to meetings these will hold up. Felt nice enough that I’m not worried about them tearing or ripping and are comfortable enough to wear going out for brunch or what have you. Will be ordering more colors!

Good fit, good quality I read various good reviews before ordering, and I'm glad I did. I really enjoy these pants. I am 5'7" and about 185 lbs, normally a size 12 pant, and I ordered an XL. They fit really well. Maybe a bit big in the waist, but I didn't want anything smaller to be tight on my thighs/behind, so I'm really happy with the sizing. The pictures don't really do justice, and I don't love this top with these pants as much (it's a looser fit, and I need something that tucks in better), but I am so pleased with these pants. I haven't washed them yet, so I don't know how they wash, but other reviews have said they wash good. If I had one complaint, it'd be that they're a touch long for me, but I just tied the bottom bow a little tighter so they don't fall down. The length would be perfect with sandals. I also LOVE the pockets. They are big enough to put my phone in (iphone 7) AND to put my hand in at the same time. Overall, the material lays/wears in a flattering way, and I love that. I couldn't be happier with these pants.

Easy Care, Resists Wrinkling, Super Comfy Super comfy. True to size. Good stretch. Resists wrinkling (in the photo I had been sitting in them for 6 hours, and as you can see, they don't wrinkle). I am 5'7", 153 lbs and my lower body is proportionately smaller than my upper body (I'm usually a size 6 in pants, but an 8 in tops). I ordered a size MEDIUM (Apricot in color) and they fit perfect. They look more fitted in the front, but there's unused stretch in the back, for comfort. I think these pants are very forgiving and super flattering (even for someone like me who is conscious of their belly). Slight flaw in workmanship on my belt, which was misstitched, but overall, probably the best piece of clothing I've purchased from Amazon.

Perfection and classy Soooo, to help everyone, I am a size 12/13. I buy size 12 skinny pull on jeans at Old Navy for size reference. I’m 5’4. I weight 183 pounds on the thick side. You have to put a tiny effort to get them up your hips. 41 in Belly (around belly button) 39 in waist 46 Buttock 42 thigh to thigh under butt 26 Left and right thigh

Yeah, go head and buy them sis…but my thicc girlies need to size up For reference, I’m 5’5, 180 lbs with a short torso, “shmedium” hips and nice back end. I always struggle to find the perfect pants because my thighs are gargantuan, so I played it safe and ordered a size up (XL) to give the pants a fighting chance to get up my legs. They fit *JUST* right. Not baggy, not clinging to the skin. Despite the waist area not being super stretchy, the pants were comfortable to move around in. If you’re worried about height, my legs are pretty long for my body, and the end of the pants came 2-3 inches above my ankle. A bootie or strappy heel compliments it well. I’ll be buying another pair soon.

Pear shaped? Then pass I guess I didn’t realize these are ankle pants, and boy are they ankle pants! With the waist o the pants pulled up to my true waist (now they are supposed to fit) they fall at about 4 inches above my ankle. If you pull them down so that they are not ankle pants, you have a strange “sagging” look and crotch line (second pic). I am 5’4” and bought a medium at first based on the pant measurements on this site. Those were too big, so I bought the small. Those fit right, but I’m not sure I like them. I’m a “pear” shape (more weight in my bottom half than my top half) and these pants REALLY add some weight to your hips and butt. It doesn’t look bad in pics, but makes me look very disproportioned in person. They are so comfy, but I’m not sure the bare ankles and chunky rear end are worth the comfort. Just not for me.

I purchased the G Gradual Women’s joggers pants with side zipper pockets stretch tappered athletic joggers in white size medium. They are not see through. First I bought these pants because they have zipper side thigh pockets for my iPhone. I prefer my phone on the side of my thigh when I run! I love the pockets. My actual body measurements are 37 hip, my natural waist is 27, the circumference around my belly button where the pants sit is 31 inches. When I buy shorts I base my purchase decision on the waist. I go for looser so i do not get muffin top. The waist on the medium was a true medium. I compared it to my Under Armor pants size medium. I am 5”1’ tall and 110 pounds. I have super skinny stork-like legs but you’d never know it in these pants. They do not cling or hug me. I wear size 4 in Lulu Lemon speed up shorts and size 4 in jeans. But I do size up to size 6 in Lulu lemon speed up shorts if I am running up stairs for training. Size 4 is fine for jogging. Why I returned these: I had to return these because the 28.5 inch on size medium which I ordered is too long because I am 5”1’ tall. So the pants pool at my ankles a bit. I now know I need a 26 inch inseem. If you are 5”5’ or taller these will fit you without pooling. Second, I tried these on in 70F degree temperature room and I sat down and watched TV for 10 minutes and I got overly hot. (And I am always cold). These joggers insulate and trap heat in. They would be good to wear outside when it’s below 55 degrees if you are going to run or jog in them. In my 70 degree house just sitting I was way, way too hot. (And I am always cold because I have a hole in my heart). Polyester keeps you warm. Depending on the weight. I wear Colorful Koala polyester blend leggings in the winter, fall and spring and they don’t show sweat. But in the summer I switch to leggings with nylon they breathe 100% better here in Texas. In my experience nylon pants breathe very well in high Temperatures in Texas. I wish G gradual had some joggers with pockets made of nylon for summer. So these G gradual are great for outdoor workouts in much cooler weather. But I couldn’t wear these casually indoors at 70 F degrees. Regarding sizing: According to the size chart provided based on my natural waist 27 inches and my hip 37 inches instead of buying a medium it says I should have purchased the XS. I hate the waist being too tight when I bloat so I probably would have sized up to a small so the waist would be bigger. . I bought these because they said they were stretchy. These are NOT very stretchy. I returned them because they were too long and too big and not stretchy enough for yoga and running up stairs or doing high knees. I tested them. They feel stiff. They were a polyester papery feel. I hope this helps. If your tall these will work for colder climates workouts.

They Work for a Petite (Although as Medium/High Rise pants) These are super comfortable joggers which appear to be made of medium weight swimsuit material. I got the camo ones because I've got a roll of fat around my lower belly that I wanted to hide, but these pants hide that imperfection without the help of the camo. (for reference I'm a chunky petite, about 5'2", 35 " waist, 41" bum with a short waist, and ordered the XL, which is my usual size on Amazon). PROS: 1. Comfortable 2. They can be worn as high or medium (or low?) rise. 3. Because they're cuffed at the ankle they're not too long for me 4. Two Deep zippered pockets. 5. A wide waist without a knit band is flattering. It also has a tie at the waist. 6. They claim to be breathable- I'd say they're about 50% breathable, which given their medium thickness seems pretty reasonable. Considerations: 1. When I wear them as medium rise there's a little extra material in the crotch area, but not enough that it is uncomfortable. 2. After wearing these awhile they got a little static cling, but not enough to be bothersome. Note that the material is similar to a medium weight swimsuit material. All in all I'm happy with this order.

Nice, warm, comfortable and stretchy I have worn and washed these pants a number of times before this review. I really like the cargo pockets for my phone - it keep the phone secure and flush with your thigh. The fabric is soft and stretchy and very comfortable. I am about 5'9 and these feel a little bit short for me, would have loved an extra inch of length. The pants fit extra snug around the thigh area, this might partially be due to the cargo pockets and the extra seams they introduce. The back pockets are functional and have nice placement. The waistband is high and doesn't dig in. Great pants for when you want to comfort of yoga pants, warmth of fleece, but still want them to look a bit more like slacks.

Comfy! **Update 04/2024** I repurchased these in a size XL in the color OLIVE GREEN, just to see if I liked them better in a bigger size. I often prefer to size up now, as due to a surgery I had years ago, my lower tummy has gotten sensitive to things pressing on it. While these are definitely looser in the waist than the L, I think they unfortunately look a little sloppy. They're baggy in the crotch and just overall don't look like they fit as well. I may have not minded that in black, but in this slightly lighter shade, it's pretty obvious. I'm a bit in the fence about if I'll repurchase the large or not, we'll see. I got these in the color BLACK in a size LARGE. For sizing reference, I'm usually a medium-large or a 8-12/29-31 in bottoms and jeans. If it helps, I wear a size 6 or 8 in Lulu leggings. I'm 5'3" tall with a short torso, and my measurements are about 44"-33"-44" (bust-waist-hip) right now, give or take. I found these to be mostly TTS, though I'd recommend potentially sizing up if you have larger hips/butt. I still had plenty of room but would potentially size up to an XL if I wanted them baggier. The material is super soft and stretchy, more brushed than athletic, though it has a hint of slickness to it. They are super comfortable. The style is interesting, as it looks like it would be a jogger but it's cropped instead. I personally hate joggers, so I thought these were a nice change. I have thicker thighs and calves from years of soccer and these were super forgiving in those areas. I'm a little on the shorter side and found the length was a bit longer than I'd prefer, but I can just roll them up twice and they're perfect, or get them hemmed. In my picture, my left leg has the hem rolled and the right leg does not, so you can see the difference. They hit the top of my foot unrolled. I really like these because they're good for running around, working out, or even for casual workwear. I do wish they didn't have the logo on the leg and instead had them either on the back of the waistband, and/or made it tonal so it wasn't obvious. Overall, I'd definitely recommend these. I don't know if I'll grab any other colors, as I don't wear this type of thing often, but I still like them.

I found these to be mostly TTS, though I'd recommend potentially sizing up if you have larger hips/butt. I still had plenty of room but would potentially size up to an XL if I wanted them baggier. The material is super soft and stretchy, more brushed than athletic, though it has a hint of slickness to it. They are super comfortable. The style is interesting, as it looks like it would be a jogger but it's cropped instead. I personally hate joggers, so I thought these were a nice change. I have thicker thighs and calves from years of soccer and these were super forgiving in those areas. I'm a little on the shorter side and found the length was a bit longer than I'd prefer, but I can just roll them up twice and they're perfect, or get them hemmed. In my picture, my left leg has the hem rolled and the right leg does not, so you can see the difference. They hit the top of my foot unrolled. I really like these because they're good for running around, working out, or even for casual workwear. I do wish they didn't have the logo on the leg and instead had them either on the back of the waistband, and/or made it tonal so it wasn't obvious. Overall, I'd definitely recommend these. I don't know if I'll grab any other colors, as I don't wear this type of thing often, but I still like them.

Comfy! **Update 04/2024** I repurchased these in a size XL in the color OLIVE GREEN, just to see if I liked them better in a bigger size. I often prefer to size up now, as due to a surgery I had years ago, my lower tummy has gotten sensitive to things pressing on it. While these are definitely looser in the waist than the L, I think they unfortunately look a little sloppy. They're baggy in the crotch and just overall don't look like they fit as well. I may have not minded that in black, but in this slightly lighter shade, it's pretty obvious. I'm a bit in the fence about if I'll repurchase the large or not, we'll see. Great fit and comfortable with a small adjustment I bought two of these in two different colors and sizes and I love them both. Green is Medium size and black is Small size. For a reference, I'm 5.8 and 155 pounds. One thing I didn't like was the elastic waist (an enemy to apple shapes) it's super tight and constricting. However I ended up removing the inner elastic from the waist and now they fit perfect. They sit flat on my waist and the draw string allows for a natural waist adjustment. The waist may not be an issue for smaller waisted women, but for apple shapes the elastic waist may be a bit problematic in terms of creating a muffin top with the way it is. However if you're handy with the sewing machine, it's an easy and quick fix. Otherwise, I like the fabric, it's smooth, cool, it doesn't wrinkle, dries super fast, and no changes after multiple wears and washes. The side pockets are also great, they are functional and again create a nice hip line for someone with narrow hips. Definitely reccomend this one.

Great so far! I was a little wary about ordering these pants since I didn't see any reviews of any sort on the page. It was a bit of a hail mary ordering these, but I have to say I'm not disappointed! I never write reviews, but I wanted to write one in case anyone was looking at the pants feeling iffy about buying them since there's no reviews. I've been wearing them around all day today since I got them, and they are extremely comfortable. I think that they fit really well and are true to size (I'm about 5"4 and 128 lbs). The waistband is adjustable, so you can tighten them as much as needed. The fabric feels really nice and it doesn't feel cheap, in my opinion (and the pockets are deep enough to hold my phone!). The color is spot on as well. The pants come down to right above my ankle, but, once again, since the waistband is adjustable, I think you should be okay if you want to have the pants sit higher/lower on your hips. I really hope everyone has as good of an experience like I did. I definitely recommend these pants as of right now! I'll be buying more in different colors if I stay impressed with the pants. I'll be sure to update if I notice any changes in the quality as I wear them more.

Nice & thick, but not great about having to cut! I love the comfort and the pockets and the thickness! Very nice pants overall. But, as you can see in the front pic, very long. Even with long legs! So, I will have to cut these if I keep them, and not being a seamstress, that makes me a bit nervous. Otherwise, great pants! I just haven’t quite decided about whether I want to have the cut them to keep them yet.About me. I am 5’6, used to be 5,9” (still have the leg length), and weigh about 125. My measurements are 40”-28”-36”, and 36DD. I usually wear a small or size 4 in bottoms, and a small to medium in tops depending totally upon the cut and stretch. Overall, I’m a fairly thin, not in great shape, 63 year old woman. So, probably like a lot of people.I have worn the Sketchers Go Walk pants for years, and have many pairs. I love them, except for their unique ability to attract cat hair!! I happen to have tried a couple of pairs in a different brand of 4 pocket yoga type pants last year. They were made by a company called FirstWay. They were quite similar to the Sketchers pants, except they did NOT attract the cat hair so bad. So, I recently searched for them on Amazon, and couldn’t find them. So, I’ve tried 3 other brands and this is what I found.One of the other two brands I tried, Gradual was also nice and thick. The third brand, Nuveti were both definitely thinner than these! I will post reviews of both there.

Great color, fabric feels amazing For reference, I am 5ft 1in, 112lbs, 34 D/E chest, 27 waist, 37 hip. I ordered the small in plum. I would call the fit accurate to slightly generous. It's great-feeling fabric that is soft on your skin, not itchy, nothing that feels binding or tight. The wide waist and lines of the hip. and backside are flattering. The style is relaxed, classy athleisure chic - great for going for gourmet ice cream or getting on the plane. Concerns I have - The color is lovely but nothing like the advert which looked like an elderberry color, this is more aubergine/red plum. - They were very wrinkled on arrival, so they may need to dry flat, which is annoying Even with these concerns I still think they are a good pant and a good value. Very good looking and comfortable.

Roomy in the legs I ordered navy blue and went up one size for these Gradual Capri Pants to see how they would fit. I'm normally XL but ordered 2XL and they are roomy and comfortable. I wanted roomy in the waist and they deliver. I could have ordered my usual size for a more fitted look. The waist band is wide so it rests on your waist. It feels really good. The elastic in the waist is soft and moves along with your body movements with complete ease. I can't stand tight restricting waist bands. Btw, I love the high waist band. At 65 yrs I don't want to struggle with low, riding down , plumber's butt causing waist bands. Legs are cut full. These are not form fitting capris but they don't look baggy. Side pockets are ample. They sit flat against the capris and a bit towards the back of the garment. They arrived not directly on the side, at least they aren't for me. I'm 5'4" and these are true capris, not too long, not too short. Fabric stretches very nicely in all directions.

Wide-legged corduroy barrel pants are super comfortable The fabric weight of these pants will take you from late fall into winter. Made of 100% polyester, the material is an extra soft corduroy. I like that the pockets are on the front rather than built into the side seams. The pants feature a modern, high-waisted, wide-leg cut. There is an elastic waistband with belt loops and a zippered front. With an inseam of 28 inches, they may require hemming if you are under 5’ 4”. These are the pants you can run errands in during chilly weather. They look good with a casual sweater and loafers or ballet flats. They can be machine-washed and tumbled dry.

Great pants, but too long I like these pants, but I wish they were about an inch shorter. The material is good, mostly cotton, it is thick and durable, but breathable, soft and feels great on the skin. The elastic waist fits comfortably and is neither too tight nor too loose. Pulling the pants on and taking off is easy. The brown color is beautiful, and matches all sorts of tops. The legs are wide and almost baggy, but my main concern is the length. I am 5’5” tall, and when I wear these pants they reach the floor. When I walk down the stairs, the pants, being so long and wide, tend to get under my feet. A couple times I stepped on them and almost fell down.

Not the nice stuff These are the kind that feel like 1980’s velour outfits, at least to me anyway. I have ordered several velvet type clothing and there silky soft and then theres this. This picks up fuzz like no other. My others it slides right off on these its like a magnet for fuzz. They are also very shiny. They are so high rise they went up to my ribs. The waist part was quite tight even for a large. A size US size 12/14 should have been larger in my opinion. Be sure to measure your inseam. 32 inch inseam is not for shorter legs that these days are usually petite with jean sizes. If you are less than a 32 inch inseam then probably will need heels. Id only recommend these to people who like their pants super high and like the shinier glitter style velvet. This isnt the buttery soft kind. It will work with matching tops of similar material though. They are long so measure yourself if you dont already know your size. One upside they are stretchy just the waistband is a tighter elastic. I took stars off for how tight they are in relation to their posted measurements for US sizing. A 12/14 in US size should have been roomy for me not snug especially for stretchy material.

So unbelievably cute and fun It's kind of perfect that these exist in Barbie era. I could absolutely could not resist ordering these because they are just so cute! The only problem I'm having is trying to dress them down a little bit because I'm normally a very neutral colored dresser. And I'm having a hard time trying to figure out what to pair them with so that they're not too loud for me. I think that the fit is perfect, I'm 5'6 and they are a little bit long but I feel like with the flare of them it's kind of cute that way. They fit really comfortably and they have a wide elastic waistband which is my favorite because when there's that wide waistband it kind of helps keep everything secure and in place. They're made really well too, that kind of surprised me because I figured they'd be a really thin and flimsy type of material but they're actually thicker than I thought that they would be and then there's even a piping that comes down the front of each leg which makes them look structured and that takes them from being a little bit too little girl-like to being a little bit more adult-like. I have absolutely no issues or complaints about these at all and I wish I had gotten several more colors. I went with the pink ones that are I think called Dusty rose and the color is exactly what is pictured online. I'm about 185 lbs and got the large and they fit like a glove!

These are some really great pants! I have been ordering most of my pants on Amazon in a size Medium lately, but I took a chance on the small this time, and it was a great fit for my 27" waist, 37" hips frame. They skim my shape without being clingy or showing too much. The waist is sufficiently high to cover my belly button, and the length is perfect for just skimming the ground when wearing a low heel (I'm 5'6 and my slippers in the photo have a 1.5" wedge). The velvet catches the light nicely, and I love the front seam, which gives them an element of style. All that, and they're stretchy, warm, and comfortable too! I really can't wait to wear these for some kind of festive holiday event.

Great fabric! These pants are made well, the fabric is very stretchy but thick enough to provide enough structure so they don't look like yoga pants. The high waist is the most comfortable that I've ever worn. These pants look good with my flats and my heels so they're very versatile ! I washed in cool water on the normal cycle and tumble-dried and they didn't shrink, pill or wrinkle. No iron needed. So they're very easy care. I ordered medium and I'm 5'6" 155 lbs and they fit great. They accommodate my bigger legs and the waist could be smaller but the pleats make them very flattering. One thing I noticed when wearing is the front creases when you sit, even for a few minutes, as you can see in the last picture. But it's a small price to pay for such comfortable pants. I'd definitely order again and recommend them!

Nice Quality, Well-Made, Stylish & Versatile! I'm so glad I took a chance on these capri pants! As a 5'4", 115-120lbs woman, the size small fits me perfectly. They're well-made with strong stitching and high elastic waist that adds a trendy touch. The relaxed fit is comfortable and the rolled hems with button tabs add a nice detail. The stretchy material is just right - not too thick or thin, and lightweight. Despite initially hesitating due to the relaxed fit, they look and feel great on me. I love the 6 utility pocket's which make them versatile for casual wear, walking, hiking, and more. Plus, they can easily be dressed up for evening wear with a nice top, heels, and belt. They're reasonably priced, and despite being 68, I feel good, comfy, and trendy in them. I believe they'd suit any woman regardless of age, size, or shape. Highly recommended!

They're well-made with strong stitching and high elastic waist that adds a trendy touch. The relaxed fit is comfortable and the rolled hems with button tabs add a nice detail. The stretchy material is just right - not too thick or thin, and lightweight. Despite initially hesitating due to the relaxed fit, they look and feel great on me. I love the 6 utility pocket's which make them versatile for casual wear, walking, hiking, and more. Plus, they can easily be dressed up for evening wear with a nice top, heels, and belt. They're reasonably priced, and despite being 68, I feel good, comfy, and trendy in them. I believe they'd suit any woman regardless of age, size, or shape. Highly recommended!

Ugliest Yet I tried these on and they were immediately voted them my worst and ugliest lounge pants yet and I am pretty forgiving when it comes to my loungewear. They are way too long and so baggy that no one could make them look good unless you are 5' 10" or above. They are soft, but despite how baggy they are in the legs, they still have a waistband that is too tight to be truly comfortable. Failing the comfort test means you cannot wear them for lounging and there is no instance when you would want to be seen in public wearing these. The materials seems a decent quality, but at $29.99 at the time of this review they do not seem that far removed from the sweatpants you buy at Wal-Mart. Save your money.

More fancy than sweats, more for young people, soft and comfy, a bit short. The sweat pants came right away, pretty well wrapped up in an envelope. I loved the color, I loved the pockets but.. they seemed like a style for teenagers.. really, wide, almost puffy, That and they only came down to my ankles. That may be a style, if you wear fancy shoes..Anyway, for an XL these are just about too big.. but I will be comfy wearing them around the house.AT $29.99 they seem pricey to me.. but if you want to be fancy, then you may think that they are worth it.Three stars because they seem to stylish to be regular sweat pants.

Never know what you're gonna get It’s hard to find the right fit because I’m 5’2”, 30” inseam. Inverted triangle shape maybe? with a fuller waist. Because of this, when I order online, I get different sizes so I don't have to return and reorder all the time, but.... It’s exhausting. Normally, I wear a 6 or 8/28 in high waist jeans and 4 or 27/28 in lower rises. S-M in leggings. Inseam depending on style 25”-32”. All orders below I ordered 32” inseam and none were that. I included pics of 2 black (can't see1 of them and hard to see difference in color) and one of the ice blue.I ordered:4 (S) Ice Blue Fit was oh k, but afraid it might stretch to be too big because fabric is stretchy.6 (M) Ice Blue Too big all overThe small is more like a size 6. The fabric was decent quality, the color is a very light blueI ordered (XS) Lapis longing blue The fit in the butt was ok but bordering on too tight and uncomfortably tight in the waist. The color is med. dark blue with feathering …fading.. bleached? in the butt and on the front thighs. The fabric felt cheap. Thin, kind of crunchy or stiff but stretchy.I ordered(S) Always Black too big! Bigger than the 6(M) the color was very black, the fabric different from all the others which I kind of expected, but ok and the quality was good for the price. I liked these and was happy I had ordered the XS assuming they would be the winner or so I thought….(XS) Always Black, when I tried these, the fit ok except that the fabric felt thinner and the color had a slightly washed look to them and then the length was shorter than all of them! UGH!!! So frustrating!All sizes were shorter than the stated 32”. They varied between 30 and 31. So bummed because I liked them for the most part. I could accept the lesser quality if I could find one that had the qualities of three put together fabric of the ice blue, the smaller size and color of the Always black and then the right length of 32"! LOLMy favorite fabric and fit were the ice blue. Always black my favorite color, so I will try one more time and order XS 32” inseam Ice Blue and Always black and pray that’s what I get. I’ll try to update.****UPDATE:Ended up order the “Always Black” only in XS/32”So disappointing and frustrating. What I got was extreme faded black looked like they had been washed and again the inseam was not 32”! The last 3 photos are the newest pair! I mean come on!😞

Coloring not as pictured I am 5'5" 145lbs usually size 8/10 and ordered a Medium. They are a little big, but I think a Small would be too small. Good fit lightweight denim When my husband and I sold everything we owned & moved to Mexico, I packed on almost 30 lbs. We overindulged in all the delicious food here, and stayed in Airbnbs for months with no access to a gym. As a result, none of my clothes fit anymore, so I needed something I could wear that was at least as flattering as possible given the current state of my body. Since my size 10 jeans were too tight, I decided to get a 12 in these. In the photos, I weighed about 180 lbs, 37 waist , 42.5 hips and they were the tiniest bit snug, but the spandex in the denim allowed me to confirm to my body instead of giving me muffin top. Since I'm 5'7" with long legs, I ordered the 32 inch inseam. The length is perfect for a low wedge heel on me. I love that these jeans are lightweight with some stretch. So much better than the stiff denim from the old days (I was born in the 70s, raised in the 80s). The denim back then was thick and so stiff if you gained an ounce jeans weren't wearable anymore, and even if you could squeeze into them, they dug into your skin and left indentions and red marks. This more modern, stretch denim is so much more comfortable to wear, and the lighter weight fabric is more bearable in hot climates like Mexico.

Keeping but there are things you should know I read all the reviews and went into this knowing that these are not high quality. What gave me hope was the curvy girls I saw that loved them. For reference I'm 5'5", 36/27/40 measurements, 138lb. The good: I don't have a gap in the back. (this never happens!) The length is perfect. (also never happens) They are a little stiff but mostly comfortable. My thighs and butt have room to breathe! The bad: The distressed look on these is so tacky. I'll try to mute it by using dye. The seams are awful and could need reinforcement in stress areas. Look at the belt loop placement. Couldn't be bothered to make them symmetrical. The hardware is cheap looking and you'll need to rub a wax candle on the zipper to prevent sticking. Overall if you're athletic build/curvy these are so worth it for the cut alone. I very rarely find pants that fit me. I want to cry. When these inevitably fall apart, I'm going to take them apart and sew a higher quality copy of them with fabric that has stretch. This is such a relief after years of skinny jeans and manufacturers that pretended we didn't have bones, muscle, and fat on our bodies. With those very human things, you can also have a small waist! I'm mostly happy and glad I bought them. Took two stars off because they won't last long and I shouldn't have to dye them and resew the belt loops right out of the box.

3 1/2 stars These jeans fall between 3 and 4 stars and I don't think I would buy again. The good: The fit is great. I am 5' 7" 135lbs and normally wear either a 4 or 6 in other brands. The small in these fit nicely. They are a little tighter around my waist than I'd like, but I do have a pretty big waist-to-hip ratio (menopause belly!) However, they are stretchy so are wearable. The length is perfect on me and they are wide in the leg without being super-wide and baggy like some brands I've tried. The bad: The denim is low quality and the fabric is stiff. I have washed and steam-ironed them to soften them up a bit but they are still pretty stiff. Also, on the pair I received, the distressing is uneven. I ordered the deep blue color and there were three parallel white lines that looked like scratch marks on one leg. Luckily, I have a deep blue felt pen so I lightly touched up the white lines and now they aren't visible. I am sure I will wear these but they won't be my go-to jeans.

Cheap-ish Fit me well, nice color, soft, even if somewhat in the thin side. However the seam at the very top is scratchy and I don’t want to always have to wear a shirt tucked in. They’re also just long enough for me (I’m almost 5’9”), and I’m worried they’ll shrink in the wash. I’m afraid to will be going back as I’m at the point where I want things to work for me, or it’s not worth it.

Soft and comfy I bought these in blue and love them. Very stretchy and comfy. I’m 5’7 and 155lbs and purchased a medium. There is still a lot of room if my body decides to grow, but they did not stretch out after a few wears without washing. I am disappointed that the second pair I ordered in the Pecan had white lines on the top. I think to make them look worn but it doesn’t look natural. In the ad photo the lines are not noticeable. I will be returning. Would love them if they didn’t have the lines. Very pretty color.

Size chart true for US sizing. Super soft and cute! For comparison: I am 5’5” and I am usually 120 lbs but post holiday I’m rocking 125-130 lbs. I normally wear US size 4 or 27 in pants and size medium bikini bottoms. I have wide hips, some junk in the trunk, and shorter legs. Product: With the extra winter weight hanging on tight, mostly due to my love of chocolate and avoidance of the outdoors when the temperature is under 60, I decided to order medium Grapent jeans. I did not want the pants to be too tight and hinder my couch potato winter lifestyle. That was a mistake. The provided size chart recommended a size small but I have trust issues when it comes to size charts so I went up a size. Not only are the jeans plenty big in the waist, maybe for 8/10/12, but there is quite a bit of stretch in the material. Also, the fabric lost a bit of shape after a day of running errands. I fully regret not getting the recommended small because the material is very soft and stretchy so I think I would love them if they fit better. That’s my bad! The fabric is not a true denim but rather a medium weight cotton/poly blend. There are functional hip pockets as well as back pockets. I especially like the thigh rip because it is a different style than my other ripped jeans. As for length, I had to roll the cuffs to wear with sneakers. I’m normally a 29” inseam and these jeans were probably 30-31”. These waist just qualifies as “high” landing just past my belly button. The rear area was the only fitted part with the legs being looser. Recommendation: These mom jeans are stylish and super comfortable. I recommend going by the size chart in this case and rest easy knowing the fabric has plenty of stretch. The only negative thing I found was the tag instructs to hand wash while the product page advertises machine washable. I am going to machine wash but hang to dry and hope for the best. I really like these jeans and would recommend to a friend!

I'm 6 ft tall, 139 lbs and I'm wearing Medium. I probably could have sized down to a Small because the waist is fairly loose. They're a nice shade of grey -- and grey is on-trend for FW24/25, as is the looser, medium-wide straight leg. I'm happy to have these in my rotation. They're nice enough to go from day to evening if you're wearing a top and shoes that can make that transition; these can easily carry a sparkly top and dressy shoes.

Casual capris with beach-y fabric; a little prone to wedgies I got these capris pants so that I'd have some casual summer capris in a fun color. SIZE/FIT: I got an XXL in the olive green, and they fit as expected (the waist is a little snug on me, but I knew that going in, since I have a 42" waist - but they're pretty comfortable and stretchy regardless). I'm 5"6', about 220 lbs. The capris pant stops about 5-6 inches above my ankle. APPEARANCE: I love the color of the fabric; it's a lovely, light, slightly muted green. I like how the capris look from the front and sides. On the backside, however, the fabric kept getting caught up on my behind, giving my a slight wedgie appearance. For that reason, I took off a star. FEATURES: These pants have nice sized, open pockets, big enough for a phone or wallet. I like the wide, elastic waist. FUNCTION/FABRIC: The fabric of these pants is light and kind of crinkled; they would be great for the beach or on a hot summer day. Overall, I'm very happy with these pants, aside from the wedgie issue.

Make sure you are wearing the right underwear! These are soft and comfortable. However, with the high waist and smooth material good fitting underwear is ESSENTIAL, also make sure your shirt is tucked in perfectly, or wear one that needs no tucking. These have good stretch, I wear an 8 curvy L cut in jeans, these are a medium they fit my waist and stretch to accommodate my large hips and my booty. I can totally see myself enjoying these in the next few months here in Florida as it will be nice and warm, like today it's January and 80. These are lightweight and don't have that slightly stiff feeling of a pure linen pair of pants, instead they almost have a silky tendency! Now, the only drawback for me as a busy mom, is not being able to throw these into the dryer with everything else. I did wash these and then draped them across the rack to dry before trying them on again for pictures, I didn't notice any shrinkage in regular washing! I think I might need another pair for variety's sake, instead of my beloved leggings. I'm 5'10" about 160, long torso, long thighs compared to calves, for reference 4 stars because I'm being persnickity about not being able to toss in the dryer on medium with everything else.

Easy Breezy If you’re a short girl like me and always looking at cropped pants wondering if they’ll be good “regular pants” on you, here you go: I’m 5’ tall and these are full length on me, just right for flat sandals in the summer. This pic is right out of the package; I’m not worried about the wrinkles, though — it’s kind of a given for this style of pants that you’ll have to iron or steam them before wearing. They’re a Medium and I normally wear a 6-8 pant, so I’d say true to size and the perfect throw-em-on thing for basically any occasion in the summer. I could get away with these at my office or going to dinner, running errands, whatever. They’re light and airy and the green is a great neutral. Also, yay for pockets!

REALLY disappointed. I was super excited to get these, especially because of all the raving views. Not one single review mentioned anything about the pockets being messed up. One pocket is normal. The other one I can fit THREE fingers in, not even past my fingers. I love the length as they fit like regular pants on me (I’m only 5’). I sized up to an XL because I wanted them to fit lose. For the price, I was NOT expecting to have messed up pockets.

oversized and soft This review is for the GRAPENT Wide leg palazzo pants, xx-large, dark blue. I usually wear an XXL in pull on pants, 17/18 in jeans, and XL (XXL is usually a little too big) in leggings. Approximate measurements are 41” waist and 51” hips, 5’6” and a 30 inch inseam reaches just to the floor on me. These pants are a delight to wear. They are unbelievably soft, thin, stretchy, and flowy. The style is meant to be oversized so definitely don’t size up even if you want a loose fit. The size chart was based on body measurement and not the dimensions of the pants. It says that XX-Large, US size 20 - US 22, waist 36.5 - 38”, hips 47 - 48.5”. My waist and hips are bigger than that and the pants still are a flowy fit on me. The waist has a few inches of smocking and is very stretchy. The fabric has a good horizontal stretch to it. These pants drag on the ground for me. I wouldn’t expect this with a 29” inseam but I measured and they are indeed 29 (maybe 29.25) inches. However even with the waist an inch or two above my belly button (which is perfect for me) the crotch sits a little low. It looks and feels good but that’s why the leg length is too long for me. They look like the could be a straight wide leg but are actually about 16” at the thigh and 20” at the hem. The pockets are huge and stretchy and don’t lay completely flat across the front but rather droop out a little at the top. As I mentioned, these pants are delightfully comfortable. They are a little too oversized and long for me to want to wear as everyday “real” pants. Instead I’ll wear them primarily as loungewear that I’m OK with wearing out of the house for something like running an errand.

LOVE ❤️ I love these jeans, so much I ordered in more colors. They are lightweight, have just the right amount of stretch, comfortable and the perfect length. I am 5'6" and weigh 155. These pictured are the washed black. I saw someone's review said they had too much acid, mine don't at all!

Comfortable, Roomy Jeans with Some Stretch I ordered these jeans in size Medium in the color Medium Blue. They have a recycled look to them. They are cut roomy in the thighs and legs, which I find comfortable. They are made of 76% Cotton/26% Polyester/2% Elastane so they have some give to them. After laundering them three times, they haven't noticeably shrunk. For reference, I'm a long-waisted/short-inseamed 5' 4", 124 lbs, 29" waist and 38" hips. The length of these on me is at the longest side of OK. I think I could have ordered these in a size small, as they are a little baggy and the waist slips down a bit if I don't wear a belt. They have a strange smell that hasn't completely washed out after 3 washings, but I suspect it will eventually. Overall, I like these comfy jeans and I think they look kind of boho stylish.

Comfortable Pants First off I would say that are the most comfortable stretch dress pants I have come across. They have cute little pockets in the front and elastic waistband. This product is true to size. I am 5'3. I weigh 165 lb and I have big hips and got a large but I could have got a medium.

Extremely Comfortable ☺️ I am 5’7, 155 pounds and I ordered a size Large in the color Black. I go back and forth typically in sizes medium and large in pants. Sometimes I am a size 6 but often I am a size 8. The large fits me in the waist extremely well. These pants are made out of 80% Nylon and 20% spandex so they are very stretchy and soft all over. One of the comfiest black dressier pants I now own! The waist is elastic and although it looks like it buttons up in the front, it is only there for visual purposes. These fit me tighter at the waist and hips and then are a straight leg with the slightest gradual flare going all the way down to give a tiny amount of wideness at the bottom. I wouldn’t consider these wide leg pants though. The only thing I wish I could change about these pants is the length. This is a normal problem for me though. The length is perfect if I wear a flat shoe but anything higher they will be too short. I like to wear a chunky block sandal or something with a heel when I wear black pants like this and unfortunately I will not be able to do that. Other than that, these are extremely comfy and I love them!

Comfortable, Flattering and Versatile I seriously want to live in these trousers! The material is so soft and reminds me of a really high-quality pair of yoga leggings and the fabric is the perfect thickness. They're polished enough to wear to the office, but they're also so comfortable that they're perfect for yoga or just lounging around the house. The fit of these pants was perfect for me. I ordered the Medium, and for reference, I'm 5'6", 150 lbs, and a size 8. They fit true to size, and the stretchy material makes them super easy to slip on and off. I love that I don't have to fuss with any zippers or buttons - the buttons on the front are just decorative. The high-waisted design is perfect, and the waistband has a gentle compression that sucks you in a bit without feeling tight or uncomfortable around the tummy. I can wash these pants on a gentle cycle, hang them up to dry, and they're ready to go in no time. Plus, they don't wrinkle. I'm definitely planning on ordering more of these pants in different colors. They're such a great find and so comfy!

Small reg vs small petite (both fit great) Very good product. I like the material and the waist band elastic is comfortable, and doesn't extend fully in the front so it doesn't get bunched up when pulling the ties. Based on reviews, I ended up getting the small and small petite to try. I am 5'3.75" and 130lbs, usually wear a size 5 juniors or 4/6 depending on the pants as I have a booty. I was concerned as based on the measurements I didn't think my booty would fit in the small (39" hips) but they did, easily! Plenty of room in both sizes. I had room behind the thighs as well, and only had to pull the drawstrings slightly, which is surprising for me with pants in general. I laid out the small reg and small petite and tried on both. The only discernable difference I found is in the length of the pant leg. Small reg measured at 31" inseam, and it was 2" longer than the petite. For me both fit well, and just depended on what "look" I would want- fully over the top of the tennis shoe with wrinkles (reg) or just laying on top (petite). For reference the one pictured is the petite and the shoes are standard asics sneakers (about 1" heel).

Perfect for skinny/ curve-less girls! FINALLY!! Scrub pants that fit me so PERFECTLY! For reference, I’m 100 lbs, 5’2”, short torso/ high waist - I wear XXS regular length. I have a very thin frame with NO curves/ thighs (sigh), so it’s tough finding scrubs that don’t look insanely baggy on me. I’ve even tried pants that have a little stretch in them and are even categorized as “slim fit,” but those only enhanced my LACK of curves (doh!). I know I might be speaking to a small percentage of the population, but I hope this review helps my fellow skinny-minnie, not-so-blessed-in-that-department gals. These pants are form-fitting and truly slim fit. My colleague pointed out to me that my pants look very nicely fitted on me. Yet it still has enough room to move around in, and it doesn’t hug my behind so tight to where it accentuates my flat behind. It’s so very comfortable around the waist, and the pockets were nicely designed as they are subtle, and they don’t make your pants look bulky or like there’s just too much going on (I prefer simple designs, but with functionality). The slits sit perfectly on top of my shoes. I don’t have a side view, but I do have this view from the top. These are truly the only scrub pants I will wear!

Great fit and SO comfy!! However... had to size down. I’ve always heard great things about Grey’s scrubs, so I figured I’d give them a shot. With that being said, I am beyond glad I did! These scrubs are SO SOFT. I love the side pocket with the zipper and the slits at the bottom of the pants. When I ordered these the first time, I ordered a Small Tall. The length fit perfect but they were baggier than my liking, so I did have to send them back for an XS Tall. For reference, I’m 5’6 and around 135 lbs. They fit my waist and legs great. The length could be just slightly longer for my liking (I have long legs and a short torso, so my pants sit up higher on my waist— and I also have slim legs so finding pants that fit my body type can be hard) but overall I’m very happy with my purchase and would recommend these pants! I’ll probably end up buying another pair.

I love the fit and material of these scrub pants. I am 5'4" and weigh 130lbs. The size small fits great and is the perfect length. If buying in black color, size down Feb 2023: I normally wear a medium… Medium petite was at least an inch too large… I was swimming in them… See pictures. Reordered a small petite And it fits perfectly… See pictures. Washed it on cold and tumbled dry on low for 20 minutes, then hang dried, no shrinkage. For reference, I am 5’3”, 123 pounds, a little belly fat, And hips at 40 inches. The waist really doesn’t matter because this is a lower rise pant. Love how lightweight it is, I am a massage therapist. I have also ordered this in steel blue, let’s hope the small petite fits in that as well. Luv all the pockets and it is a very soft material and dries quickly.Mar 2023: I did order the PANTS in steel blue as well and the small petite fits perfect. I also ordered the matching scrub top… I tried the small in the scrub TOP though it fit, I like things a little looser… It was fitted across the chest and in the arm holes a bit tighter than I prefer, and when raising my arms it raised up on my hips, as did the medium, but on the small I had to pull it back down. The medium Came back down on its own when I lowered my arms. I returned the small scrub top for the medium. I am 34C. I don’t like to feel constricted in any way and when massaging I am moving my arms a lot in all directions, so I do not have time to tug down my scrub top. So for me I could wear either the small or medium scrub top but I prefer more room so I chose medium to keep. I love How light and soft the material is. I wash on cold and tumble dry on low for 20 minutes then hang dry. No shrinkage. Very comfortable.

Stylish scrub pants Nice flattering scrub pants, these do not have as much stretch as the other greys anatomy scrubs I have, and they tend to run on the smaller side maybe just because of the material. But I still really like them and they are in no way uncomfortable in my opinion. I will say I am 5’1 &125lbs. I ordered a small petite. I like this BUT... So I'm 155lbs lbs and somewhat curvy, finding scrubs that fit the way I like is a challenge. If I get Medium the hip fits but the waist is way too large, size Small fits perfect in the waist in most brands but hip is a little tight. If you're a woman with my kind of shape then you'll get the drift here. I really like Gray's Anatomy and I've been trying to find a style that fits forever! but have had no such luck due to their sizing. Literally this is the closet one I found so far that actually doesn't fit like tights. I currently own several other brands in smalls and I have some give in the hips and overall fit is perfect for me. However, I find small pants in Gray's to be very tight as you can see in the pics. I'm wearing a small Gray's active top (tops are usually ok in whichever brand as I barely have any boobs so fitting in easy) Overall, I really like the pants as they seem to be really good quality so I'll keep and try to lose some inches from my hips so I have more give for comfort. I gave 3 stars because I believe that they can add just a few more inches to the hip sizing of their pants (I have tried numerous styles and all fit just as tight or even tighter compared to smalls in other brands).

You’ve got to have a nice butt Per the reviews I did a size larger than I normally do. I am 5’6 and roughly 270lbs, I usually wear a 3X but knew to get the 4X scrubs after reading the other reviews. Sizing has changed. They split after a few wears Long story short, I bought these scrub pants, size small, one year ago, and I have been wearing them for work. I realized I needed more pairs because I’m a first time mom and I didn’t always get to the laundry on time so I ordered two more pairs of the same size, my body has not changed, and I have noticed that these are drastically smaller, even when I hold them up to the original ones that I bought. Now, I never dry my scrubs, so there really is no difference in the original pair that I have bought in the past. Do yourselves a favor and size up, but also be careful when you go back to order more. Update: you really need to size up on these. I bent down this morning and the seam completely split. I’m 5’8 lean athletic built, I’m telling you the size has changed. Size up especially if you’ve previously bought from them in the past

High shallow pockets I really like these leggings except for the pockets. The pockets are shallow and they are higher on the hip than other leggings I’ve had. **But I think the reason the pocket feels too high is because it is so shallow. On my other leggings I think the pocket starts in that area but the phone goes down lower so it sits closer to the top of your thigh instead of right on your hip. The bulk of the phone squarely on my hip feels and looks weird. That’s why I returned them. I have an iPhone 12 pro, which is not the giant one, and you can see that it sticks out. (It may look in the photo like I don’t have the phone pushed down all the way but I have the case pushed down as far as it would go.) There’s really no excuse for the pockets to not be deeper. Other than that they are really nice leggings. I love the fit and the length is great for a tall person. I’m 5’9 /170 and large fits right. They just need to improve that pocket because I am unlikely to try other leggings by this brand.

Good product, not what I was expecting. I bought these expecting them to fit like leggings you wear to the gym with a bit of compression. There was no compression and the waist band was deceptive as it looks like a 5in. waistband, but in reality that’s simply a seam and there is only a 1 in. elastic band at the waist. The material felt great and all the seams were in tact with no fraying so I think if you were to look at these, just know that they do not fit like gym leggings rather the leggings you wear around the house. At the end of the day I returned mine, while they were nice, it just wasn’t what I was looking for. Height: 5’3” Weight: 150 Size Ordered: Medium

Postpartum body: Buttery soft, stretchy, tummy compression, not see through I'm currently 3 months postpartum and have been on the hunt for some capris leggings that fit all my curves while I regain my body. ⭐PROS⭐ Color & Fabric: they are true black and not see through. Fit: I'm 210 lbs and still losing baby weight. These hold things in place just enough without being uncomfortable. Despite Amazon's recommendation, I ordered a size down from the suggested size. It also is stretchy enough that they aren't tight around my high waist or calves. Feel: so soft! Sometimes it really does feel like you don't have anything on! Haha 🚨CONS🚨 The fabric isn't the most breathable so keep that in mind if you are wanting to use these for working out. The fabric collects lots of hair. The tag inside is not centered. So, you have to be thoughtful about putting them on to make sure they aren't on backwards.

GREAT for petite women & lovely fabric! Great to not have to pay 100 bucks for Athleta petite city joggers!!! Score for us shorter ladies!! These pants actually fall in the right spots on our legs and knees. They’re not just shorter in length 🙌🏻🙌🏻🙌🏻For reference, I’m 5’4” 117 pounds and these are the purple ones.I usually get an XS in joggers and leggings, but i needed a size Small in these. The highwaisted waistband is tiny. Though that’s not necessarily a problem area for me, I’m straight like a boy there. No curves. No tiny cinched waist. I probably could have used another 1/2”-1” there. But the fabric of the waistband is soft and comfy, so it’s not bothersome.NOTE: I have one leg that’s shorter than the other, so no the waistband isn’t sewn more curved on one side. It just looks that way b/c one hip sticks out more than the other.The fabric is lovely too. But be careful with your panty line. Though the fabric is not chonchy, it’s too thin to be worn without a layer between the pants and your cheeks in order to be proper. You just need that extra thin slip like layer. Can’t be cotton b/c it’ll cling. Target has some great silky options in their Auden line in the little drawers.

Weird cut I don’t understand the 5 star reviews. Either women are wearing these skin tight like leggings or quality control sucks. The fabric is a bit shiny but that was to be expected with type of material and the fact they are activewear. The ad doesn’t say cotton so don’t expect it. Those complaining about used. You do understand people try these on and send back if they don’t fit. It’s no different than you trying on clothes at the mall. I do disagree with them not attaching new tags though since the inside of the leggings have no branding label. Please wash anything you buy before wearing. Clothing is sprayed and shipped with chemicals to help preserve it and to keep bugs out. I’m writing this review assuming the product matches what I was sent since it was a return from someone else and I know why. 5’2”, 105 lbs, 24 waist, 34.5 hips. They are cut short through the crotch. The waist was perfect. The length was as expected because I’m short, but have longer legs. BUT the thighs are a hot mess. Look at the picture. There is too much fabric and it looks like you are smuggling something in your crotch/upper thigh area. It looks just as bad when your legs are together. The pair from Leggings Depot is a bit big in the waist but they hang better and no weird crotch for $13 less. I’m really disappointed because I wanted to like them since the waist fit so well.
