Best. Yoga. Pants. Ever!!! HOLY CRAP!! I bought these after seeing my friend wearing a similar pair that had pockets and watch her phone not fall out. But little did I know the heaven that would grace my butt when I put these on. Thick, COMFY, supportive material. Stretch for days! Hides all that post baby belly chub like a dream! And the best part! The pockets! The fact that they exist is awesome but I decided, for science, to see just how mom friendly these suckers are. They hold everything and securely! As you can see in the picture that’s a full size bottle of rose in my pocket and trust me when I tell you I could run from the police in these things and trust that my precious pink (I’m referring to the rose...) is just as safe and secure as my kids once were in their baby carriers. Probably more so since I was never going to win any awards for tying those baby wrap things on... and the pocket was not stretched or bagged out after I removed the bottle, the material snapped right back in place like it was never there. If you do one good thing for yourself today it’ll be to buy a pair of these. Also, don’t worry when the package arrives and you bought your usual size. I went by the TTS fit and when I opened them up I thought they looked 2 sizes too small. But my self loathing insisted I try them on anyway and I was pleasantly surprised they fit just right. Material is legit not see thru! Pockets sit lower so easier to stick your phone in there! UPDATE: I still stand by my original review but I have to say, if your legs are more on the short side, this length of leggings are pretty long and they get baggy. They don't look very stylish for that reason. Definitely worth the money! I'm always on the hunt for leggings that don't show my underwear when I'm doing squats. The material is thick and very well made. I wouldn't say the lower legs are as snug as what I'm used to but they aren't baggy either. I'm 5'5", 145 lbs., athletic build, and I bought a medium. Perfect sizing. The top section has a pretty wide elastic band. Totally fine but it does indent into the skin a bit. Not a deal breaker. I will definitely be getting more of these. They are extremely well made, fit well, comfortable, and the feel is soft. It's not a slick fabric or a buttery soft, but very perfectly right inbetween those two. I've tried, Athleta, Lululemon, Sketchers, Lorna Jane, and all brands that are sold at Costco, and these are my new go to! Definitely recommend! And you can't beat the cost!

The band cuts into my tummy. These are not truly "thick waistband." The waistband "pocket" is about 4in tall, but the actual elastic inside it is a standard 1in elastic, which, when these sit as high on the waist as they do, means that the elastic cuts into the softest part of your tummy (well above the hips), putting pressure on your organs - and all of it on a narrow, concentrated strip. **I'm even accustomed to wearing steel bone corsets a couple times a week for a few hours at a time, so it's not that I'm unfamiliar with cinching. Corsets distribute the pressure of the cinch across your entire torso; this thing concentrates all the pressure in one single 1in band. (I'm 5'8", 145lbs, 36-28-39, and ordered the medium, as always.) Ive had other wide waistband leggings that use the stitching and material to create the 4in tall waistband - no elastic. Also, they run a little short (I'm leggy with a 31" inseam), and on me they fit more like 7/8ths leggings. Besides that, they look great, but the fit was just off for me.

Type of FABRIC Relevant to FIT UPDATE: Top of thigh has begun to pill. Very small and low pills, but no longer smooth. It’s not the worse pilling, it’s tiny but I can feel it under my hand. *** There’s a fabric term that I don’t hear used much anymore: “hand.” Hand is the feeling of the fabric- sheen, weight, stiffness, stretch, texture, pile, etc. If you read description this manufacturer uses 4 different fabrics lumped into one product listing. It makes it d*#n near impossible to select the right product because reviewers are talking about different things. This fabric is lightweight, low sheen, low compression (read: stretchy). The pattern is nice looking (grey marble). I’d personally size down on the grey marble leggings. Like you, I read ratings because I want what I want and hate making returns. The leggings I ordered are light weight and STRETCHY. STRETCHY does not generally facilitate tummy control and there is no double panel in the front. I chose the fabric that I visually liked. It’s 88% polyester/12% spandex. I sound smart but I have no idea what the hand of that fiber mix feels like so I took a stab in the dark. I kinda lost. Constantly hiking them up and no tummy control. My tummy is not that big, I just want a little flatter front, and I like the feeling of additional core support. There are 3 other fabrics/fiber combos used which I think should be listed separately because if they are a different fabric, they are a different product. I hate it when vendors group products together. Shoppers have no idea what product is being described, because apples, oranges, bananas and lemons are all being called “fruit.” For reference, I’m 5’ 5, 139lbs, 30” waist, 40” hips. I’m high waisted (I did like that these leggings hit me right at my real waist) and I have muscular thighs and butt. When reviewing, talk about the fabric hand, and your measurements. It’s sooo ! They’ve given us a communication platform. We’ll have to figure it out by talking to each other. A note on Pocket Placement: The side pockets are placed too high on the side leg, except if you’re carrying a bottle of Champaign, apparently (???). But if you’re sitting and you need to reach your phone, it sucks. Pretty much have to stand up. NOTE: I think the higher the spandex percentage, the more compression you’ll get. I also associate spandex with a higher sheen (Think Jane Fonda 80’s Workout) which I did not want. But I’m not sure. What’s your experience? To GYM PEOPLE: Include a compression rating next to the fiber content descriptions. For leggings, I suspect this may be a defining factor for buyers. Fiber content is meaningless unless you’re, well, you.

Decent workout pants I ordered 2 pairs, charcoal and military green, size M (I am 5'7 and 165 lbs). Both pairs fit me well and are comfortable. However, I should have paid attention, and the 2 styles have very different fabric and feel. Military green are more stretchy due to higher spandex content, fabric is thinner and has a cooling effect, whereas the charcoal pair is less stretchy, thicker and warmer. Both will work for me depending on the weather, though. My only complaint is the waist rubber band feels a bit thin and maybe not the most comfortable I have seen in yoga pants, but I feel like it's OK quality for the price.

Comfort & No Budge for High Intensity I previously ordered a pair of these pants in a Large around Black Friday. They were running a deal, so I thought I would try them. I love that pair, but they are starting to feel a little loose, as well as my few other brands. (I will add this is my favorite brand as of now, so I keep watching for deals. I’m ballin’ on a budget over here.) I have recently started jogging/running, and my pants would work their way down, and the seams in the crotch area are uncomfortable during running. I decided to size down to a medium to allow for a tight fit. You can tell they are more snug in the pictures, but they are not too tight. I just got these in medium today and already took them for an outdoor run. I absolutely love them! I did not have to adjust one time during my run, and I felt no seams while running. I was able to focus on my workout, and not my wardrobe. 💕 Also, no unseemly wet spots. I appreciate all the pockets as well. There are two side pockets, big enough for me to slide my phone in, and also a pocket on the inside of the high waisted back panel. Pictures were taken after 1.5 mile run. I will definitely be ordering more! For reference: Weight 170-175lbs Height: 5’5”

Mostly good These are pretty true to size but i have 2 complaints about them. The waist band is to tight compared to how loose the legs are. I have huge legs, from my thighs to my calves, everything is well above average. My waist is below average compared to my hips and thighs. The band around my waist is slightly tighter than I'd expect for being a 2x but the legs are much bigger. You can see both in my pictures. Its not a big enough problem to return them but it's still something to think about when purchasing a pair. My only other issue is that they're not 100% squat proof. My hips/butt are a bit bigger than you'd expect for my waist so that could be the issue though. They only became see through in a deep squat though. Regular bending wasnt an issue. The quality is fantastic, and i love the side pockets! My measurements are as follows, waist - 38 inches, hips - 55 inches, height - 5 feet 2 inches.

Very comfortable and stretchy w/ 3 pockets!! Material is really good and thicker than you would expect. Still breathable though! I am 5'6 with thicker legs and they stretched perfectly to fit! These have like an extra waistband for sucking in at the top compared to the usual waistband. Pockets on both sides AND inside the back part. Did some stretches in it and they looked squat proof w/ no sliding down. I'm not used to the upper butt seam but it doesn't make it uncomfortable. I also typically don't like leggings that have a front seam but this one doesn't seem to ride up in the front like usual. I haven't washed them yet so we will see how they are in the long run. Overall, they seem perfect!

I purchased XL and think it was probably the best choice for my size.

Comfy quality leggings great for tall friends-capri & full length review Edit- they have been washed multiple time and have the same smoothness and elasticity. I do have to do a weird squat dance when I first put them on just to get them in the right place! They hold phone and keys and whatever I need with NO RISK of falling down. They don't roll when changing positions and moves. I love them so much I now own multiple pairs in different colors. These are the only things I wear to work out in now. Have not washed them yet.. these are initial observations. Full length leggings AND capri review below Pros: Soft and warm (these are thicker than typical work out leggings I've had). Fabric is a brushed matte finish feel. Thick elastic band is wider than my hand and has good compression. The capri feels like more compression throughout, but still good in full length. The length is actually long enough for me. They did not roll when I did squats. They successfully held my phone without pulling down leggings. Pockets on both sides and hold my hand to wrist. Cons: for full length: too much fabric behind knee Slightly loose at ankle (could fit 2 fingers under with no tension) -->this is not an issue with the capris, they have a snug fit everywhere. 5'11 239lbs size 14 pants (apple body shape) bought XXL

Nice compression and high waist, but runs small. 3 month postpartum review I like the thickness and compression of these but they definitely run small. High waisted is accurate. I got a large based on recommendations but definitely could have sized up. They still fit comfortably and I will keep them, in hopes of slimming down. Granted I am right at 3 months postpartum, I'm hoping my tummy will continue to tighten and the fit will be better. 5'3" and 169lbs today. 3 months pp. Usually size 12, M/L tops, leaning more toward large in postpartum.

Honest review These are the best workout leggings I’ve come across so far, and I’ve tried several. Love the pockets. They are not super soft but they are thick and comfortable. I am 5’6” and 185#. I machine wash on cold and hang to dry. They are a perfect fit, great length—they don’t bunch up at the knee. They are a higher rise and stay put! I can’t stress the importance of this enough! When running on the treadmill my leggings must stay up or it becomes uncomfortable. These stay put throughout my run. I’ve also worn these on a non workout day and they stay up all day—no rolling down. They do show sweat. They’ll show your panty line so don’t wear them underneath if that bothers you. (Not see though other than the panty line due to the fact that these are meant to be tight and hug your curves). Photo included after my run to show they aren’t sweat proof. It doesn’t bother me at all but wanted to include that info if it’s a deal breaker for you. I have green and navy so far and ready to purchase more colors!

Beware of sizing I’m 5’7, 140lbs. 28” waist. 38” hips and mediums are not tight enough to stay up during workouts. See picture where waist does not fit snuggly. I was worried a small would be too tight across hips/not squat proof. Not sure these are my leggings any more 😭 if you have a larger waist, or smaller difference between waist and hips these may fit well. Otherwise, I’d keep looking.

The S was big for me My usuals size : XS,0,1 Measurements: 5’4. 122 lb According to my measurements on the Amazon profile the recommended size for me was s, even though usually xs fits me better. So I went with the algorithm suggestion. I believed it runs small, but if you are in my range I recommend you to get an XS. It is stretchy and the S feels big on me and doesn’t fit me well. It was a longer wait time for me, plus I did not like the elastic at the top of the waist, if it snaps than I can no longer wear them. Overall I would not replace them with smaller ones, but would just return them. The material was ok, they are not transparent.

Each legging varies!! I discovered these leggings back in 2019 & have written raving reviews as I thought they were the most amazing workout pant I’ve ever worn. I am beginning to replace older pairs as I work out a lot and they are experiencing some wear & tear. I am extremely disappointed in the newer ones that I have ordered. The fabric varies from legging to legging, the sizing seems to vary as well & today on my most recent purchase I noticed neither pair matched with the website description of 88% polyester & 12% spandex. I am 160 pounds, 5’8” & the medium was a perfect fit. These leggings were always reliable, good quality and GREAT for running, HIIT workouts, lifting & lounging. I also notice the crop had gotten shorter & I never used to notice how much my ankle was exposed (especially in the winter months) . These are still my favorite leggings, but it’s been extremely annoying buying the same size product in different colors only to be surprised with how each one fits. Just an FYI.

Beware of sizing I ordered a Medium which is my usual size. I’m 5’5” 145 lbs. they fit well just a little tight around the stomach but I figured they would stretch over time. Everyone should have the staple black pair of leggings in their collection and these would make a great addition. I got a Large in them and they fit perfect so I would say true to size, if you're in between you could go either way as they do have a lot of stretch to them, it would just depend on how tight you want them, for these type of leggings I use more for comfort that actually working out so I don't like them to be too tight so a Large was a perfect fit for me.Fabric is more of a performance material - not the buttery soft fabric like the Lulu aligns or anything - so it is smooth on the outside - I will say they are still very soft and comfortable for a more performance material. Length will definitely be full length for most people - if you're on the taller size then these will become more of a just above the ankle length - I am 5'4" and personally I am fine with either a fuller length legging or a slightly shorter length so these get A++ for the length they have and being able to accommodate lots of people.The leggings in general have minimum seams to them - no side pockets on this set - it has a slightly tapered seam in the back (more on that later) but overall if you like minimal seams then you will like these for sure. I do get rolling down in the stomach area with these which isn't uncommon in leggings as I carry a lot of my weight in the midsection so something to consider - Overall I am very impressed by the comfort and quality of these leggings, if it wasn't for two things I would have rated these a 4/4.5 stars.The reason I gave them only 3 stars is for two reasons: The biggest reason for me is the back seam detailing - it is very low - at least on me and the reason this annoys me is because they feel like they are falling down all the time when they aren't because the seam hits me below my hips so I constantly want to pull them up and drives me a little bananas. The 2nd reason is that due to the type of material stuff clings to them - as you can see in the last picture, hair, dust etc. really cling to them which isn't the end of the world but it is a bit annoying when you have to constantly "clean" them off.

In the product description the title specifically said leggings with pockets and so that’s what I expected to get.. The leggings that arrived to look like the leggings in the photo. There are no pockets, which makes them not practical for my purposes, since I always need to have a place to keep my phone. These leggings are a polyester spandex blend, and the fabric is relatively thick so there is no risk they will be transparent. The level of stretch is moderate. I personally prefer a more stretchy leggings. The sizing of these leggings is generous. I ordered a medium and they fit comfortably, but they are even loose on the lower leg. I don’t mind the looseness, and I prefer a legging that you don’t have to struggle to take on and off. That length is also generous. I’m about 5‘6“., and these would definitely be fine for a taller person, both in the rise of the waist, and the length of the leg. These leggings are well manufactured. The seams are secure and straight. My rating of these leggings is largely based on the fact that they’re not especially stretchy, but most importantly, there is no pocket despite the description saying so. It’s very disappointing, and for those of us who need a place to carry a things the absence of a pocket makes them very impractical.

I’m no stranger to black yoga pants (read - my go-to everyday wear pants). I own several different kinds which I rotate regularly. I can say that these leggings are top notch! They are super soft on the outside, and a more slippery material on the inside which makes them easier to get on/off. They are actually high rise I don’t feel the need to tug on them constantly to cover my belly button. They are stretchy and comfortable. They also have a little inner pocket at the front waist for keys. My only disappointment is there being no side pocket for my phone. That would have made them perfect! They are light so good for warm to moderately cool weather - good for this in-between season time. For reference I am 30” waist and 40” hips and a Large fits me. 37$ is a little bit more than I’d want to pay - you can get similar tights at Winners for 30$.

Really soft; well made; fit very well. The fabric is soft and light while also being just heavy enough to drape well. The fabric is also surprisingly warm or cool as circumstances require. Cool when walking around a warm house, but warm and cozy late in the evening when my body temp cools down and I get into bed chilly. The size was very good. I am 5'7", 155 lbs, and a Large fits really well for indoor relaxing and sleeping. I also bought the same pants without cuffs. I really like that they are long enough to fit me. I have a 30-inch inseam and pants often are not long enough to fit me well. I am about 20 lbs over my normal weight this year (pandemic eating and an anti-depressant for eight months), and I was hoping a Large would help me be comfy this winter while getting my weight back to where it needs to be. Since I gained weight in my hips, I wanted lounge pants that would fit my waist comfortably while still giving my hips and leg length enough room. These did the trick. I will buy them again.

Worse than I imagined I didn't go in to this with super high hopes. Unfortunately it was a lot worse than I could have imagined. Even aside from the 'runs' these have like pantyhose get. I ordered up for length and it's just super ridiculous. The one photo shows how low the crotch lands when the ankles are long enough, and the other how high when they are pulled up. I'm a medium usually and although I went up several sizes, they actually still did fit fine around the waist but not knees. No length seems to be added for sizing up. The result was super awkward fitting tights. I love the print but that was not enough to save this. If you have long legs or are over 5'6" imo avoid these sheer tights or you'll be super disappointed.

Hanes EcoSmart Joggers I like these! They're cozy and for the price I ordered them at ($16) I think they're just right. They feel nice, they've washed nice but my biggest issue is that they don't have a drawstring. Sizing for reference - I like my sweatpants to have a more "boyfriend" fit so I ordered a size up (X-Large). I am 175 lbs and 5'3" and normally wear a Large/Size 12 in pants. Amazon suggested the Large for me but I ordered the XL. Pros: +Thickness - listed as "midweight" and I'd say this is accurate +super soft for the price +wash well +slim fit (I like a slimmer silhouette on my sweats so that I feel more put together if I wear them out of the house) +Pockets!! +stretchy waistband Cons: -Ankle taper - I wish the jogger cut ankle taper band was a little smaller. -No drawstring

So comfy I love wearing sweat pants on days when I don't have to go anywhere, or especially if it's a snow day. There's just something awesome about them that kind of helps you to relax when you wear them. Now, I must admit that I have bought and wore this brand many times before, but not this particular kind. Needless to say, if you, me, or anyone else sees the name Hanes on the tag - you know that it's a good product. Since I've never wore this kind of sweats before, I wasn't really sure on the size. I tried them on the night they were delivered and much to my surprise, they fit perfectly!! I'm 5'9" and around 170 pounds. These are so soft and comfortable to wear that I almost don't want to take them off!!! The pockets are a good size - and thank goodness they're not the fake pockets that a lot of other brands have, which I do not understand whatsoever. I cannot recommend this pair of sweat pants more!! If you don't want them to possibly shrink, I definitely suggest washing them in cold water and drying on low or no heat. That's what I always do and it works great. I promise you will love how these feel when you put them on!

I bought these pants for the sole purpose of wearing them at home. I have several allergies and synthetic fabrics irritate my skin. I needed 100% cotton pants to lounge in. They serve the purpose. They’re comfy, they don’t irritate me, and they fit me well. They’re not the most fashionable pants but I don’t mind since I’m only wearing them at home. You know it's good quality..it's by Hanes When I saw the Hanes brand I knew what to expect & I was not disappointed. Instead of guessing if I need a large or XL I ordered a Medium size. The fit is perfect, not tight anywhere or to baggy & the waist is comfortable. I like the wide elastic on the ankles & I fold the material over the elastic so the pants don't drag on the ground. I'm around 5'1". The 2 deep pockets on these sweat pants are a total plus & they lay flat. The fleece inside the pants is not so heavy that you could only wear them in a snow climate. I live in CA and can wear them right now b4 Thanksgiving. I went with the nice Java Frost Tan color for something different instead of the same-o black but the color doesn't stand out on my skin color like it does on the striking model in the photo. It almost blends in with my skin color so I'm glad they're not tight leggings or from a distance it would look like I forgot to put pants on. You will really like these sweatpants no matter your height. Excellent quality. No need to size up.

Great material These sweats are sooo nice! I love the material. They are fuzzy on the inside and smooth on the outside. They're so soft and comfy. They are a lighter weight so I don't get too hot in them. They feel like higher end sweats. For fit, I think they will run true to size. I ordered them too big because my size wasn't available and they seem to fit as they should. I'm 5'9, 170lbs, size 12 or large, sometimes an extra large in sweats because I like them baggy, but only an XXL was available and I figured the extra room might be nice. I was hoping they would have a drawstring but they don't. They are very baggy on me but I can still wear them around the house and I have been pretty much every day. The length is actually long enough on me, which is rare. I'm definitely ordering a pair in an extra large when they become available and I'm going to search if they made a matching top to order too. The material is just amazing!

Good quality I bought Med. I’m 5’5” and 130 lbs I needed sweats that were comfortable and baggy enough to fit over a knee brace. I bought a grey pair and black pair. If I didn’t need extra room I would have been fine and in a small. They are a little bit long for my short frame but the legs have an elastic cuff so that helped. However the cuff is not so tight or big that I can’t pull it all the way up my leg. That was important factor because it didn’t want to have to take them off to pull the knee brace on. The waist elastic is comfortable and sits at the waist line. Not super high waisted but not super low. As for quality, all seams are fine and the pockets are even lined. Pockets aren’t super deep but deep enough for my cell phone. Over all the quality exceeded my expectations for the price.

The legs are baggy and the cuffs at the ankles are the skinny type rather than the thicker “jogger” style. These Hanes are more 90’s/early 2000’s style that I have noticed the teens wearing.

I got my normal size in these and I'm swimming! I could go down at least one size with no problem. I am 5'10" and these are even too long for me. They have short inseam as well, so you can't pull them up higher to compensate. I'll still use these for lounging around the house. They are well made, I found no frays or loose threads along the seams. The material is warm, soft and comfortable. The pockets are large, which is nice. Nice with some minor annoyances These are basic sweatpants in a pretty pink color. They are an 80/20 cotton/polyester blend. The gathered waistband has covered elastic just under 2" wide, and the cuffs are gathered with 2" wide covered elastic. The 32" inseam is plenty long for my long-legged 5' 6.5" frame. The sizing is generous overall, but there's no drawstring to adjust the fit/make sure your pants stay up so picking the right size really matters. The pockets can hold a small smartphone, but they are shallow enough that it tends to fall out when sitting. The tags are a bit scratchy so these might not be your cup of tea if you are sensitive to sensory issues. Overall, these are adequate sweatpants, in my opinion, but there's nothing about them that makes them a compelling choice.

Not bad, but not the thick "sweat pant" of old I usually wear a size10-12. I bought the large in these. They fit nicely with a bit of room, perhaps they might shrink a little. They are about 2-3inches too long. I like a 31inch inseam. Even if they shrink, I’ll need to roll the top or hem the bottom. With the drawstring, I can snug them up and they fit well enough for now. They are a light weight material. They do not have a fuzzy inside, and the density is akin to maybe 1 1/2 to 2 t-shirts of thickness. SO, these are not overly warm, but are fine for around the house, and especially suited for spring or summer weather.

Update: the black are not as loose a fit as the charcoal, so size varies a little.

Well made and fits as expected (5’2” 135lbs) They’re very comfortable and pretty warm. They’re great for the fall, or just on cooler days. I love that they actually have pockets! They’re not super thick, and they don’t feel cheap at all. I have a short torso, so this mid-rise is great. I’m 5’2” so they’re long on me, but I’m used to that. I have to fold the bottom up 3.5” if I don’t want it to touch the ground. I’m 135lbs and they fit just right. As long as I do something about the extra length, they don’t make me look frumpy. These are my go-to pants when I just need to throw something on.

Ugly ragged seams in back. Ugly ragged seams ran horizontally across the back of both legs halfway down. Seemed an inch short for a 5' 11" woman. I was worried they would shrink even shorter after washing. Can't understand this sewing flaw with the crazy seams on back of pants legs.

My waist is 35”, hips 40”. There are two size charts with this product. In the drop down box by the size selection, “Women's Pants Size Chart”, I fit in a L for the waist and M for the hips. But in the “Hanes Find Your Perfect Fit” table further down in the description, it would be a 2XL for the waist and L for the hips. As I had to return a M for a L in another pair of pants I bought (not Hanes), I decided on the Large in these as I can take roomier hips but not tight waists. Mistake! They were humongous in the waist. I exchanged for a Medium and they are good with some room for shrinkage in washing. Very comfortable material. I am 5’0” so they are way too long. I made a 1” hem and inserted an elastic. My only gripe – pic shows the pants inside-out. The right pocket is 2” shorter than the left. It feels and looks weird; won’t be putting both hands in pockets at the same time. UPDATE June 16, 2021 - Wore the pants for a full day, extremely comfortable BUT they are a cat fur and lint magnet.

Great for Teams, lightweight and comfy These pants were just what I needed. I do bodybuilding competitions and needed something lightweight, plain, with long sleeves/long pants to sleep in and wear around the event so the heavy spray tan didn’t get all over the sheets and venue hall. I wanted something 100% cotton because I didn’t want to sweat and streak. I found them! They match the Hanes zip up hoodie perfectly and I was able to get my coaches logo printed on them easily since there are no other designs. The inside is a thin, breathable feel. I didn’t find that they shrunk at all when I washed them but I did tumble them on a very low delicate cycle to avoid this as much as possible. The length of these are long, which I was happy with, but one hem is slightly longer than the other. This didn’t bother me since it was less than an inch. Overall they are comfortable and light and a good price for what I needed. I am 5ft 4in - 119lbs - size small in both the pants and hoodie. Pictures taken after 1 wash.

Saved me from the daily imprisonment of tight jeans. 5’7”, 130 lbs, 36” hips, 27” waist. Got the medium. Fits comfortably loose as desired. Without using the drawstring, the pants fall on my hips (would probably fall down in some situations, especially if they get caught on something) and the hem hits the floor. With the drawstring I can pull them up to my waist and the hem no longer brushes the floor. The drawstring doesn’t slip over time. The legs fit very comfortably and flowy and there’s plenty of room through the hips and crotch. Texture is comfortable, not supremely soft though if that’s your thing. I love the gray heather and how the pants look on me. They retain a bit of warmth, not to a tremendous degree but I’m not sure if I would wear them in the summer if my AC broke. I find them cozy in mid autumn. I live in these pants at this point. Considering buying more. Perfect for lounging and teleworking and sleeping and...just about everything except going to a restaurant so fancy that the menu is printed on a single piece of card stock with no prices.

Does not feel like there's much cotton in the blend These sweatpants (for lack of a better word) look nice and fit well and are very comfortable, but I don't believe that the fabric is 60% cotton. I have numerous other items of clothing that have the 60/40 cotton-poly blend and you can feel that there is cotton in the blend. These sweatpants feel like they are 80% polyester at least. I think they are mislabeled or there is active deception at work. They are not thick and warm, they are thin and lightweight. I would not call them sweatpants, but not sure what else to call them. I bought a size medium. I'm 5'4", 142 lbs.

Just okay I took a chance on these pants because they were under $10. The fit is weird everywhere. I'm only 5'2" so I expected the pants to be longer than capri length, but they hit a couple inches above the ankle. This might be okay if they didn't flare out at the bottom. The waistband doesn't have enough elasticity, and even when I pull the drawstring as tight as possible the pants are still a little big (I weigh about 115 pounds). If I roll the waistband over the pants fit okay, but I will only be wearing them for pajamas.

Perfect fit!! I have been looking for some lounge pants that are cotton and aren't too long. And this pair is exactly that. I got a size small since i am petite and 5’2”. Definitely gonna get more colors.
I’m 5’4” and a size 14/16, I bought an L and it fit perfectly. Love it!

Comfortable and nice enough to wear out of house Comfortable, wide, gentle waistband with drawstring is nice! Best of all, these taper nicely so that if most of your weight is in the stomach, you're not left with billowing fabric around thighs and legs. They're actually flattering enough to be fine for running errands, or casual wear. The side seam on legs is prominent, that is, the stitching is visible (like top-stitching?), but the thread color blends well with the fabric, and I'm fine with it. (see pic) The fabric is on the thin side, but very soft and comfortable against the skin. Reminds me of some Lululemon joggers from a few years back. I ordered another pair right away - they were so comfy. Size Medium feels perfect for me, at 5'6"' and 136 lbs (have to use drawstring - there is ample room in waistband).
Comfy but thin Very cute. Material is thinner than I like. I’m 5’7 and bought the large. I like the fit but they are a little long. Small hole noted after wearing twice ;(

Must buy I love these pants ! Like love love them. As a stay at home mom these pants are LIFE. They are soft, comfortable and they have POCKETS. I usually have my phone in one pocket and a baby monitor in the other. While they are def lounge pants I don’t feel like I’m wearing pajamas. Typically I don’t like pants with cinched ankles but these are ok I actually really like that part of the pants. They have a tie around the waist as well as elastic so they can be tightened if needed. They used to come in 4 colors but now it looks like they only come in 3 which is sad. I currently have two colors but will be getting the third and even possibly multiples in the same color because I love them that much. I like a baggier fit in my clothes so I went with a medium size I’m 5’10” and at 1month postpartum 131pounds. I wear these pants basically everyday for in the house walks around the block/park and to the store. Discovering the Ideal Fit: Awkward Pockets, Functional Waistband, & Versatile Comfort SIZING & FIT I found myself quite torn between sizes when selecting these pants, so I decided to try both a small and medium. After much consideration, I have decided to keep the black ash camo in a size small and plan to purchase a pair in black in a size medium when I have the funds. The size choice ultimately depends on the desired look and feel. I have provided pictures wearing both the small and medium sizes to illustrate the difference. The small size offers a flattering fit without sacrificing comfort, while the medium size exudes a laid-back, jogger-style vibe with a more relaxed fit. When I asked my other half for his opinion, I first showed him the mediums. He preferred the smalls, citing a better fit for my body type. Although I leaned towards the mediums initially due to owning plenty of yoga pants and I was seeking comfy joggers, his insight prompted me to reconsider my choice. For reference, I am 5'8" tall (these pants hit right at my ankles) and weigh 123 lbs., with an average to athletic build. My current measurements are bust 35 inches, waist 27 inches, and hips 35 inches. POCKETS The pockets on these pants feel a bit awkward to me as they are positioned higher up than I am used to. When I put my phone in the side/s pocket (iPhone SE), I have to push it over further, so it stays in place, but it does stay secure. It ends up sitting more on my hip. The zipper on the right front pocket operates smoothly in both directions. OVERVIEW I really appreciate that functionality of the adjustable waistband on these pants; it's not just for show, which I have unfortunately encountered in the past. While I was initially wary of the thickness of the waistband depicted in the product photos, it is not uncomfortable at all. These pants have a soft, pleasant feel against my skin. They are versatile enough to wear for workouts, lounging around the house, running errands, or simply dressing casually. These are excellent joggers that I highly recommend!

Discovering the Ideal Fit: Awkward Pockets, Functional Waistband, & Versatile Comfort SIZING & FIT I found myself quite torn between sizes when selecting these pants, so I decided to try both a small and medium. After much consideration, I have decided to keep the black ash camo in a size small and plan to purchase a pair in black in a size medium when I have the funds. The size choice ultimately depends on the desired look and feel. I have provided pictures wearing both the small and medium sizes to illustrate the difference. The small size offers a flattering fit without sacrificing comfort, while the medium size exudes a laid-back, jogger-style vibe with a more relaxed fit. When I asked my other half for his opinion, I first showed him the mediums. He preferred the smalls, citing a better fit for my body type. Although I leaned towards the mediums initially due to owning plenty of yoga pants and I was seeking comfy joggers, his insight prompted me to reconsider my choice. For reference, I am 5'8" tall (these pants hit right at my ankles) and weigh 123 lbs., with an average to athletic build. My current measurements are bust 35 inches, waist 27 inches, and hips 35 inches. POCKETS The pockets on these pants feel a bit awkward to me as they are positioned higher up than I am used to. When I put my phone in the side/s pocket (iPhone SE), I have to push it over further, so it stays in place, but it does stay secure. It ends up sitting more on my hip. The zipper on the right front pocket operates smoothly in both directions. OVERVIEW I really appreciate that functionality of the adjustable waistband on these pants; it's not just for show, which I have unfortunately encountered in the past. While I was initially wary of the thickness of the waistband depicted in the product photos, it is not uncomfortable at all. These pants have a soft, pleasant feel against my skin. They are versatile enough to wear for workouts, lounging around the house, running errands, or simply dressing casually. These are excellent joggers that I highly recommend!

comfortable, flattering, reasonable, and OK for shorter folks! i can't tell you how much searching i've done on joggers/loungers, and until these arrived i resolved to give up Amazon hunting and just go to the store. i've had the size M army green joggers for over a month now (washed + worn 4x now, still great). i'm 5'2", 163 lbs, athletic build, hourglass shape (more butt/thighs than average jane, but nothing drastic). photo doesn't do them justice (i tried, haha) but they are very soft/sleek-feeling, comfortable, flattering, great pocket room (with a cute zippered mini pocket), and they have the perfect amount of elastic at the waist and ankles - won't squeeze you to cause rolls but stays where it needs to (the ankle elastic is also for the shorties). i will warn that if you're heavier and/or have extra curves/rolls/dimples, these pants may show those (they do cling slightly and the material tends to hug a bit, but comfortably). to avoid that, a darker color would probably be best (that's why i ordered dark, i don't know that a lighter shade would work for me). i've returned every pair i've tried in the past 2 months *except for these*, and i just ordered another pair in gray (M) today. The only reason I'm not also ordering more (or in their capri style) is because it's cold weather now and i'm still losing weight/toning, so I may be in a size S by warmer weather time. but i fully plan on ordering the capri style too. i can wear them to workout, while running errands, or to lounge around the house. Oh, and they're very reasonably priced. Perf. Highly recommend these!

200lbs and these are THE BEST! These could even go up to a size ... Hallelujah for these pants. I was nervous about these fitting because of my big booty and hips and ordering online is always gamble for me. I ordered an XL (normally a size 14) I have a 47” hip, 36” waist, 200lbs and these are THE BEST! These could even go up to a size 16. The material is very thin and I’d give it 8/10 on the quality (some will say they feel cheap) but I wouldn’t put it that way for the overall pant. I would say they are definitely breathable and wearable on even hot days. I haven’t washed them yet if I have any trouble there I will be sure to update this review. I got these in black peacock and for the most part the colors are as expected, the only thing that is a bit off is the oranges and yellows are a bit brighter than I would have liked but other than that they are pretty spot on. I did notice after an evening of wearing them that the elastic at the bottom was cutting off a bit of circulation at my ankles but it was nothing severe, it was just like leaving a hair tie on your wrist when you sleep. SERIOUSLY buy these pants, I even got a personal email from the company asking if they were satisfactory so I know customer service is good as well they do donate some of the profit.

Super cute pants but? Okay I love the look of these pants, beautiful color, style is cute and although very thin, they look nice on. As one reviewer said, not sure how long they will last but they are very pretty. I would definitely hand wash them in cold water and let them drip dry. So why only 4 stars? And better yet? Why am I returning them? For me it's a personal preference, I ordered a medium because I figured they would be more generous in the waist, my waist is 27 inches. I really dislike anything tight around my waist. I am very picky about how things feel on me, if I feel scrunched in before I eat? I can only imagine how uncomfortable it will be after I eat. With these pants, they're not super tight but they felt just too constricting for me in the waist area. The bottoms are also elastic - not my preference again. I actually think a large would fit me better and flow better. I'm 5'5 and 110 lbs and the smallest part of me is my waist so....well? Try them on but I would size UP - especially if you like things not to feel tight. Pretty pants though huh? They look cute on in my pics - just wish I felt more comfortable in them.

Overall Nice Boho Pants If you don’t mind not washing your pants and you aren’t looking for something particularly durable, but you want some pants that are super boho, then these are the pants for you. The colors on them are beautiful. Sure they’re a little bit cheaply made and I probably will avoid washing them at all costs seeing the other reviews. They are super comfy though and relatively flattering. I’m 5’3” and 140 pounds. I do have a bit of a pooch in my lower stomach (FUPA) which you can see in these pants. Don’t expect them to hide anything. Also the band around the ankles is very cheaply made and a little tight. I don’t have particularly large ankles or anything. The pair of pants that I bought it doesn’t really matter if the seams match. The pattern hides unmatching seems really well. I noticed that in some reviews on pants where there was a distinct line they complained that the seams didn’t match. Overall they’re a nice pair of pants for festivals and other special occasions.

Really Nice. Buying More! I’ve been looking for comfortable stretchy pants like this for a year! I’m plus, around 5’2” and 160 pounds after having a baby. The weight just isn’t coming off. YAY! So I’ve bought VERY disappointing stretch pants in the past year and either they’ve been too tight, too thin, cut awkwardly, just uncomfortable. But THESE ARE PERFECT! They fit great! I got an XL and usually I get 2-3X because some overseas manufacturers size down. But these are true to size in my opinion and with my experience. They are a bit long for me snd I’ll have to hem them 3-4 inches but that’s not a big deal for me. THE ONLY THING I wish was different is the waist band inner seam/hem. You can see the stitching if you’re not folding it properly and it can look a bit sloppy. I find myself adjusting the band but it’s fine(see pics :) ) . These are a bit thinner than my other handed stretch pants from Kohl’s (SO brand) but they’re not sheer so you cannot see panties through them. These aren’t tight all over like yoga pants. These are the perfect comfortable pants for any time of day. Recommended.

Unfortunately, I bought them with the intent on adding it to my work clothes rotation. While comfy, the material is a little too thin, especially around the waist band. They're also a bit long. I'm only 5'2" and I would have to hem these in order to wear them with flats and not step all over them. If you're looking for a pair of pants to wear to bed or hang around the house, I'd recommend them. But if you're looking for a pair of comfy yoga pants to sneak into your workweek wardrobe, you may want to keep looking.

Soft stretched out pj bottoms I always try to write the review I wish I would have seen prior to ordering- I originally gave these 5 stars. But unfortunately as the day went on on my 1st day wearing them, they stretched out so bad and if I wasn't yanking them up every 30 seconds they would slide right down my butt and my legs. Im not kidding. They are glorified pajama bottoms. And way too expensive. I would not wear these in public. They ARE super soft, but because there's no elasticity in it there is NO shape to them and they literally just slide right off you. I'm 5'4. They do go under my feet a bit but I don't care about that. They are NOT see through, they have a "cooling" fabric to them, they aren't too thick and they are definitely not thin. They won't make you hot. If you are looking for a pair of pj bottoms or pants to wear around the house, these are fine, but order a size smaller in my opinion. I promise you, they stretch wayyyyy out after a couple of hours.

Accurately Sized, Interesting Material My clubbing days are behind me but I still dress like I’m going out sometimes. I thought these pants would be a fun addition to my wardrobe. They are… interesting. The size is right on, they don’t make me feel like a sausage squished into a casing. The length is a bit much. I’m 5’9 and they still hit the floor in the back. I did realize they might be useful for things other than looking stylish. I’m going to give them a go in the snow. I feel like the fabric will repel water pretty well, and it won’t get saturated with water like denim would. So maybe pleather pants for winter can be my new thing. Overall they are fine, good quality, sized properly, well made. If you’ve got an aesthetic that goes with them, go for it.

So comfy and perfect for TALL gals!! I am 5' 9", 145 lbs, and a size 6...so fairly tall and slender, and I have really long legs. And I have the HARDEST time finding pajama pants that are long enough. They are all high waters on me and look ridiculous. Jeans and dresspants... no problem...they come in long/tall sizes, but not PJ pants. But these are super long and actually touch the floor around my feet, even after washing. I didn't dry them for fear they would shrink up, so I can't speak for after drying. But after washing they still fit great. I ordered medium and they are perfect....they fit...not too tight, but not baggy. I love that the waistband is wide and comes up a tad higher to suck in the tummy pooch. Lol. I'm a mama of 2 csection babies and have a little extra in the tummy area let's just say. They are very flattering. Would definitely order again!

Love em I had already bought a pair of this scrub before and since I am 5’6” wearing size3-5 I order XS tall. The first pair they sent me was really baggy compared to my previous pants. Not sure if the pair w/tags was just an older/different version? Just a little sketchy feeling when ordering but other than that the second pair fit SO well. This brand is amazing. Wash and dry over and over with no problems.

YAAASSSSSS I now have 3 pairs after my first ones lasted so well for 2 years and numerous washes. I have 1 in pewter, caribbean, and light gray now and I love them all. Im 5'3" 34-28-39.5 and usually wear a size 2 womens or size 3-5 in juniors pants and i wear an xs regular in these. They don't drag on the floor with shoes on and they fit my waist and butt comfortably. The drawstring is basically for show, it doesn't tighten much. The material is soft and light. Doesn't shrink in the dryer. Doesn't hold on to wrinkles or lint. Colors don't fade. No pilling in the inner thighs even after 2 whole years of use. Which, if u have this problem you understand it's almost unheard of. I LOVE THEM. If i was allowed to wear scrubs to work every day I'd buy a bunch of these. I pair it with the Juliet 2245 top in xs.

After a year, these are the best scrubs When I first started working at the hospital I didn’t know which scrubs I’d like better or find more comfortable so I got one of each (your typical ones: Cherokee, Greys Anatomy by Barco, and some others along with the Purple Label). Six months later, my Greys Anatomy started thinning out between the thighs. So i bought a new bottom, which only lasted another 6 months. I tried contacting their customer care and they said that they couldn’t do anything. Other scrubs are starting to thin out too. Cherokees are washed out by this point. The only pair that lasted are my Purple Label by Healing Hands (for the exception on a ripped drawstring). So I ordered two more pairs and they fit great. I am 5,4 and size 8. I got both in medium and regular length. Fit perfectly!

Perfect fit , I’m wearing xsmall Cherokee shirt and purple label extra small tall pants.. I wear those brands mostly. My job requires this color( Caribbean Blue) for my department I highly recommend both , true to size I’m 5’4” 140 lbs ..

Comfortable, flattering, and professional! This is my first time trying these scrubs and I will definitely be buying more! I received them today and I can’t believe how comfortable they are and they’re much cheaper than some other brands! I love the waist band and the cut of these pants. I purchased a small petite in black and royal blue and both were true to color. For reference I am 5’1 and 133 lbs. These pants were a little more form fitting but I love it. If you prefer your pants to be a little looser on the butt then I would maybe purchase one size up from your usual. I would also like to note that they were the perfect length for me and do not drag on the floor in tennis shoes, but if you are more than an inch or two taller than I am I wouldn’t get petite unless you want to look like you’re waiting for a flood. I paired these with the Juliet top which I will also be reviewing. I have yet to test how they hold up at work or over time but I am not worried, they feel like they’re made out of quality material and they look very professional!

Not flattering on everyone I'm 5'2"...ish 130lbs. With my sneakers, these graze the floor. If you're shorter then 5'3" you may want a petite. Not sure if it's because I'm shorter, but joggers just don't look right on me. I'm bottom heavy and these balance that out well, but they have a lot of fabric in the front and um... Idk what to say about the unfortunate way it works itself into my butt... So maybe not the best of your chonkier in the bottom. The fabric is soft but not very stretchy. The cuffs ride up pretty high in a sitting position because the fabric doesn't give and stretch while you bend. The waist band is comfortable with a firm hold. Your pants are unlikely to slide down with full pockets. If I sized down, I would not be able to tuck in a shirt. You'll see it.

The talls are about 3-4 inches longer in leg and rise. Ladies, I am 5’7” and I usually wear a Small (Cherokee) or Medium (Healing hands or Greys anatomy) regular length scrub pant, but decided to try a tall just to see if the length would give a slimming effect. Well unfortunately the Medium Tall is way to big on me. Not only are the talls about 4 inches longer in the legs but, the rise was about 3 inches higher as well. I put my size M regular length pants (green) over the size M talls so you can see the difference. (These are the exact same pants, unwashed.) Just wanted to share in case this would help someone make a decision on what length to get.

Great pants, but they shrink! I’ve just had abdominal surgery and ordered so many pants from Amazon in the search for something soft and comfortable, but smart enough that I can wear them outside. The yoga flares are all too long for me. I’m 5ft4. I also wanted the pants to have pockets! These pants seemed to be it! I ordered several packs and loved them! Unfortunately after I washed them per the instructions, they all came out of the washer at different lengths! The black ones didn’t shrink and then the two grey ones shrunk a lot. They’re still wearable for me as low rise pants, but I was disappointed that they shrunk.
Love but need to size down I’m 5’7 and 192 lbs. I got a large tall, however they are baggy so I’ll need to size down to a medium tall.

**TRUE PETITE SIZING** Okay sooo……. I am super pleased with these scrubs. I am a new grad RN, I am 5’0” tall, and 95 pounds. I wear a 00 in jeans. I have been on the hunt for some TRUE petite sized scrubs. So far I have tried white cross, barco, Adar, and these HeartSoul. None of the others have even come close in comparison to how good these scrubs fit me! I got the XXS petite in the pants and the XXS in the top. I do have a little booty for my size, and these pants actually kind of show it off 🤣 which I never can say in scrubs because in all other brands i get the absolute smallest size and they’re still baggy on me. I may could have gotten just an XS petite in these pants and they would still have fit me good. Very pleased with the true petite fit. I haven’t wore them for a shift yet so I’ll update later on their durability, but so far the quality seems good.
